1292-48922026-04-05Blk 57
Enquiry about why tennis court has fees during off-peak hours.

Title: Fee for Tennis Court

Description: I wonder why is there a fee for the tennis courts throughout the day? I understand if there is a fee during peak hours to curb demand (before / after office hours) but I don't get why would there be a fee throughout the day when we already pay MCST fees? I've never heard of a condo that charges fees throughout the day - can we please revisit this?

MA Reply: [07 Apr 2026] Dear Ms Fel, Thank you for your feedback regarding the tennis court usage fees. We understand your concerns and would like to share that it is a common practice for MCSTs to implement user fees for certain facilities within a condominium, as part of managing maintenance costs and regulating usage. That said, we hear your feedback and appreciate you taking the time to share your perspective. We will be bringing this matter to the attention of the Management Council for their discussion and consideration. Thank you once again for your valuable feedback. Best Regards, Rui Ling

Title: please update my status since Jan 2026

Description: No description

MA Reply: [31 Mar 2026] Dear Ms Soh, Thank you for your feedback. We have worked closed with Starhub and M1 to resolve the lack of mobile network coverage for their respective services and they have been working to connect their network mains to our existing infrastructure. Starhub and M1 have informed us that network coverage for their services will be up sometime in April 2026. We understand the inconvenience that this matter may have cause you and seek your kind understanding as we work with the relevant parties to resolve this matter. Best Regards, Rui Ling

Closed
1292-47702026-03-24Blk 49
Inquiry about e-voting process for council.

Title: council minutes point 2.3 - eVoting

Description: is the e-voting for council members or all SPs?

MA Reply: [25 Mar 2026] Dear Ms Vera, Thank you for your feedback. This is only a decision-making platform for the council member to vote on certain matters. Best Regards, Desmond

Closed
1292-47622026-03-23Blk 47
Told to remove towels from glass panel, seeking clarification.

Title: Removal of towels.

Description: Good morning, a security lady just came up to inform me that I’m not allowed to hang my towels on the glass panel. I’ve secured it with a large clip and i have not lost any towels before. it’s the first time I’ve been asked to remove my towel. may I seek a bit more clarity of this please?

MA Reply: [23 Mar 2026] Dear Aaron, We understand your concerns and appreciate that you have taken steps to secure your towels. However, as outlined in the Resident’s Handbook: Page 11, Item 22 states that residents should not hang or dry items beyond the strata unit area, as it may affect the building’s aesthetics. Page 12, Item 30 highlights that placing objects on balcony or window ledges in a manner that may cause injury or damage is not permitted. As such, hanging towels on the glass panel is not allowed, even when secured, due to both safety and estate appearance considerations. This guideline applies consistently to all residents. We seek your kind understanding and cooperation in adhering to these regulations to help maintain a safe and pleasant living environment for everyone. Best Regards, Desmond

Closed
1292-47332026-03-18Blk 51
Request to upgrade intercom for remote phone access.

Title: Intercom upgrade to all remote acces.

Description: Many condos, including older ones, have remote intercom functionality via phone.

MA Reply: [19 Mar 2026] Dear AK, Thank you very much for your feedback. The management council is aware of this matter and is in a midst of securing this feature (amongst other additional features) for the estate. We are targeting this feature to be up and ready in the month of April 2026. Thank you very much for your patience while we continue to improve the estate. Best Regards, Desmond / Rui Ling

Closed
1292-45502026-02-16Blk 57
Previous feedback not brought up at council meeting.

Title: Re: Case ID [phone]

Description: Hello team, I have previously submitted feedback for consideration to the MCST/MA team and yet in the recent 4 Feb meeting, this was not brought up. Can I understand what's the full process and how do we get it for actual consideration?

MA Reply: [19 Feb 2026] Dear Fel, Thank you for bringing this matter to us. We apologise for the initial oversight and were not able to share at the latest council meeting your question regarding upgrading the HiLife system. Rest assured that it will be brough up to the council at the upcoming meeting. Additionally, we will sincerely appreciate it if you can send us the request in a formal email to [email] so that it is recorded down officially by the MCST. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. We will update you again once the matter has been deliberated by the council, and an outcome is achieved. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead. Regards, Nurhidayat

Requesting help for kitchen light and bathroom vent repair.

Title: kitchen light and bathroom vent

Description: hi management office, I wanted to ask if some agency from Normanton park management office can be contacted for kitchen light repair and increasing the fan speed of bathroom ventilation. the kitchen light got fused and needs replacement. can you please advise? if not, then do I need to contact some handyman for the job? can you please provide some contacts?

MA Reply: [16 Feb 2026] With reference to your enquiry regarding the kitchen light repair and increasing the fan speed of the bathroom ventilation, please contact the handyman directly at [phone] to arrange for assistance. We have also provided your contact number to the handyman for follow-up. Kindly note that all costs incurred will be borne by you. Best Regards - Joanne

Title: [phone]

As such, the contractor is no longer responsible for rectifying issues reported after this period. You may need to engage the contractor or your own contractor at your own cost. Please find the contractor details as below: CASA Pte Ltd Contact: [phone] Email: [email] We appreciate your patience and understanding. Warmest Regards, Alfred

1292-44822026-02-08Blk 47
Request to allow children to ride bicycles in condo.

Title: riding bicycle in normanton park

Description: why is the condo forbidding children from riding bicycle now, our children is not able to learn bicycle due to this. please let us know why and also adjust the rule.

MA Reply: [09 Feb 2026] Hi Ethan Tan, thanks for the feedback. Based on the House Rules, common areas are not allowed for cycling. This measure is implemented to: 1.Ensure the safety of all residents, especially children and elderly. 2.Prevent accidents, injuries, and damage to common property. 3.Maintain a peaceful and orderly living environment. Your cooperation and understanding in adhering the house rules are greatly appreciated. Thanks again.

Closed
1292-44372026-02-03Blk 57
Inquiry if scooter riding is permitted in condo.

Title: riding scooter

Description: hi, my kids told me that security guard mentioned cannot ride scooters? I am aware bicycles cannot riden but scooters also? please confirm. and I called [phone] several times and the line is always engage. do you’ll only have 1 line? is there another number I can call?

MA Reply: [04 Feb 2026] Hi Yogesvari, and thank you for your enquiry regarding the use of scooters within the common areas. We would like to clarify that bicycles are not permitted to be used at common areas due to safety concerns. Similarly, the use of scooters at common areas is also not allowed, as they pose potential safety risks to pedestrians, especially children, elderly residents, and other users of the facilities. We seek your kind understanding and cooperation in adhering to the house rules to ensure a safe living environment for all residents. Meanwhile, we have only 1 number and the number is correct.

Title: Tennis courts cannot be reserved

Closed
1292-44272026-02-02Blk 45
Objection to ban on scooters and bicycles for children.

Title: Ridiculous rule of banning scooter and bicycles

Description: Dear Management

MA Reply: [03 Feb 2026] Hi Stella Leong, thank you for sharing your feedback with us and we appreciate you taking the time to express your concern. Firstly, the house rule is not intended to be unreasonable but rather is implemented with residents' safety as the top priority. Common areas such as corridors, lobbies, and pool surroundings are shared spaces with high pedestrian traffic, including children, elderly residents and visitors. Secondly, riding scooters or bicycles in these areas poses a risk of accidents, collisions, and potential injuries. Apart from that, we have also received a lot of feedback on miss incident for these activities which may lead to accident or injury. Thirdly, such activities may cause damage to common property and the estate's house rules are enforced consistently for the benefit of the entire community. We sincerely appreciate your understanding and cooperation in helping us maintain a safe and pleasant living environment for all residents.

Closed
1292-41482025-12-11Blk 57
Request to remove owners' names and units from public display per PDPA.

Title: PDPA!

Description: Please remove all owners name and their unit! this is against personal data protection act! how can you reveal full name and full address publically like that! please remove before I report to authorities on abuse of my personal information.

MA Reply: [12 Dec 2025] Dear Chun Kit, Thank you for your feedback and for sharing your concerns regarding the display of the list of eligible voters for the upcoming Annual General Meeting. We would like to clarify that under the First Schedule of the Building (Strata Management) Act, the Management Corporation (MCST) is required to display the list of eligible voters, including the corresponding strata lots, on the notice board at least 48 hours before the General Meeting. This ensures transparency and allows subsidiary proprietors to verify their voting status ahead of the meeting. The list will be displayed only for the minimum required duration and, in line with PDPA retention principles, will typically be removed within the week after the meeting concludes. We hope this explanation provides clarity on the statutory requirements and the MCST’s rationale for displaying the list. Thank you for your kind attention, feel free to reach out to us should you have any further enquiries. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-41462025-12-11Blk 57
Full names posted on notice board violating data protection.

Title: Singapore Personal Data Proctection Act

Description: When listing eligible voters, you can use Grok or other tools to do masking such as John Doe becomes J* D* etc. Neighbours highlighted the full name list pasted in the notice board without adhering to Singapore Personal Data Protection Act.

MA Reply: [12 Dec 2025] Dear Hu Shien, Thank you for your email and for sharing your concerns regarding the display of the list of eligible voters for the upcoming Annual General Meeting. We would like to clarify that under the First Schedule of the Building (Strata Management) Act, the Management Corporation (MCST) is required to display the list of eligible voters, including the corresponding strata lots, on the notice board at least 48 hours before the General Meeting. This ensures transparency and allows subsidiary proprietors to verify their voting status ahead of the meeting. The list will be displayed only for the minimum required duration and, in line with PDPA retention principles, will typically be removed within the week after the meeting concludes. We hope this explanation provides clarity on the statutory requirements and the MCST’s rationale for displaying the list. Thank you for your kind attention, feel free to reach out to us should you have any further enquiries. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

We understand this has raised concern and appreciate your patience as we work to determine the source of the sound. If you have any specific information (e.g., location, video, or photo), please feel free to share it with the Management Office to assist our review. Thank you for your understanding and continued cooperation.

1292-32802025-05-26Blk 47
Safety concerns about feeder bus service and route.

Title: feeder bus safety

Description: Dear [Management/Team], I would like to bring to your attention two safety concerns regarding the current feeder bus service: 1. Steering Alignment Issue It has been observed that the bus requires the steering wheel to be tilted in order to maintain a straight path on the road. This misalignment may indicate a mechanical issue that could affect vehicle control and overall safety. (Please refer to the attached image for reference.) 2. Low Travel Speed The bus is frequently travelling at speeds of only 20–30 km/h along Science Park, which results in road-hogging and potentially disrupts traffic flow. This may also cause delays for passengers and other road users. I kindly request your review and attention to the above points to ensure the safety and efficiency of the service. Thank you for your understanding and support. Best regards,

MA Reply: [27 May 2025] Dear Mr Max, Thank you for the feedback. We have conveyed your feedback to the shuttle bus service provider. They have thoroughly inspected the bus and found no mechanical issues. According to their assessment, the gearbox performance is consistent with the standard operation for this model, and they have five similar buses from the same manufacturer with identical gearbox characteristics. Regarding the travel speed, the driver maintains a lower speed in order to arrive at the designated pick-up point on schedule. Arriving too early at Kent Ridge MRT is not permitted, as waiting at the pick-up location before the allocated time is not allowed.

Closed
1292-32382025-05-16Blk 53
Enquiry about where furniture pickup truck can park.

Title: furniture pickup

Description: hello- a furniture truck 14 feet long and 3.4 m in height will take some furniture from me in block 53. where can they park? what arrangements are needed in advance? thank you

MA Reply: [16 May 2025] 1) The carpark height limit - 2.2m therefore the truck must park outside Normanton Park. 2) You can get the moving permit in Hi Life under Eforms. Application forms must be submitted to the Management at least 7 days before commencement of any moving in/out or delivery. Thank you

Closed
1292-32262025-05-13Blk 61
Feedback about restrictive estate rules.

Title: Restrictive rules that dont make sense

Description: please refer to attached for my feedback

MA Reply: [14 May 2025] Dear Jia Jun Oh We understand your concerns regarding the need to apply for leave to facilitate servicing activities. Please allow us to provide some context and clarify the guidelines in place. In Singapore, condominium developments are governed by the Building Maintenance and Strata Management Act (BMSMA), which sets out prescribed by-laws to ensure harmonious living among residents. These by-laws include regulations on noise, renovation works, and the use of common property. Many condominiums have additional house rules established by the Management Corporation Strata Title (MCST) to further regulate activities within the estate. These rules often specify permitted hours for renovation and maintenance works, typically allowing such activities during weekdays and limiting them on weekends and public holidays to minimize disturbances. The rationale behind these restrictions is to maintain a peaceful living environment, especially during times when many residents are at home resting. By adhering to these guidelines, we aim to balance the needs of all residents and ensure that everyone can enjoy their homes without undue disruption. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in this matter. Should you have any further questions or require assistance, please feel free to contact the management office. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Closed
1292-32132025-05-12Blk 55
Parcel Santa locker service is down, screen blank.

Title: Parcel Santa Out Off Service

Description: Tried to pick up my parcel and the parcel santa locker service was down. The screen was blank. We should dispose of the machine if it’s so unreliable.

MA Reply: [13 May 2025] Hi Mr Ameer Mohamed, thanks for the feedback. There was a power trip on the santa locker yesterday evening so we have informed the Security Officer to reset the power. After that, the locker works fine around 8.15pm. Sorry for the inconvenience.

Your cooperation and understanding in adhering the house rules are greatly appreciated. Thanks again.

1292-44282026-02-02Blk 45
Complaint about sudden ban on children cycling and scooters.

Title: Banning of Scooters and Bicycles

Description: Dear Management, why is there suddenly a ‘ban’ of children playing scooter and bicycles with no discussion or rationale ??? this is a ridiculous rule. Normanton park is supposed to be a family friendly residence, if children are not allowed to play freely on their scooters in the condo, then where are they supposed to do so??? drag it to the park ?? if there have been complaints about children misbehaving on scooters, you can put up a notification and we will educate our children accordingly. but banning this is really too rigid and senseless. even the government is trying to promote outdoor play for kids. please stop this senseless banning or else we will discuss this with the MP.

MA Reply: [03 Feb 2026] Hi Stella Leong, thank you for sharing your feedback with us and we appreciate you taking the time to express your concern. Firstly, the house rule is not intended to be unreasonable but rather is implemented with residents' safety as the top priority. Common areas such as corridors, lobbies, and pool surroundings are shared spaces with high pedestrian traffic, including children, elderly residents and visitors. Secondly, riding scooters or bicycles in these areas poses a risk of accidents, collisions, and potential injuries. Apart from that, we have also received a lot of feedback on miss incident for these activities which lead to accident or injury. Thirdly, such activities may cause damage to common property and the estate's house rules are enforced consistently for the benefit of the entire community. We sincerely appreciate your understanding and cooperation in helping us maintain a safe and pleasant living environment for all residents.

While no tentative date has been given, Starhub and M1 have informed us that network coverage for their services will be up sometime in March 2026. We understand the inconvenience that this matter may have cause you and seek your kind understanding as we work with the relevant parties to resolve this matter. Best Regards, The Management

1292-43052026-01-10Blk 57
Proposal for more HiLife app features like gate unlock.

Title: Feedback: Increased HiLife App Functionality

Description: Hi MCST & MA team, I would like to formally propose looking into having increased functionality on the hilife app to include functions such as unlocking the lobby gates from our phones, registering visitors, unlocking the gates with our faces. 2 main reasons this could be a huge value add for the residents: (1) Many competing condos have their own apps or uses the iCondo interface which allows all of the above and more. Normanton Park, though a young condo, can't even seem to be on par with older condos in terms of technology and convenience. This is a huge a minus to future buyers and tenants. (2) We are now largely dependant on the hiLife intercom in each house and many people have given negative feedback on the system or have encountered the intercom screen complete dying and it costs us $600+ to have it replaced. With the subpar quality of the screen, it's likely that $600+ is not a one time cost. Appreciate your attention to look into this.

MA Reply: [13 Jan 2026] Thank you for your proposal regarding the expansion of functionality within the HiLife app to include additional features. We have brought this proposal forward to the Council Members for their consideration.

Currently, it is quite difficult to locate new notices, as we need to search through multiple sections. Having the most recent notices shown first would make it much more convenient for residents to stay updated. Thank you for your consideration.

MA Reply: [22 Dec 2025] Dear Andrew, Thank you for your feedback. We have recently cleaned up our circulars and notices. Right now, only the house rules and condominium handbook will be pinned at the very top of the column for ease of reference by all residents. We appreciate your valuable input that will hopefully benefit all residents in receiving announcements and notices promptly and with convenience. Closed
1292-41562025-12-13Blk 57
Request to remove unit number and names from public notice board.

Title: Request for Removal of Personal Details from Notic

Description: Hi! MCST, Please refer to the attached photo. We noticed that our unit number and names are displayed publicly on the notice board without our prior consent. We are not very comfortable with this information being made public. We would appreciate it if MCST could kindly remove our details from the notice board. Thank you for your understanding and assistance.

MA Reply: [15 Dec 2025] Dear Resident, Thank you for your feedback and for sharing your concerns regarding the display of the list of eligible voters for the upcoming Annual General Meeting. We would like to clarify that under the First Schedule of the Building (Strata Management) Act, the Management Corporation (MCST) is required to display the list of eligible voters, including the corresponding strata lots, on the notice board at least 48 hours before the General Meeting. This ensures transparency and allows subsidiary proprietors to verify their voting status ahead of the meeting. The list will be displayed only for the minimum required duration and, in line with PDPA retention principles, will typically be removed within the week after the meeting concludes. We hope this explanation provides clarity on the statutory requirements and the MCST’s rationale for displaying the list. We wish to highlight that the list have been removed after the AGM 13 Dec 2025. Thank you for your kind attention

Closed
1292-41442025-12-11Blk 61
Inquiry about facial recognition and auto-unlock for lobby.

Title: facial recognition and auto unlocking

Description: hi can you let me know: (1) is there a facial recognition feature at the lift lobby area where residents can use facial recognition to unlock the door to enter the building instead of using access card or scanning QR code? (2) is it possible to unlock the lobby entrance using the HiLife app when a visitor press the buzzer at the lobby ?

MA Reply: [11 Dec 2025] Thank you for your enquiry. Please see the clarifications below: 1) Facial recognition at the lift lobby At the moment, our estate does not have a facial-recognition feature installed at the lift lobby. Entry is currently supported only through the existing access card/QR code methods. 2) Unlocking the lobby entrance via the HiLife app Currently, it is not possible to unlock the lobby entrance using the HiLife app when a visitor presses the buzzer. Visitors will need to key in the unit number at the lobby panel, and the resident must be at home to answer the call and grant access through the intercom.

1292-37502025-09-11Blk 47
Inquiry about authority for new pool construction without MCST.

Title: Clarification on Construction of New Pool Facility

Description: I recently noticed that a new pool has been constructed within the condominium premises. As the Management Corporation Strata Title (MCST) Owners’ Committee has not yet been formed, I would like to understand under what authority this decision was made. Specifically, I am concerned about: 1. Whether the construction of this facility required prior consultation or approval from unit owners. 2. If so, why no voting or communication was conducted with residents before proceeding. 3. Whether this addition complies with the relevant BCA and MCST regulations in the absence of an established council. I would appreciate your clarification on the above points, and request that any supporting documentation or regulatory reference be shared with residents for transparency. Thank you for your attention, and I look forward to your reply.

MA Reply: [11 Sep 2025] Dear Xiaofan Xu, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation and the photo attached in your feedback. Kindly note that the aforementioned photo is not a new pool facility. It is a planter box that was constructed by the developer as part of landscape enhancement works. This includes the construction of this planter box as well as a built-up path between terrace houses 31-33 to lead into the rest of the estate. With regards to your query on the authority that this decision was made, these works were undertaken by the developer, who performs the duties of the council during this Initial Period. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-34172025-06-30Blk 45
Lack of emergency protocol for child incidents at pool.

Title: lack of protocol for child emergency

Description: Dear team I hope you’re well. I wanted to share some feedback and a suggestion following an incident that occurred last Sunday at our unit. Our two-year-old daughter accidentally locked herself inside the main bedroom while my wife was home alone. Unfortunately, the phone and other essentials were also inside the room, leaving her unable to call for help immediately. While we appreciate the willingness of the on-duty security staff to assist, it became clear that there was no clear or effective protocol in place for handling such emergencies. The staff member attempted to open the door himself, which was understandably not successful. Eventually, it was the emergency services—contacted later—who were able to help open the door safely. In situations involving children or potential danger, time is critical. I would like to strongly suggest that the security team be trained to escalate such cases immediately to emergency services like 995 or 999. Having a clear SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) for lock-in or similar household emergencies could make a huge difference and avoid panic or delays in the future. I hope this suggestion will be taken in the constructive spirit it’s intended. We’re grateful that things ended well this time, but it could have been much worse, and preparation is key. Please let me know if you’d like to discuss this further. Warm regards, Arun Aggarwal 1403 block 45

MA Reply: [30 Jun 2025] Dear Arun, thank you for your feedback with regards to the incident that occurred last Sunday. We are sorry to hear about the incident and hope that everything is okay now. After referring to the occurrence book and checking in with the security personnel, it is concluded that security followed the protocol and responded with the appropriate action based on their assessment of the situation. While security did take the right action to seek approval to engage the locksmith with the resident, we seek your understanding that the time taken for the locksmith to arrive is beyond our security's control. Rest assured that security will engage the relevant authorities when a situation calls for it. Nevertheless, thank you for your feedback and we appreciate all suggestions received from our residents. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead. As no further response is required, we will close this feedback automatically, thank you.

Closed

Closed
1292-49112026-04-07Blk 47
Proposal to convert tennis courts into multi-use sports facility.

Title: Proposal to Convert Tennis Courts into Multi-Use P

Description: Dear Management Committee, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to propose the conversion of our existing tennis courts into multi-use courts that can accommodate both tennis and pickleball. As our condominium currently has two tennis courts, this presents an excellent opportunity to better optimize the space and cater to the evolving interests of residents. Background & Rationale In recent years, pickleball has grown rapidly in popularity across all age groups due to its accessibility, social nature, and lower physical strain compared to tennis. Many residential developments are beginning to incorporate pickleball facilities to meet this demand. At present, the tennis courts are underutilized during certain periods, while there is increasing interest among residents to play pickleball. Proposed Plan - Convert one or both tennis courts into dual-use courts - Add pickleball court lines (non-permanent or permanent) - Install portable pickleball nets (no major structural changes required) - Retain the ability to use the courts for tennis where needed Benefits to Residents - Maximizes usage of existing facilities - Provides an inclusive sport suitable for all ages - Encourages community bonding and social interaction - Adds value and attractiveness to the condominium Cost Consideration The proposed changes can be implemented with relatively low cost: - Court line marking - Portable nets (removable and shared) - Optional signage and booking adjustments No major renovation or structural work is required. Suggested Approach - Start with a pilot implementation on one tennis court - Gather resident feedback - Expand to the second court if demand increases Conclusion This proposal aims to enhance the usability and value of our shared facilities while meeting the changing recreational preferences of residents. I believe this initiative will be well received and benefit the community as a whole. Thank you for your time and consideration.

MA Reply: [07 Apr 2026] Dear Mervis, Thank you for your thoughtful proposal regarding the conversion of the existing tennis courts into multi-use courts to accommodate both tennis and pickleball. We appreciate the time and effort you have taken to share this valuable feedback and suggestion with us. Your proposal highlights several meaningful benefits for the community, and we recognize the growing interest in such recreational facilities. Please be informed that we will be bringing this matter to the Management Council for their deliberation. As this proposal involves a change of use of the existing facilities, it may also require approval from the general body during the Annual General Meeting (AGM). Thank you once again for your valuable feedback and for contributing towards the improvement of our community. Best Regards, Rui Ling

Closed
1292-48052026-03-30Blk 47
Proposal to install water coolers in condo common areas.

Title: water cooler installation

Description: I would like to propose that the management consider installing water coolers within the condominium premises. Currently, there are no water coolers available throughout the entire development. This can be inconvenient for residents, especially those who are using common facilities such as the gym, function rooms, or outdoor areas, where access to drinking water is important. Having water coolers installed would provide several benefits: • Improve convenience and comfort for residents and guests • Encourage hydration, especially for those engaging in exercise or outdoor activities • Reduce reliance on single-use plastic bottles, supporting more environmentally friendly practices • Enhance the overall living experience and perceived value of the condominium facilities I believe this addition would be a practical and appreciated improvement for the community. Thank you for considering this suggestion.

MA Reply: [02 Apr 2026] Dear Mr. Chua, Thank you for your thoughtful suggestion regarding the installation of water coolers within the condominium premises. We appreciate you taking the time to share your feedback and for highlighting the potential benefits this addition could bring to residents and guests, particularly in enhancing convenience, promoting hydration, and supporting more environmentally friendly practices. Please be assured that we will take your suggestion into consideration as we continue to review and improve the facilities within the development. Thank you once again for your valuable input. Rgds, Desmond

Request for tennis court refund due to rain waterlogging.

Title: refund request for tennis court

Description: hi. I couldn’t use tennis court facility today because of inclement weather. the tennis court is water logged due to heavy rain. as per point h in terms and conditions, please refund or replace my booking.

MA Reply: [19 Feb 2026] Dear Bimba, thank you for your feedback. As per the terms and conditions of the tennis courts, no refunds will be permitted for cancellations made on the day of play. However, as the cancellation was due to inclement weather, kindly write in to us at [email] your request to reschedule the tennis court appointment, as well as the preferred replacement date and time. Our team will assist you with the booking of the new appointment, subject to availability. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. Closed
1292-44982026-02-09Blk 47
Function room storage locked during rental, limited space.

Title: Function Room 1

Description: We rented the function room 1 on 7th February just to realise that you have the storage room in the functuon locked which left us unable to store our event stuff appropriately. We were left with very little space to work on the pantry and the kitchenette counter with stuff and buffet accessories that had to be placed just by the counter which was very unsightly to look at as they were not appropriately stored away. Had to work on a very small space with the counter and kitchen. We've rented the functions rooms on many occasions and noted that the storage rooms were never locked. It serves as a functional room to help out caterers to store their stables of food trays and food trolleys. Except that this time we noted the storage rooms were stuff and stored with management's office stationaries and supplies. Why is this so ? Please ensure that the storage room is available for aby of the residents to rent and use especialy for the caterers to use it as a storage room where they could store away the trays of food trolleys. please advise why was these store rooms closed and lock away?

MA Reply: [09 Feb 2026] Dear Shirin, We refer to your feedback with thanks. Please note that the storage room contains an electrical Distribution Board (DB) box connected to the common area. For safety and security reasons, the room must remain locked at all times and is not available for event or caterer use. We appreciate for your understanding. Warmest regards, Alfred

1292-37482025-09-10Blk 53
Request to use Function Room 2 on 11 October.

Title: Request for Use of Function Room 2 on 11 October

Description: Dear Management Office, We have noted that Function Room 2 has been blocked out for use by the management office. We are planning to host an event on 11 October and would like to request if the room can be made available for our use on that day. Additionally, we would appreciate clarification on whether it is within the management office's authority to block out the function room for an extended period, such as the entire month. As residents, we believe the shared facilities should remain accessible and fairly available to all, and long-term exclusive bookings may not reflect this principle. We hope the room can be released for community use on the requested date and look forward to your response. Thank you.

MA Reply: [11 Sep 2025] Dear Eunice, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation that the function room has been blocked. We would like to inform you that the management office will be relocated temporarily to Function Room 2 due to enhancement works planned for the management office. The enhancement works is scheduled to start in October and will take about a month to complete. A circular will be posted to address this relocation in due course. We hope that this elaborates why function room 2 is booked for that period. Thank you for your kind attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically.

Access Card (21 cases)
Case IDDateBlockTitleStatus
1292-47782026-03-25Blk 47
Concern about visitor screening at condo entrance.

Title: Enquiry on visitors screening

Description: Hi MA. Could you please share the current measures to ensure only residents or residents’ intended visitors are allowed to enter the facilities? Especially during peak hour at the dropoff. From what i observe, any random person can enter via grab to use our facilities. There is no record of who is entering or checking of access card to see if they are residents.

MA Reply: [25 Mar 2026] Dear Jooo, Thank you for your feedback and for bringing this matter to our attention. We would like to assure you that current measures are already in place to regulate access to our premises. Our security personnel conduct checks on visiting vehicles and registered visitors, particularly at the drop-off points, to help ensure that only authorised individuals gain entry. In addition, we are in the process of enhancing our access control system through the installation of a Licence Plate Recognition (LPR) system. This upgrade is intended to further strengthen the monitoring and management of visitor and vehicle entry. The system is targeted to be fully operational by 1 April 2026. We hope that the concerns you have raised can be better addressed with the implementation of this technology. Best Regards, Desmond / Rui Ling

Blk 55 level 1 doors not springing back to close.

Title: blk 55 level 1 lift lobby doors

Description: the door on level 1 is constantly left open morning afternoon and night. see attached picture. we noticed when we open the door and going in and out, the door would not spring back and close shut. instead, it stays open. this occurs on both of the doors at level 1. you can reference other doors at B1 or other blks, the door will close by itself. this has been happening for quite a while, at least 6-8 months of staying here. this poses a security issue, the defeats the purpose of having access card! pls look into this urgently.

MA Reply: [26 Feb 2026] Hi Valerie Quan, thanks for the feedback. We will put up the notice at the door entrance to remind all residents to ensure that access doors are not propped open and are fully closed after use to maintain the safety and security of the building. Apart from that, we have also reminded the security to assist on closing the door during their patrol and that goes to the cleaner as well to look out for the door. We appreciate your vigilance in highlighting this matter to us. Thanks again, Shafiee

Closed

MA Reply: [15 Jan 2026] Thank you for the information. We have updated the extension details in our system accordingly.

Title: Urgent: Security Issue with Gate Door

Description: Dear Management, I hope this message finds you well. I would like to bring to your attention a security concern regarding the gate door at 57, Level 1. It can currently be opened without the use of an access card, which poses a significant risk as it allows unauthorized individuals to enter the block easily. For your reference, I have attached a video clip demonstrating the issue. We would greatly appreciate it if you could look into this matter urgently and take the necessary action to rectify the issue in order to ensure the safety and security of all residents. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Please be informed that we have already assigned this matter to our contractor, who will be attending to it as a priority. We will continue to monitor the situation and follow up to ensure the necessary repairs are carried out as soon as possible. Thanks again for bringing this serious matter to us.

It’s particularly disturbing to see this as it really reflects poorly on the security of the estate.

MA Reply: [25 Aug 2025] Dear Ameer, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation regarding the doors at the level 1 lift lobby being left open. Please be assured that security includes the L1 lift lobbies on their routine patrol to include checking and closing of the doors. However, we will also be putting up signages at the lift lobby to remind residents and visitors that they should also ensure that the door is closed behind them when they leave the lobby. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. As no further response is required from your end, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Thank you.

Title: ziptrak

1292-47162026-03-15Blk 47
Renovation at Blk 47 and 49 without lift padding.

Title: Renovation Works - Blk 47&49

Description: Dear MA team, see the 2 renovations notice - but I never see any lift paddings or floor protectors (cardboard) being put up. Can you check & confirm what are the reno works to be done ? why no need lift padding or floor cupboard? thanks

MA Reply: [16 Mar 2026] Thank you for informing us about the two renovation notices. However, we have not observed any lift padding or floor protection (such as cardboard) being installed. For Blk 49, which was scheduled to start on 13 March 2026, the contractor has not begun the renovation works yet. For Blk 47, which was scheduled to start on 9 March 2026, there has been no machinery or equipment brought in as of today. The security team will continue to monitor the situation. Contractors must ensure that lift padding is installed when transporting renovation materials. Best Regards - Joanne

Closed
1292-45892026-02-25Blk 57
Resident moving at night violating condo moving guidelines.

Title: Concern Regarding Enforcement of Moving Guidelines

Description: Dear MCST Team, I would like to bring to your attention an incident that occurred at approximately 8:18pm involving a resident from Block 57, Unit [unit], who was carrying out moving activities within the premises. Based on the condo guidelines and policies, such activities are not permitted beyond the stipulated hours. I am concerned as to why the Security Team allowed the movers to enter and proceed with their work at that time. During my own renovation period, the Security Team was very strict in enforcing the rules. On multiple occasions, my contractors were required to stop work around 4:30pm, and security personnel would personally come up to escort them out of the premises each day. While I appreciate proper enforcement of the condo’s guidelines, it is important that these rules are applied consistently to all residents and contractors without exception. I respectfully request that this matter be looked into seriously. If the management intends for all residents and contractors to strictly adhere to the condo guidelines and policies, enforcement must be fair and consistent, without any form of double standards. Please investigate this incident and ensure that the Security Team is reminded to apply the condo regulations uniformly. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [26 Feb 2026] For your information, the moving activity involved an internal relocation from one block to another within the premises. The moving process was halted at approximately 5:30 PM due to heavy resident traffic and high lift usage at that time. Subsequently, the security guard assisted the resident at around 8:00 PM to complete the remaining moving activities. Thank you for your understanding regarding this matter. Best Regards - Joanne

Closed
1292-45682026-02-20Blk 57
Cleaning staff at Blk 57 behaving rudely to residents.

Title: Feedback Regarding Cleaning Staff Behavior at 57

Description: Dear Normanton Park Management Team, ​I am writing to raise an ongoing issue regarding the behavior of a member of the cleaning staff (older chinese lady) who is frequently stationed at the ground floor public area of my block (block 57). On multiple occasions, this staff member has made unprovoked, negative comments whenever I walk my dog past the area. Please note that my is always kept under strict control, and has never once approached or touched this individual. It is highly disappointing to be made to feel unwelcome in the common areas of my own residence. If this staff member is uncomfortable around pets, sitting in a shared public space where residents routinely walk their dogs is not appropriate. Her ongoing commentary is uncalled for and is becoming a nuisance to residents who are simply trying to enjoy the estate in peace. I would appreciate it if management could speak with the cleaning contractor or the individual directly to remind them of the expected professional conduct while on the premises, ensuring a respectful environment for all residents. ​Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [21 Feb 2026] Dear Fel, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks. For your information, we will address this issue with the cleaning team during the meeting on Monday. Thanks again for bringing this matter to management attention.

Title: no signal on my mobile

MA Reply: [26 Feb 2026] Dear Javin, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks. For your information, we have been liaising closely with the network operators, M1 and StarHub. Based on the latest correspondence received from them, there were delays encountered during the cabling works. The operators are now expecting the transmission works to be completed by March 2026. In the meantime, we will advise using access card for the door access. We appreciate your patience and understanding in this matter. Should there be any further updates, we will keep residents informed accordingly. Best regards, Alfred

Case IDDateBlockTitleStatus
1292-50442026-04-22Blk 61
Resident receiving noise complaints from neighbour without specific details.

Title: neighbor sending noise complaint but no details

Description: I wanted to flag an ongoing issue I’m dealing with. A neighbor has been sending guards and written notices to my unit with noise complaints, but without specifying what the actual issue is. I’ve now received three complaints, the most recent tonight at 11pm. A few things I’d like to raise: • The guards have come to my door as late as 2am, which is unsettling given that I live alone. • One notice asked me to stop moving furniture and heavy boxes — but the neighbor in question lives above me, not below, so I’m confused about how that applies. • I’m not being given any details about the specific complaints, which makes it impossible for me to address anything. I’d really appreciate your help understanding what’s going on and what my options are here. I live along and this situation is making me uncomfortable and I’d like to resolve it properly

Pending
1292-50232026-04-21Blk 61
Noise disturbance reported from nearby unit at Block 61.

Title: Repeated Late-Night Noise Severely Affecting Sleep

Description: Dear Property Management Team, I am writing to report a recurring noise issue that has been seriously affecting my sleep. I reside at Blk 61, Unit [unit]. Almost every night, I hear the sound of chairs being dragged across the floor from the unit above. The noise is extremely sharp and disturbing, especially during late-night hours. Approximately two weeks ago, a resident from the 20th floor had also raised a similar concern with the management team. At that time, I communicated with that resident, and it was confirmed that the noise was not coming from my unit. Based on what I have consistently heard, the source of the noise appears to be from the unit directly above mine, which is on the 22nd floor. At the time of writing this message, it is already 1:00 AM, and the upstairs unit continues to produce repeated dragging noises of furniture. This ongoing disturbance is severely impacting my ability to rest. It also clearly violates the quiet hours policy stated on the notices displayed on each floor of Blk 61. For verification, the management team may wish to speak with both myself and the resident of Unit [unit]. In addition, I have recorded audio evidence of the noise and can provide it if required. I kindly request the property management team to investigate this matter and take appropriate action to resolve the issue as soon as possible. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Sincerely, Ted

Pending
1292-50072026-04-19Blk 59
Persistent engine noise throughout day and night disturbing resident.

Title: Persistent noise across all day and night

Description: Dear Officer, I'd like to report that there is some sort of engine noise from time to time across whole day, which is quite disturbing during sleep (I'm at 59 BLK [unit]). It sounds like air con external unit but I couldn't confirm. It may also come from the lift, or some kinds of renovation, but it's quite strange that the noise can be heard even on 1am every day. This noise has been there for over 1 week, from day to night, and has been significantly disturbing our sleep. Could you please help with this issue? Please also let me know if anything I can do on my end. Thank you so much! PS. I tried to record the noise but this kind of low-frenquency noise can hardly be heard in the recording; it would be good if you could arrange someone to visit and hear the noise in person as it's across all day and night.

MA Reply: [20 Apr 2026] Dear Muli, Thank you for your feedback. Can arrange to visit your unit tomorrow 20 April 2026, 10.30am. Kindly let confirm the schedule to visit your unit. 1292-47992026-03-29Blk 61
Persistent furniture-dragging noise from above every night.

Title: Escalation of Ongoing Noise Disturbance

Description: As I previously reported (Case ID: [phone]), I have been experiencing persistent noise disturbances for over a month. The issue involves furniture-dragging noises lasting several minutes to up to half an hour, occurring almost every night around or after midnight. I initially suspected that the noise originated from Unit #61 21-80. However, after contacting them, they informed me that they were not the source and that they had heard the same noise at the same time. They believe the noise may be coming from an upper-level unit. I am now completely unsure of the source of the noise, but the disturbance consistently occurs during quiet hours and is significantly disrupting our rest. Given the ongoing nature of this issue and the uncertainty regarding its source, I request that mgmt take broader and more effective measures to address it: 1. Kindly remind residents of Units 22-80, 19-80, and 20-72 to avoid dragging furniture during quiet hours, or to use protective padding where necessary. 2. Post notices throughout the building to remind all residents to refrain from dragging furniture during late-night hours, or to use appropriate protective measures. This situation has persisted for an extended period without resolution, and I would greatly appreciate prompt and concrete action to restore a quiet living environment. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [31 Mar 2026] Dear Mr Wang, Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to learn that the noise disturbance has been ongoing and understand how frustrating this must be, especially during late-night hours. We also appreciate you sharing your observations and the steps you have taken to identify the possible source. In view of the situation, we will draft a circular regarding this matter and distribute it to all residents at Blk 61 to remind them to be mindful of noise levels, particularly during quiet hours, and to refrain from dragging furniture or to use appropriate protective measures where necessary. We will continue to monitor the situation and take further action where required. Your patience and understanding are greatly appreciated. Best Regards, Desmond / Rui Ling

1292-47672026-03-24Blk 61
Persistent grating noise from unit above after midnight.

Title: Urgent: Unresolved Noise Disturbance

Description: As I reported previously (case ID [phone]), I am experiencing persistent and severe noise disturbances in my room (Unit 61), most likely originating from the unit above ([unit]). Every night after midnight, there is a sharp, grating noise consistent with furniture being dragged across the floor. This has become a regular occurrence. More concerningly, last Saturday at approximately 3:30 AM, the residents began dragging furniture intermittently for about half an hour, which is completely unacceptable. I had already reported this issue earlier, and while I was informed that the residents had been reminded, the situation has not improved at all. I followed up to indicate that the problem remains unresolved, but I have not received any response for over a week. Additionally, last Saturday midnight, I contacted the security number provided and requested intervention. I was informed that the residents refused to communicate. I then asked for further follow-up, but again received no response. At this point, I must insist that concrete action be taken. Simply reminding the residents is clearly ineffective. I request that management take stronger measures to resolve this issue, including requiring the residents to implement preventive steps such as installing protective pads under furniture, laying carpets, or lifting furniture instead of dragging it. This situation has gone on for far too long and is significantly affecting my ability to rest. I would appreciate a prompt and effective resolution.

MA Reply: [25 Mar 2026] Dear Mr Wang, Thank you for your feedback, and we apologise for the inconvenience caused. We understand how disruptive the noise disturbance can be, especially during late hours. We have attempted to contact the occupants of Unit [unit] but were unable to reach them. The unit owner has since been informed and requested to advise their tenants to take immediate steps to minimise the noise. We will continue to monitor the situation. Should the issue persist, please contact the security team for immediate assistance. Best Regards, Desmond / Rui Ling

I would appreciate it if you could kindly inform the landlord and arrange for an inspection to determine whether there is any potential safety hazard involved. I look forward to your assistance and a timely resolution.

So, if this noise happens again, please call the security directly at [phone] so that they can attend to this noise immediately. We will also get the security to patrol at your unit at nighttime to capture the knocking noise as well. We will close this item at HiLife and if you need us to follow up again, please e-mail to us at [email] Thanks again.

MA Reply: [01 Dec 2025] Hi Xinyu Guo, thank you for your feedback regarding the continuous screaming and loud noises coming from your neighbour between the early hours of 1am to 2am. We sincerely apologize for the disturbance caused. Please be assured that the Management takes such matters seriously. 1292-40442025-11-19Blk 45
Neighbor still shouting loudly at midnight despite prior warning.

Title: Regarding the nighttime noise pollution issue

Description: I apologize for bothering you, but I still want to file a complaint about the noise pollution from apartment 1801. We communicated with them a month ago, but after a month of observation, they are still shouting loudly at midnight, sometimes even 2 or 3 a.m., causing us to be frequently awakened in the middle of the night. I have recordings to provide. Please help with communication and coordination. Thank you.

MA Reply: [20 Nov 2025] Hi Mr. Maxwell, thank you for your e-mail and for bringing this matter to our attention. We are very sorry to hear about the disturbance caused by noise and shouting during midnight hours and early in the morning. We understand how disruptive and stressful this can be, especially during rest hours. We will investigate the situation and speak to the occupants of the unit involved to remind them on the noise. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we work to resolve this matter.

The security has updated us that they have gone to attend to unit 10-28 to stop all noise works as they are only permitted to perform no noise works on Saturdays, between 09:00-01:00PM. 1292-40202025-11-11Blk 53
Resident repeatedly slamming metal object in common area daily.

Title: Damage to common area and noise pollution

Description: I have submitted a feedback on this matter on the 21st of September 2025. I noticed the lady has been doing the same thing every (other) day at around the same time when she has just finished her coffee - I reckoned the metal item is part of a coffee machine. Not only is she causing disturbance to people who are working from home, she is also wrecking the wall next to the balcony which can cause structure and aesthetic damage. Can the management do something about this unruly behavior?

MA Reply: [11 Nov 2025] Hi Mr. John Yeng, thank you for your feedback regarding the noise pollution coming from the unit below. We understand that such disturbances can be disruptive and appreciate you bringing this matter to our attention. Our team will approach the resident concerned to remind them to be considerate and to avoid causing any noise disturbance, especially quiet hours. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

Closed
1292-39892025-11-03Blk 49
Upstairs neighbor noise worsening, daily dropping and running sounds.

Title: Inconsiderate neighbour, happening again.

Description: Hello, I posted a feedback about a year ago about my upstairs neighbour, but I think the situation has gone from bad to worse. These people are so inconsiderate, not just causing a ruckus on the weekends (dropping things almost on a daily basis, running back and forth, stomping on the ground, slamming doors), this is happening on the weekdays as well. Just yesterday (Sunday night), these guys have kept me up until ungodly hours. I’ve called the security, and I’m not sure if they actually went to talk to the people in the unit on top of me, but the constant noise continued. To add on to that, I had to see a doctor because I was constantly having panic attacks due to this. I am no longer having good sleep, I am always cranky because of this nonsense. I’ve tried to reason with them, but this is just getting out of hand. I would also like to suggest that when management goes to talk to them, please include me. I need to get things off my chest. Thanks.

MA Reply: [03 Nov 2025] HiJacko Is Not My Name, thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand that the persistent noise from your neighboring has been causing significant disturbance and we appreciate your patience and cooperation in addressing the issue. Please be assured that the Management takes noise disturbance complaints seriously and our Security team will also continue to monitor the situation. We will approach the resident above your unit to have better understanding and gather more information from them. Please allow us a week to address this matter and will update you again. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation. --- [07 Nov 2025] Hi Jacko Is Not My Name, thank you for your feedback on the persistent noise disturbance from the unit above. We have approached the resident above your unit, and they have shared that they are not aware of any noise coming from the premises. Furthermore, we have also arranged the Security Officers to patrol at the above unit for 4 consecutive nights at different timing and so far, there wasn't any noise detected at all. We would appreciate it if you could assist us by recording the specific dates and noise occurs as this will help us to identify any recurring pattern and take the appropriate follow-up actions where necessary. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation as we continue to ensure a peaceful living environment for all residents. Thanks again. --- [13 Nov 2025] Hi Mr. Haris, thanks for the feedback. We have checked from the Security, and they have attended twice to your call on early Monday morning at 1.24am and 2.22am and discovered that the noise was coming from your next-door neighbor and not from above your unit. Your next-door neighbor had acknowledged it when the Security approached them about the noise disturbance, and they have complied it Thank you for your patience and understanding. --- [12 Mar 2026] We will close this case.

Closed
1292-39252025-10-17Blk 55
Neighbor still slamming chute cabinet door loudly at 2am.

Title: Neighbor slam the chute bin cabinet door at night

The same neighbor still slam the door loudly as normal- example as of tonight as it wake me up at 2 am and i need to escalate the same issue again. Could you please help putting the warning sign at the chute cabinet door? I hope that person would be more considered after seeing the warning. Thank you.

MA Reply: [17 Oct 2025] Dear Minnie, Thank you for your email and for bringing this matter to our attention once again. We understand your concern regarding the noise from the chute cabinet door. Closed
1292-39122025-10-14Blk 57
Repeated loud shouting from neighbor nightly until 11:30pm.

Title: URGENT: Repeated Noise Disturbance fm Unit [unit]

Description: Dear MCST Team, I am writing to express my frustration regarding the ongoing noise disturbance from my neighbour at Unit [unit]. Once again, the occupant was shouting and scolding loudly from around 8:00pm until 11:30pm last night. Despite my effort to knock on his door and request that he lower his voice, he refused to respond. As the disturbance continued, I had no choice but to contact your security guard for assistance. However, even after the guard’s intervention, the shouting resumed again in the middle of the night, causing my dog to keep barking and seriously disrupting our sleep. This is not the first time such an incident has happened, and it has become extremely intolerable. I strongly urge the Management to take immediate and firm action against the occupant — including issuing an official warning and notifying the landlord to address the matter with their tenant. Residents deserve a peaceful living environment, and I hope this issue will be treated with the seriousness it warrants. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [15 Oct 2025] Hi ACAC, thank you for your e-mail and for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand that the persistent shouting and loud scolding from your neighboring unit between 8pm to 11.30pm has been causing significant disturbance and we appreciate your patience and cooperation in addressing the issue. Please be assured that the Management takes noise disturbance complaints seriously. We have gone up to the said unit twice which was yesterday and today, and nobody was around. Apart from that, we have sent an e-mail to them to be mindful of the noise level particularly during evening hours. At the same time, we have informed the Security Team to go up to the unit and continue monitor the noise situation during the mentioned period and take the necessary follow-up actions. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

Closed
1292-38952025-10-10Blk 59
Neighbor at Blk 61 screaming daily morning and evening with door open.

Title: Extreme Noise from next door neighbor

Description: Dear Sirs, I would like to bring to your attention that the residents at unit [unit] have been constantly screaming on a daily basis, typically occurring twice a day—once in the morning and once in the evening. They often leave their door open, and the noise level has affected all residents staying on the same floor. Everyone is suffering. Please look into this matter and appreciate your soonest response.

MA Reply: [11 Oct 2025] Thank you for your message and for bringing this matter to our attention. We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience caused by the ongoing noise disturbance from unit [unit]. We understand how disruptive this has been for you and the other residents on the same floor. We will be reaching out to the occupants of the mentioned unit to address the issue and remind them of the community guidelines regarding noise levels and being considerate of their neighbors. In the meantime, should the noise occur again, please do not hesitate to contact our Security hotline at [phone] so that our team can promptly attend to the matter and advise the residents to keep their door closed. We appreciate your patience and cooperation, and we will keep you updated on any further developments.

Closed
1292-38842025-10-08Blk 57
Severe persistent noise nuisance from next-door neighbor daily.

Title: Noisy neighbour 19-59

Description: Dear Normanton Park Management, I am writing to file an urgent complaint regarding a severe and persistent noise nuisance emanating from my next-door neighbor, Unit 19-59. The Nature of the Disturbance This is a chronic and escalating issue that has been ongoing since we moved in in late July. Source: Unit 19-59, located next door to my residence. Nature of Noise: Frequent, extremely loud shouting and screaming. Timing: Since we moved in in July, the noise has occured about 70% in the last 2-3 months. It usually begins at 11:00 PM and often continues late into the night, frequently lasting up till or even beyond 2:00 AM, after we have slept. Impact and By-Law Violation The noise level is severe enough to be clearly and constantly heard within my unit, making normal nighttime activities impossible. The consistent shouting and screaming between 11:00 PM and 2:00 AM is a direct breach of community by-laws regarding Quiet Hours. This chronic disturbance severely impacts the peaceful enjoyment of our home, preventing us from relaxing and disturbing our ability to get adequate rest. Demand for Action I respectfully request that the management team take immediate and decisive action to investigate this matter. Immediate Notification: Please issue a formal warning to the resident of Unit 19-59 outlining the time, type, and severity of the disturbance and reminding them of the quiet hour regulations. Enforcement: Please implement the necessary measures to ensure this behavior ceases immediately and permanently. This chronic issue requires prompt intervention to restore the quiet enjoyment of my residence.

MA Reply: [08 Oct 2025] Hi Fel K, thank you for your e-mail and for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand your concern regarding the severe and persistent noise disturbance from your next-door neighbor especially during late hours between 11pm to 2am. Please be assured that the Management takes such feedback seriously and we will approach the unit [unit] and remind the residents involved to be considerate and to avoid causing noise nuisance particularly during the night. If the noise still persists over the late-night hours, please call the security directly at their hotline no. [phone]. We appreciate your patience and understanding while we work to resolve this matter.

Closed

Noisy neighbor, wants investigation done before case is closed.

Title: its noisy again at this time 12:01am

Description: whats the status of the platform - has this been investigated? pls dont close. i want this open until the investigation is done. please do your duty as a management office in ensuring all renovation permits are aligned to the construction done inside the unit !!

MA Reply: [01 Oct 2025] Hi Mr. Regge, thank you for the feedback. We note your request for Management Office to follow up closely on the noise matter. We have followed up with your neighbor (22-33) and they have mentioned to us that they will mitigate the noise disturbances so that it will not affect the surrounding neighbors. It seems their noise mitigation is still insufficient as the noise still occurred again, so we have sent another reminder to the resident again to address this matter. We will not close this issue and keep you updated on any significant developments, and we appreciate your patience and understanding while we work towards resolving this matter. Thanks again. --- [02 Oct 2025] Hi Mr. Regge, as spoke just now, we will have a discussion tomorrow at 3pm Function Room 2 together with the Management and the affected neighbor. Thanks again. --- [09 Jan 2026] This case will be closed in Hi Life, and the update will be communicated via email.

Closed
1292-38352025-09-29Blk 61
Neighbor repeatedly splashing water from balcony onto unit below.

Title: Repeated Inconsiderate Behavior by Neighbor

Description: Hi Normanton Management , I am writing to formally raise a concern regarding our neighbors’ repeated inconsiderate behavior that has been affecting us in our unit. On Saturday (28th of September) at around 2:10 PM, water was splashed from their balcony for almost ten minutes. Unfortunately, during that time, we were drying our clothes outside and the water caused our balcony floor to become wet. This is not the first occurrence—we previously overlooked the same issue thinking it was only a one-time incident. For your reference, I have attached a video recording of the incident that shows the water splashing onto our balcony. In addition to this, we have been experiencing disturbances late at night from loud noises such as heavy or careless walking and other disruptive sounds. These have been frequent enough to interrupt our rest and daily activities. We respectfully request that this matter be addressed, as we value a peaceful and considerate living environment for everyone. We hope that a reminder can be given to our neighbors about being mindful of their actions. Thank you for your understanding and prompt attention to this matter. Sincerely, Aerone Ong 61 Normanton Park, [unit] [Attachment: Video Recording of Incident]

MA Reply: [30 Sep 2025] Dear Aerone, Thank you for your feedback and for sharing the video recording. We have informed the unit owner regarding the matter. The owner has acknowledged the issue and conveyed her sincere apologies. She believes that the incident was caused by her helper and has assured us that she will speak with her helper to ensure that such incidents do not happen again. We appreciate your patience and understanding, and we remain committed to maintaining a considerate living environment for all residents. Thanks again for bringing this matter to management.

Closed
1292-38192025-09-26Blk 51
Follow-up on thumping noise and renovation permit issue, case closed prematurely.

Title: issue id : [phone]

Description: this is related to the thumping issue that I brought up and also if they are the renovation permit to construct the ledge. im not happy why the issue was closed without informing me the outcome. can you kindly pls call and let me know about this? this morning AGAIN so noisy, been awakened at 6:30am due to that. it is getting worse everyday

MA Reply: [26 Sep 2025] Dear sir, We refer to your feedback above regarding the subject matter and we apologize that without any update for the matter. For your information, we have visited the neighbor but the owner mentioned she is not convenient at this moment to entertain us and she requested us to come back again at a comfortable. Apart from that also requested her to take the photo of the elevated ledge but she turned down. Additionally, we have wrote an email to them regarding the matter also, they have acknowledged and replied us that they will work towards mitigating any noise disturbances. Based on this, we are currently treating the case as closed. However, if the noise issue persists, we kindly prefer you to record the sound as evidence and email it to us at [email] , or contact our security hotline at [phone] so we can further monitor the situation. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

Loud thumping from neighbor's raised kitchen ledge day and night.

Title: renovation permit

Description: i am constantly hearing loud thumping sound from my neighbor next to me (22-33). Apparently, they have asked the contractor to put an elevated ledge at their kitchen that everytime they walk into it, it is producing a loud thumping sound both daytime and nighttime. i have already asked them few times to keep it down, but it wasnt helping. it was really disturbing and loud especially during early morning and late night. i am wondering if they have sought renovation permit with this kind of renovation and design, and was the elevated ledge approved by admin? was this part of what they submitted to you as part of their renovation? i really need some help here. it is affecting me on a daily basis due to the loud sound. i dont hear my other neighbor to the other side but this one is really loud due to this elevated ledge in their kitchen!!! thanks for checking in theie renovation design and permit.

MA Reply: [17 Sep 2025] Hi Mr. Regge, thanks for the feedback regarding the thumping sound you heard from the neighboring unit. Please be assured that the Management takes all feedback on noise disturbance seriously. As spoke to you just now, we will visit your neighbour tomorrow to understand more on this noise disturbance. Noisy works on Blk 57 rooftop above level 24.

Title: Works on the top floor of Blk 57 above level #24

Description: Hi Pls check and advise what sort of works are ongoing at the rooftop of blk 57 above Level 24 as there has been noises of banging and items and loud drops of tools etc early mornings on weekday and weekends on some occasions as well. it would be good to put a notification to the residents so that we avoid workers moving around and rooftop accesses with such noises unknowingly.

MA Reply: [09 Sep 2025] Thank you for your feedback regarding the recent noise disturbances from the rooftop of Block 57 above Level 24. We understand the disruption caused by the sounds of banging, falling items, and loud drops of tools. Following your reports, we’ve consulted with the main contractor, and they confirm that no work is being conducted on the rooftop at the moment. However, please note that renovation work is currently ongoing at Block 57, Level 10, from 8 September 2025 to 7 October 2025 (as listed in hi-life). These works may be contributing to the noise. Should you hear any further noise from the rooftop, kindly call the security team immediately so we can investigate and address the issue promptly. Thank you for your patience and cooperation.

Thank you again for bringing this matter to management attention.

1292-33232025-06-05Blk 51
Loud piano playing after 10pm from Blk 51 unit.

Title: Piano Noise disturbing the neighours 51-2033

Description: The resident of BLK 51 - 2033 frequently practices the piano repeatedly after 10 PM, which has caused significant disturbance to the neighbors.

MA Reply: [06 Jun 2025] Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand how late-night noise, particularly from musical instruments, can be disruptive to residents’ rest and well-being. Please be assured that we take such feedback seriously in our efforts to promote a peaceful living environment for all. We will reach out to the resident of [unit], Block 51, to remind them to refrain from playing the piano or any musical instruments after 10:00 PM, in line with community guidelines on noise control. They will be advised to confine such activities to reasonable hours and to be mindful of their neighbors. You may also contact the security at [phone] Should the issue persist despite this reminder, please do not hesitate to update us. We will monitor the situation and take further steps if necessary. Thank you once again for your cooperation.

1292-32372025-05-16Blk 53

Title: Noise from Blk53 [unit]

Description: Hi, I am writing to report a noise issue originating from the unit above mine, [unit], Blk53. I reside in unit [unit]. I have been consistently disturbed by the sound of chairs being dragged or moved on the balcony and within the apartment at various times throughout the day and night. This noise has become quite disruptive to my peaceful enjoyment of my home. I kindly request your assistance in addressing this matter. Perhaps you could reach out to the residents of [unit] and suggest they consider implementing measures to reduce the noise, such as installing noise-dampening pads on their chair legs. Thank you for your time and attention to this issue. I look forward to your assistance in resolving this. Thank you

MA Reply: [16 May 2025] Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand how important a peaceful living environment is for all residents. We will reach out to the occupants of unit [unit] to remind them about being mindful of noise, especially during early mornings and late evenings. Please allow us some time to follow up with them. Should the noise persist after our intervention, do not hesitate to inform the security at [phone] We appreciate your patience and cooperation.

MA Reply: [10 Apr 2026] Dear Mr Gary, Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand your concerns regarding the cigarette smoke affecting your unit. At present, there are no specific by-laws or house rules that prohibit residents from smoking within their own premises, including at their balconies. That said, we recognise the inconvenience this may cause to neighbouring units. As part of our efforts to maintain a harmonious living environment, we will be reaching out to the resident involved to highlight your feedback. We will also appeal to their understanding and request that they be more considerate, such as reducing the frequency of smoking and, where possible, avoiding smoking at the balcony. We appreciate your patience and understanding as we manage this matter, and we will continue to encourage mutual respect and cooperation among residents. Please feel free to contact us if you require further assistance. Best Regards, Rui Ling

MA Reply: [30 Jan 2026] Hi Tzy Tiing Lim, thank you for the feedback regarding the strong cigarette smell entering your unit. We understand that this has caused you inconvenience and concern. We will approach the resident underneath your unit to be mindful of the neighbors during smoking. Apart from that, we will also put a notice to reminding the residents in the vicinity to refrain from smoking near the windows or balconies to prevent smoke from drifting into neighboring units. In the meantime, you may consider keeping your windows closed temporarily when the smell is detected or using an air purifier to help reduce the odour. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation. Thanks again.

We take such matters seriously as smoking in the common areas including staircases is strictly prohibited. We will inform the security to increase patrols at the affected blocks and monitor the staircase landings at different levels to identify the source. We will also paste up the NO SMOKING signage after we have identified the areas. Thank you for your cooperation, and we appreciate your patience.

Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention and we appreciate your patience and cooperation.
