1292-40322025-11-16Blk 61
Increasing cigarette smoke drifting into unit via windows.

Title: Cigarette smoke drifting in

Description: Hi Normanton management team, we have experienced an increasing frequency of cigarette smoke drifting in via our kitchen windows, balcony and other room windows. We understand that you previously posted a notice in May about secondhand smoke drifting in via balconies in block 61, but these smokers don't use hilife. We also personally talked to the tenants of a unit (they smoke), but to no avail. Could we request for management to print the May notice in both Mandarin and English and put them up in the lift lobbies of each level at block 61 please? Perhaps these would get the smokers' attention. Thank you.

MA Reply: [17 Nov 2025] Dear Ness, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks. We acknowledge your concern regarding the recurring cigarette smoke drifting into your unit. As part of our efforts to remind residents of the importance of considerate behavior, we have already put up the notices in both English and Mandarin at the lift lobbies of Block 61. Closed
1292-40272025-11-14Blk 53
Smoking smell more prevalent at Blk 53, asking for options.

Title: smoking smel

Description: hi, there has been more prevalent smoking smell at block 53. I’m aware limited can be done, what are options available? I’m aware some condo pass by-law on smoking at balcony. Thanks for help.

MA Reply: [15 Nov 2025] Dear Kean Leong Chng, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation regarding a prevalent smoking smell at Block 53. While the MCST is not able to pass a valid by-law regarding smoking at the balconies (as it will be in conflict with NEA's list of permitted smoking areas), the management can encourage and educate the residents of good neighbourly habits, which includes avoiding causing nuisance via excessive smoking. At the same time, management will continue to work closely with security to enforce and educate any person found smoking at the common areas of the condominium as it is prohibited under the Act. We hope this will reduce the impact of secondhand smoking amicably. Thank you for your kind attention. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

We understand your concern and apologise for the inconvenience caused. Please be informed that while smoking within individual units is a private matter, residents are reminded to be considerate and ensure that smoke does not cause nuisance to neighboring units. The Management will conduct checks and monitor the affected levels (10 to 14) to identify any possible sources. We appreciate your kind understanding and cooperation as we look into this matter. Thank you for your patience and cooperation.

Since you never observed anyone smoking at the balcony edge, we will remind residents in the vicinity to refrain from smoking near windows or balconies to prevent smoke from drifting into neighboring units. In the meantime, you may consider keeping your windows closed temporarily when the smell is detected or using an air purifier to help reduce the odour. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation as we work towards maintaining a comfortable living environment for all residents.

Closed
1292-38832025-10-07Blk 57
Widespread cigarette smoke intrusion at Blk 57 common areas.

Title: Smokers at Block 57

Description: Dear Normanton Park Management, I am writing to file an urgent and formal complaint regarding continuous and widespread secondhand smoke intrusion that is creating a persistent health hazard and community nuisance within our estate. The Problem: Consistent Smoke Drift from Named Units The smoke nuisance occurs consistently in the evenings and is confirmed to originate from multiple sources using balconies as smoking areas. The primary units repeatedly identified by multiple residents as sources of smoke drift include, but are not limited to, Unit 16-60 and Unit 11-59. Smoke from tobacco products being consumed on these balconies frequently enters residential units through windows, balcony doors, and shared ventilation systems. Critical Health Justification (Priority Concern) This situation is paramount due to a medical crisis: the intrusion constitutes a serious, immediate health hazard affecting vulnerable residents across multiple units in Block 57. Direct Threat: I currently have a bedbound patient with a severely compromised immune system in my unit. Exposure to secondhand smoke poses an immediate and significant danger to their respiratory health. Community-Wide Risk: Multiple neighbors have documented that they also house elderly parents and young children, all of whom require access to clean air but cannot open their windows due to the smoke. The introduction of toxins into the breathing space of our most vulnerable groups is unacceptable and requires the fastest possible intervention to protect the health of these at-risk residents. Collective Impact and Documented Evidence This is not an isolated neighbor dispute but a major community enforcement issue. The widespread nature of this problem, and the resulting health concerns, is publicly documented and verifiable within two specific, large resident communication platforms: the general Normanton Park Telegram group chat and the specific Blk 57 resident chat. Demand for Immediate and Comprehensive Enforcement I respectfully request that the management team take immediate action to investigate and resolve this matter, requiring a comprehensive response that addresses all offending units: Enforcement of By-Laws: I request immediate and strict enforcement of all relevant by-laws regarding nuisance, smoke drift, and the use of common elements (balconies). Immediate Notification: Please communicate the seriousness of this complaint, citing the documented collective concerns and the critical health risks involved, directly to the residents of Units 16-60 and 11-59 (and any other named units). Definitive Resolution: I ask for a concrete plan to ensure my unit remains permanently free of secondhand smoke, thereby resolving both the individual health crisis and the documented collective community nuisance. Please treat this as the highest priority due to the medical and widespread nature of the complaint. Thank you!

MA Reply: [09 Oct 2025] Hi Fel K, thank you for your feedback regarding cigarette smoke emanating from the units below your apartment. We understand your concern about the potential health effects of the second-hand smoke and the discomfort it causes. Please be assured that the Management takes such matters seriously. We will issue a reminder to the residents to refrain from smoking in areas where the smoke may drift into neighboring units. Apart from that we will also advise the smokers to do so with windows and balcony doors closed. We will also continue to encourage residents to adopt a considerate approach so that everyone can enjoy a comfortable living environment. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention.

Closed
1292-38022025-09-21Blk 57
Ongoing smoking at Blk 57 level 19 common area causing discomfort.

Title: Smoking Activities at 57, Level 19

Description: Dear Management, I hope this message finds you well. I would like to bring to your attention the ongoing smoking activities at the common area of 57, Level 19, which have been causing significant discomfort and health concerns for residents. On the morning of 20 September 2025, when the lift doors opened, the entire corridor was filled with a strong cigarette odor, making it difficult to breathe. We observed a lady smoking in the corridor at the time, but unfortunately, we were unable to identify which unit she is from. In addition, we experienced similarly strong cigarette smells seeping into our unit on 5August 2025 and 15 September 2025, causing further discomfort. We kindly seek your assistance to look into this matter and take appropriate enforcement actions to ensure that the common areas remain smoke-free in accordance with the estate’s regulations. Thank you for your attention and support.

MA Reply: [22 Sep 2025] Dear Sir, Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. For your information, we have prepared signage to remind all residents that smoking is strictly prohibited in the common areas and will be placing them accordingly. We wish to highlight that we have uploaded the circular regarding the subject matter in our resident app. We appreciate your cooperation and understanding.

I was in the same lift as this young Chinese chap, with hair length slightly above shoulders. He denied when I asked why is he smoking in the lift. It is clear he has a lit cigarette in his hand and there is smoke in the air The lift came from above level 8 and he got off at level 4 and turn right when he exited the lift I like to bring this to your attention because smoking in the lift is not just prohibited but also a hazard to others. I hope the management will monitor this closely and to take appropriate actions where necessary If you need to identify the person, I can assist with you security camera footage. I was the only one in the lift with him & I was in a grey top and grey shorts

MA Reply: [19 Sep 2025] Dear Vanessa, We refer to your feedback above with thanks and appreciate your prompt action and concern for the safety of others. Please be assured that we take this matter seriously and monitor the situation closely. Smoking in lifts is strictly prohibited, and appropriate action will be taken if necessary. 1292-37052025-08-29Blk 45
Persistent cigarette smoke from neighbouring unit.

Title: Persistent Cigarette Smoke Smell

Description: Subject: Complaint on Persistent Cigarette Smoke Smell from Unit I am writing to lodge a formal complaint regarding the persistent and intrusive cigarette smoke smell that has been affecting my unit, [unit], for a prolonged period. Despite our patience, the situation has not improved, but became intolerable and requires urgent intervention by the management. The smoke smell occurs at various times of the day (morning, afternoon and night) and almost everyday. The smell is especially strong in all rooms facing the main swimming pool. As a result, during those times, my household is compelled to keep windows and balcony doors closed, severely limiting ventilation and preventing us from using our balcony. This has reduced the enjoyment of our own home. It is also unfair that we are subjected to health risks from second hand smoke coming from other households. I urge the management to treat this matter with utmost seriousness and to investigate the source of the smoke without delay. Appropriate enforcement measures (such as posting an internal circular) should also be taken as soon as possible to ensure smoking activities do not adversely affect other residents. Thank you.

MA Reply: [30 Aug 2025] Hi DavinaLax, thanks for the feedback. We have activated our Security Officer on this matter, and they have knocked at your unit yesterday around 4pm and 5pm plus but nobody is around. During the 2 timings that they went up to your unit, they have also checked on the common corridor and the fire exit staircase as well but there was no smell of cigarettes at all. So, in future if it happens again on the cigarette smell, please contact directly at the Security Hotline at [phone]. Thanks again.

Closed
1292-36692025-08-23Blk 53
Recurring cigarette smoke smell at Blk 53 lobby and carpark.

Title: SMOKING SMELL AGAIN AT BLK 53

Description: hello MA, this is the 3rd time I am writing. The situation improved for a few days but it is back and getting worse. Please go smell the lobby and the carpark. It stinks with Cigg and Air Freshener smell. We are owners and residents of the estate, such things should happen to us and for us to endure what the cleaners are doing. Please resolve it ASAP, if things do not improve will be calling in HSA and NEA.

MA Reply: [23 Aug 2025] Dear Yuen Sern Seah, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation regarding the smoking smell outside of Blk 53 Basement Lobby and are sorry to hear of the inconvenience you experienced. Management have gone to investigate the area highlighted and observed the same smoking and air freshener smell, but did not see anyone smoking in the area. Kindly note that we spotted residents smoking in the area before as well and have given them sufficient warning as well that smoking in the carpark is prohibited. To further deter smoking in the area, we will put up more "no smoking" signs in the area closer to Blk 53 and increase security patrol in the area. Additionally, we have since contacted the cleaning contractor as well to be on alert of these smoking incidents and to alert any smoking to the management/security when spotted. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for your kind attetion and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

1292-36592025-08-21Blk 53

Title: Blk 53 basement carpark strong cigarette smoke

Description: hi, this has been a recurring problem about the cleaners smoking within the rest area/office area at Blk 53 next to Blk 53 basement side entrance. the cigarette smoke permeates into the air conditioned lobby, and into the carpark, when residents exit out of B1 lobby to get into our vehicles, the basement is filled with cigarette smell AND can be misty. additionally when we get into our vehicle, naturally this cigarette stench wafts into the car and remains inside our car, we have to wind down the window to get it out. I would like to ask management to look into this, they should not be smoking inside a rest area, the cigarette is vented into the smoke free air conditioned lobby. if this matter persists I would escalate to NEA or MP, as this is a common public space and area, not a matter about private smoking space.

MA Reply: [21 Aug 2025] Dear Claudia, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation of the cigarette smell found near Blk 53 basement lift lobby. We will forward this matter to the cleaning company to take the necessary action. Additionally, we will go to the rest area to remind the cleaners that smoking is not permitted in this area. Moving forward, kindly share with us any photos/videos of the cleaners smoking in the rest area if possible and email it to us at [email] so that we may forward it to the company as well. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

1292-36452025-08-18Blk 57
Persistent smoking and vaping from neighbor despite prior warning.

Title: Persistent Smoking and Possible Vaping

Description: Hi, sorry for the incomplete feedback earlier. Thank you for following up on my earlier feedback and taking action to communicate to the tenants and landlord about being more considerate when smoking. However, the occupants of the same unit [unit] have persisted to be inconsiderate and continue to smoke in the balcony daily in the evenings and causing discomfort. I am concerned about my own well-being and health. I am constantly exposed to second-hand smoke on a regular basis as the balconies of both units are facing each other without any wall separation. I enjoy the cool breeze from my balcony in the evenings as I unwind after work but I am unable to do so due to the smoking habits of the occupants in the next unit. Perhaps another reminder to the tenants and occupants is needed as not much change has taken place. On the another note, I have also noticed the strong scent of vape along the corridor in the morning when I leave for work around 7 am near the lift lobby. The scent is much stronger on Sunday mornings. I am very concerned about this as well as there are young children living on the same floor. The exposure to vape is detrimental to their development as well as my own health. With the stricter regulations about vaping recently, perhaps it would be good to send a timely reminder that vaping is illegal.

MA Reply: [19 Aug 2025] Hi Ms Doreen Tan, thank you for the feedback on the smell of cigarettes at the balcony and vaping along the common corridor. We understand your concern and would like to assure you that vaping and smoking are strictly prohibited in the condominium's common areas as it causes inconvenience to other residents and affect the overall environment. We will put up a notification at level 24 as to remind all residents on the smoking at balcony causing discomfort and health concerns. We truly appreciate your effort in helping us maintain a pleasant living environment for everyone. Thanks again.

MA Reply: [24 Jul 2025] Thank you for your update and for providing the specific unit number. We appreciate your clarification regarding the smoking originating from unit [unit] at Blk 57. We will issue a reminder to the residents of [unit] to be more considerate, particularly when smoking near shared spaces or open windows. Please do not hesitate to reach out if the issue persists. Title: smoking in balcony

Description: hi, my neighbors (husband and wife) has been smoking daily in his balcony and the second hand smoke affecting my quality of life and enjoyment of staying at Normanton Park. may I know if there are any by-laws about smoking in balconies? thank you.

MA Reply: [23 Jul 2025] Thank you for the Feedback. As of now in Singapore, there are no specific laws or estate by-laws that outright ban smoking at private residential balconies. The Smoking (Prohibition in Certain Places) Act mainly applies to common areas in public places (like void decks, corridors, stairwells, and lift lobbies in HDBs), but not within private property. -w

Closed
1292-35202025-07-22Blk 51
Secondhand smoke from neighbour entering unit every evening.

Title: Neighbour smoking in balcony

Description: I have been experiencing second hand smoke in my unit every evening after 9pm. I believe it comes a smoking neighbour from the units a few floors below mine. kindly look into this matter. thank you

MA Reply: [23 Jul 2025] Thank you for your feedback and for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand your concern regarding the cigarette smoke affecting your unit. Please be informed that while there is currently no regulation under the law prohibiting residents from smoking within their own private balconies, we do recognize that second-hand smoke can be a serious nuisance to neighbours. To address this, we will be issuing an advisory notice to remind all residents to be considerate of their neighbours, especially when smoking at balconies or near windows. In order to better follow up on the matter, may we kindly ask if you are able to identify the specific unit(s) where the heavy smoking appears to be coming from? This will help us to gently remind the resident(s) involved directly. We appreciate your cooperation and understanding as we work to maintain a pleasant and healthy living environment for all. -w --- [31 Jul 2025] We are truly sorry to hear that you're experiencing issues with secondhand smoke in your unit. Your comfort and well-being are very important to us. Please feel free to call our security team if you’d like them to visit your unit, investigate the situation, and identify the responsible party. They’ll approach the situation carefully and respectfully to help resolve the issue. Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

Closed
1292-35052025-07-18Blk 47
Cigarette butt found on balcony from upper floor.

Title: Cigarette Butt found on Balcony

Description: Dear MCST, I have found used cigarette butt on my balcony. There are no smokers in my family. I suspected the cigarette butt is disposed by my neighbour on the upper floors. Appreciate if you can send a reminder to all residents to be considerate, smoke within their premises and not to dispose cigarette butts unethically. I have kept the cigarette butt as evidence, let me know if u need it. Thanks.

MA Reply: [18 Jul 2025] Hi Min Li Lee, thanks for the feedback and bringing this matter to our attention. We understand your concerns about the safety and inconvenience caused by cigarette butts landing on your balcony. We will send the reminder through HiLife to all the residents which are staying above your unit so as to be considerate when they are smoking around the premises. Thanks again.

Closed
1292-34942025-07-14Blk 47
Excessive smoking smell from neighbour affecting multiple units.

Title: excessive smoking

Description: hi, I’m from block 47, 17-11. my neighbours and I from 18-11, 16-11 are all having issues with excessive smoking smell which bothers us on a daily basis. please get someone to check which unit is this coming from

MA Reply: [15 Jul 2025] Dear Annie, thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear about the inconvenience you are experiencing from excessive smoking. We seek your assistance in providing a timeframe when the smoking usually happens. After which we will forward this information to the security to perform their investigation.

In Progress
1292-32822025-05-26Blk 57
Cigarette butt landed on balcony from upper floor.

Title: Cigarette butt in balcony

Description: Good afternoon. Found a cigarette butt in my balcony today. Looks like someone threw it over their unit and it landed on my balcony hand rail or zip track and it fell in as I lifted up the zip track. This is the second time this month. I thought the first was random as it has never happened before. It happened again today.

MA Reply: [26 May 2025] Thank you for the feedback. We are sorry to hear about the incident involving a cigarette butt landing in your balcony, and we understand your concern—particularly given the potential fire hazard and the disturbance this causes to your household. Please be assured that high-rise littering, including the disposal of cigarette butts from windows or balconies, is strictly prohibited under NEA regulations and our estate’s house rules. As part of our follow-up, we will visit the two units directly above yours to advise the occupants on the proper disposal of smoking materials and to raise awareness about the safety risks involved. In addition, we will be issuing a circular to all residents to remind everyone of the dangers and legal consequences of high-rise littering. We appreciate your vigilance in reporting this matter and your commitment to maintaining a safe and pleasant living environment for all. Should you encounter any further incidents, please do not hesitate to reach out to us. William Wong (MR) Condominium Executive Normanton Park --- [26 May 2025] We have personally visited the units above, and the Managing Agent (MA) has also conducted checks to investigate the ongoing issue of cigarette butts being discarded from height. In addition, we have put up reminders and a circular to raise awareness and discourage such behaviour. However, the residents either denied smoking or refused to admit responsibility. We will continue to monitor the situation and take further action if necessary to ensure the safety and well-being of all residents. --- [28 May 2025] it is hard to pinpoint exactly where the cigarette butt came from, especially in a high-rise setting where wind or carelessness from any upper unit could be the cause. It’s thoughtful of you to consider the possibility that it may not be just the immediate neighbors. A gentle reminder from the management pasted notice about proper cigarette disposal and the risks of high-rise littering might encourage more responsible behavior overall. Hopefully, with more awareness, residents will indeed be more considerate moving forward.

Closed

Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

We hope this has helped reduce the impact of the singing noise you previously experienced. --- [04 Nov 2025] Thank you for your message. We understand your concern regarding the noise disturbances. We have already reminded the resident in question to keep their windows closed and to refrain from singing or making loud noises after 10:00 p.m. --- [26 Nov 2025] Could we please have your email address so that we can continue communication through email? My manager is willing to arrange a mediation session if both parties agree to participate. --- [09 Jan 2026] This case will be closed in Hi Life, and the update will be communicated via email.

Description: Good afternoon I noticed a plastic bag stuck outside of my AC ledge area ceiling, from the unit upstairs, after this afternoon wind and heavy rain. I tried to remove it in case it gets blown in, but it looks like the plastic is clipped to the railings using a clothes peg, see photo. Can you please check? Thank you.

MA Reply: [19 May 2025] Dear SLee, thanks for the feedback. We have gone up to the unit above yours and have already removed the plastic bag which was stuck at the aircon ledge. thanks again.

Renovation at Blk 53 causing dust with door left open.

Title: Blk53 10-42 work

Description: Hi Blk53 [unit]’s contractors are working without closing their door. dust and smell from renovation work are spreading in level 10 corridor and into my unit. Pls let them close their door during the work.

MA Reply: [14 May 2025] Thank you for the feedback. We have informed the security to remind them regarding this matter. Any enquire, please do drop us a feedback.

Closed
1292-32162025-05-13Blk 47
Persistent cooking smell from neighbouring unit.

Title: Request for Assistance Regarding Persistent Cookin

Description: I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to seek your assistance with an ongoing issue concerning persistent cooking odors emanating from the neighboring unit. Despite previous efforts to mitigate the situation, including closing my windows and discussing the matter directly with the neighbor, the odors continue to affect my living environment. To provide context: Previous Actions Taken: I have closed my windows as per your earlier advice and have spoken with the neighbor to address the concern directly. Current Impact: The cooking odors remain pervasive, particularly during morning, evening and even wee hours too, and continue to disrupt my daily activities and comfort within my home. Given that these odors significantly impact my quality of life, I kindly request that management intervene to find a resolution. One potential solution could be to request that the neighbor use their kitchen exhaust hood while cooking, which may help contain and reduce the spread of odors. I appreciate your attention to this matter and look forward to your guidance on the next steps to address this issue effectively. Thank you for your support.

MA Reply: [13 May 2025] Thank you for reaching out and bringing this matter to our attention. We understand how unpleasant and disruptive persistent cooking odors can be and appreciate your efforts to resolve the issue directly with your neighbor. As this matter relates to activities within individual units, our authority to intervene is limited. However, we will issue a general reminder to residents to be considerate of their neighbors and to ensure the proper use of kitchen exhaust systems while cooking, especially during early or late hours. Should the issue persist, you may wish to consider installing draft stoppers or air purifiers to help improve air circulation within your unit. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in maintaining a harmonious living environment at Normanton Park. Please do not hesitate to contact us should you require further assistance.

Closed

Title: The neighbour above me [unit] throw water

I have tried to be amicable but she is really very inconsiderate, and I need the management to speak to her. I have our chat records as proof that this is an ongoing issue.

Such behaviour is not acceptable, especially when it affects the cleanliness and comfort of other residents. To assist us in our follow-up, we would appreciate it if you could share the chat records or any relevant correspondence with us. Please be assured that any information provided will be treated with strict confidentiality and used solely for the purpose of addressing this matter. We will be engaging the resident of the upper unit to address the issue and will also be issuing a written warning to ensure that such incidents do not recur. Thank you for your patience and cooperation. Rgds, Desmond --- [15 Apr 2026] Dear Mr Seah, We have given a letter to 18-39 yesterday (14 Apr 2026) to ensure that such incidents do not recur. 1292-46272026-03-03Blk 51
High-rise littering of debris onto aircon ledge.

Title: High-Rise Littering on Aircon Ledge

Description: I am writing to formally report an issue with high-rise littering that is affecting the air conditioning ledge outside my unit. As seen in the attached photo, items (toilet paper roll/paper debris) have been dropped from upper floors and are now lodged under or around the external condensing units. I have since managed to remove the item myself for safety reasons, but I want to bring this to your attention as it may pose some risks. Fire Hazard: Discarded smoking materials from upper floors could ignite this dry debris, posing a direct threat to the building. Equipment Damage: Litter can obstruct the fans and heat dissipation of the compressors, leading to overheating or mechanical failure.

MA Reply: [04 Mar 2026] Hi KC20, thanks for the feedback. We will monitor the affected stack / area and apart from that, we will issue the notification to the upper level of your units as a reminder to them to refrain from throwing items from windows, balconies, or onto air-conditioner ledges. We appreciate your vigilance and cooperation in helping us maintain a safe and pleasant living environment. Best regards, Shafiee

Thank you for your understanding and cooperation in helping to maintain a peaceful living environment for all residents.

Title: Request for Immediate Action on Safety Hazard

Description: I am writing to raise a serious safety concern about Blk 59. Almost every day, the resident living above my unit has been throwing blocks of ice from their balcony onto the area below. This poses a significant risk of injury to residents and damage to property. I kindly request that the management office take immediate corrective action to address this issue and prevent it from recurring. Please let me know how this matter will be handled. Thank you for your prompt attention.

MA Reply: [01 Oct 2025] Hi Jessica, thank you for your feedback regarding the incident of ice cubes being thrown into your balcony. We understand your concern and the inconvenience caused. Please be assured that Management takes such matters seriously as they pose both safety and nuisance issues to the residents. We will approach the resident above your unit and advised them on the high-rise littering. At the same time, we will paste a circular/notice above your level as a reminder to all residents. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation as we work to maintain a safe and pleasant living environment for all residents.

MA Reply: [22 Aug 2025] Thank you for bringing this issue to our attention. 1292-35612025-07-30Blk 61
Residents throwing trash over balcony after sweeping.

Title: Throwing trash over the balcony

Description: High floor units throwing trash over the balcony after sweeping their floor. Not sure if it’s the owners/tenants/helpers that are doing it, but on a regular basis, I’ve been finding lumps of dirt/hair chunks on my balcony every now & then. This has been happening for some time and I’m sick of clearing other peoples’ trash from my balcony. Is there anyway of finding the culprit or putting out another notice for all neighbours to refrain from such inconsiderate actions?

MA Reply: [30 Jul 2025] Thank you for your feedback. We understand how distressing it is to find debris such as hair and dirt on your balcony, and we take such incidents seriously. In response to your report, we have issued a written reminder to the unit suspected of causing the issue, reminding them not to dispose of any trash, dirt, or waste over the balcony. Additionally, a general advisory notice will be circulated to all residents to reinforce proper hygiene and neighborly conduct, especially when cleaning balconies or common areas. We appreciate your cooperation and understanding as we work to ensure a clean and respectful living environment for all. -w

Closed
1292-34822025-07-13Blk 47
Metal clip dropped from high floor, balcony safety concern.

Title: Falling Object Incident and Balcony Safety Violati

Description: am writing to raise a serious safety concern regarding a recent case of falling objects—a metal clip dropped from a high floor—which could have caused severe injury. In addition, I have observed that many units have improperly placed items extending beyond their balcony railings, which poses significant risks to residents below. I urge the management to investigate the incident, inspect balconies for unsafe storage, and enforce stricter guidelines to prevent future accidents. This issue directly impacts the safety of all residents and must be addressed with urgency.

MA Reply: [14 Jul 2025] Thank you for your prompt report. We have received the photo and details of the incident. Our team is currently investigating the matter, including an inspection of Block 51 and nearby units to identify the source of the falling metal clip. It is possible that strong wind may have dislodged the item from an upper unit, and we will take this into account during our checks. As a precaution, we will also issue a safety reminder to all residents on securing loose items, especially during windy weather. We appreciate your vigilance and concern for the safety of the community. -w --- [14 Jul 2025] Thank you for raising this serious safety issue. We acknowledge the incident involving a metal clip that fell from a high floor. This could have caused grave injury. Our security team has been instructed to monitor balconies during patrols. Should they observe items hanging over or extending past railings, they will immediately inform residents to remove them. We’ve also posted a notice on Hi‑Life reminding everyone of safe balcony use and overhanging or unsecured storage. Falling objects and improperly stored items on balconies pose real risks. According to building safety guidelines, loose items—even something as small as pebbles—can cause serious harm when dropped from height It’s critical to ensure balconies remain clear of loose or heavy items that could fall or be blown off.

1292-33582025-06-14Blk 51
Recurring litter thrown from high floor unit.

Title: Complaint Regarding Littering from High Floor

Description: I am writing to formally raise a concern about a recurring issue in our building: litter being thrown from one of the higher floors. Floor from level 21 to 24-33.

MA Reply: [14 Jun 2025] Thank you for your feedback and for highlighting the recurring issue of high-rise littering from levels 21 to 24–33. We understand your concern. Unfortunately, there is no CCTV coverage of the specific area where the littering is taking place, which limits our ability to directly identify the culprit. Nevertheless, we will issue a circular to the relevant units to remind residents that high-rise littering is a serious offence under NEA regulations and poses danger to others. We strongly encourage residents who may witness such incidents to report them immediately, including the date, time, and if possible, photos or videos, to assist with further action. Thank you for your understanding and continued cooperation in maintaining a safe and pleasant environment for all. -W

Closed
1292-32582025-05-20Blk 45
Broken flower pot found at Blk 61, fallen from high floor.

Title: high floor fallen object

Description: this broken flower pot was spotted at blk 61 when my husband walk pass around 4pm today, 20th May 2025. please investigate, and educate the residents to take noye and prevent anyone being harm from such high floor fallen object.

MA Reply: [20 May 2025] Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We are concerned to learn about the broken flower pot spotted at Block 61 on 20th May 2025 around 4:00 PM. High-rise littering is a serious offence and a safety hazard, and we share your concerns about the risk it poses to residents and visitors. Our team has conducted a site inspection and cleared the debris to ensure the area is safe. We will continue to monitor the area closely for any recurring incidents. A notice will also be issued to all residents to remind them not to place unsecured items near windows or balconies, and to refrain from any form of high-rise littering. In the meantime, we will investigate the source of the fallen item. Should we be able to identify the responsible unit, an advisory notice will be issued to the resident accordingly. Thank you once again for your vigilance and cooperation.

1292-38772025-10-06Blk 57
Strong cooking smell from neighboring unit after 9pm nightly.

Title: Cooking Smell from Unit [unit] After 9:00pm

Description: Dear MCST Team, I hope this message finds you well. I would like to bring to your attention that we have been experiencing a strong and unpleasant smell of cooking coming from unit [unit], particularly after 9:00 PM. Unfortunately, the smell travels upwards and enters our unit, even with our kitchen windows closed. It seems to be coming in through the balcony area instead. We would appreciate it if the MCST could kindly speak with the residents of unit [unit] and advise them to keep their kitchen windows and balcony doors closed while cooking, especially during the late evening hours. This would help prevent the smell from affecting neighbouring units and disrupting our rest. Thank you for your attention to this matter and for your continued efforts in maintaining a pleasant living environment for all residents. Best regards, Amy Chiong [unit]

MA Reply: [07 Oct 2025] Thank you for reaching out and bringing this matter to our attention. We understand how unpleasant and disruptive strong cooking odors can be, especially during the evening hours when residents are trying to rest. We sincerely apologies for the inconvenience you’ve experienced and appreciate your patience and understanding. We will be reaching out to the residents of unit [unit] to kindly advise them to keep their kitchen windows and balcony doors closed while cooking, particularly during the late evening hours, to help minimize the spread of odors to neighboring units.

Closed
1292-34072025-06-27Blk 45
Common toilet shower washing at inconvenient evening timing.

Title: Toilet and shower Washing and Flooding

Description: I’d like to report 2 issues for the toilet and shower room. First- Washing time - it has arranged around 7-8pm, it is very not appropriate time. Surprised that why this timing as peoples usual swimming time, we also always to queue up for bathing after swimming. The cleaner washing the floor with detergent make the floor more slippery. In addition, the water flow to the shower room. All the dirty water and rubbish flow in when we are bathing. Cleaner didn’t hold on washing even seeing we are inside bathing, this is very uncomfortable. Hope management could train the cleaner seriously in this case. This is not first time, but multiple time that I have no choice to submit report. I wish the management could study the usual toilet cleaning like other condo do, they will do early first thing in the morning, there is lesser peoples around. Second - Shower rooms are flooding due to drainage stuck, I believe they didn’t clear the hairs or rubbish, when shower the water flooding even till toilet area. In short, the whole toilet are wet everywhere. Looking forward to hear the solution soonest. Thank you

MA Reply: [27 Jun 2025] Hi Foong, thank you for your feedback. We understand your concern regarding the cleaning routine for the toilet. Please be assured that a cleaning supervisor is in place to oversee and manage all the blocks and toilets, ensuring that tasks are completed diligently. We will highlight this matter to the Supervisor on these matters so that it can be addresses at the soonest. Thanks again. --- [16 Jul 2025] Dear Foong, thank you for bringing the matter of the toilet cleanliness to our attention and we apologized for the inconvenienced caused. Please be assured that we take hygiene and cleanliness very seriously, and we have discussed with the Cleaning Supervisor that the cleaning after 8pm will be focused on routine cleaning with mopping only for the flooring instead of wet washing. This is to prevent from slip and floor As usual in the morning time, the cleaner will carry out the routine job and wet cleaning as well so as to maintain the cleanliness of the toilet and we have instructed the Cleaning Supervisor to monitor especially during the nighttime cleaning. Thanks again.

Closed

Title: Cleanliness Issue: Lift Mirror at Block 45

The mirror inside the lift has been foggy and full of visible spots for the past month and does not appear to have been cleaned during this period. I regularly see cleaning staff stationed around the B1 lobby, which makes this oversight rather disappointing. The lift is a high-traffic area and reflects the overall standard of cleanliness within the condo. May I check if this is the level of cleanliness that management intends to maintain? I believe the team is capable of doing better, and I hope this issue can be addressed promptly. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [29 Jan 2026] Hi Sophia Lim, thanks for bringing to our attention the condition of the lift metal mirror. Nevertheless, we take your concern seriously. We will arrange the cleaning company to further assess the condition of the mirror and explore alternative cleaning methods or remedial solutions where feasible to improve its appearance. We appreciate your patience and understanding and thank you for helping us maintain the common property.

1292-41162025-12-03Blk 45
Glass panels foggy and masking tape residue from decorations.

Title: Request for Improved Housekeeping Standards

Description: Hi, I would like to highlight a few ongoing cleanliness issues around the condo. The glass panels are often foggy and dirty, and there is still a significant amount of masking tape left behind after the National Day decorations. I hope the management can conduct proper due diligence to ensure the upkeep of our shared spaces. Additionally, I noticed a concerning incident at 45 B1: the female Malay cleaner took out a cloth, placed it briefly on the glass door to take a photo, and immediately kept it without actually cleaning. I have also seen her on multiple occasions sitting at the B2 lobby using her phone during working hours. Kindly look into this matter to maintain the cleanliness standards of our condo. Thank you.

MA Reply: [04 Dec 2025] Hi Ms. Sophia Lim, thank you for your feedback regarding the dirty glass panel with left over masking tape. We have informed our maintenance team to attend to the area and remove the remaining masking tape as well as clean and restores the glass panel to proper condition. Apart from that, please be assured that we will review the matter with the cleaning supervisor and remind the staff to maintain the expected standards of cleanliness throughout the estate. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention and thank you for your understanding.

1292-31842025-05-07Blk 53
Renovation dust covering Blk 53 level 10 corridor daily.

Title: Dusty corridor at Blk53 level 10

Description: Due to the construction at Blk 53, unit [unit], the 10th-floor corridor is covered daily with construction dust tracked in by workers' shoes. Consequently, residents and their pets on the 10th floor get dust on their shoes and paws every time they walk down the corridor, and this dust is then carried into their homes, creating an unsanitary environment. We request corridor cleaning at least twice a day during the construction period (around 1 PM and before 5 PM). Residents have been greatly inconvenienced by the noise and dust from this month-long construction. Please remember that residents have the right to live in a hygienic environment.

MA Reply: [07 May 2025] Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We acknowledge the inconvenience caused by the ongoing renovation works at Unit [unit], especially regarding the dust accumulation in the 10th-floor corridor. We understand the impact this has on residents' daily comfort and hygiene, and we sincerely apologize for the disruption. In response to your request, we have informed the contractor responsible for the unit to ensure proper containment of dust and to take additional care in cleanliness during the course of their work. We appreciate your patience and understanding during this period. Should you notice any lapse in the increased cleaning schedule or have further concerns, please do not hesitate to contact us directly.

Closed
1292-43242026-01-14Blk 61
Blk 61 common areas not cleaned, same dust for days.

Title: Dirty common areas at Blk 61

Description: This is not my first time providing feedback on the cleanliness and hygiene of the common areas at Blk 61 and it doesn’t seem like anything has changed much. Level 3 of Blk 61 always does not seemed to be cleaned. The same dust ball can be at the same place for days and this is really frustrating as the cleaning staff does not seem to be doing their job diligently/properly. It is really embarrassing when I’ve friends coming over and them being shocked at the cleanliness level (or rather the lack of it) of this condo, the common areas and the lifts to be exact. To be fair, i’ve neighbours living at other blocks and not all seem to be as dirty. Correct me if I am wrong but shouldn’t the cleaning schedule be on a daily basis as the current situation certainly does not look like it. Is there any way to make sure level 3 (not sure about other levels) is cleaned everyday? To add on, someone accidentally spilled something on one of the lift floors and it actually took more than 1 day for the mess to be cleared. This makes me wonder what the cleaning staff are at to not notice it for more than a day?

MA Reply: [14 Jan 2026] Dear Emma, thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and frustration caused by the unsatisfactory cleanliness of the common areas over the past few days. We fully understand your disappointment, especially the embarrassment you felt when hosting your friends. Please be assured that this is not the standard of service we strive to maintain for our residents. The feedback has been highlighted to our cleaning contractor for immediate rectification. We have instructed them to carry out the cleaning at the affected areas. We truly appreciate your feedback as it helps us improve our services. Thank you for your patience and understanding and we apologize once again for the inconvenienced caused.

The bracket of the cover is also disloged from the wall and it will be a matter of time before the bracket drops off. There is also visible spillage on the left side of the bin. Closed
1292-43572026-01-23Blk 49
Bad stench returned at bin and chute area.

Title: bin and chute area 2

Description: earlier feedback submitted without adding this point: the stench was gone do a few days following feedback but this morning it is very bad again

MA Reply: [23 Jan 2026] Dear Vera, We refer to your feedback regarding the subject matter with thanks. For your information, the bin centre is part of a new pneumatic waste collection (suction) system. During operation of the system, some odour may be released temporarily, particularly when waste is being transported through the suction process. That said, we understand that strong or persistent odour is a concern. We have informed our maintenance team to check the system and carry out the necessary cleaning and rectification works to minimise the smell. We appreciate your understanding and bringing this matter to management attention.

Closed

MA Reply: [23 Jan 2026] Dear Vera, We refer to your feedback regarding the subject matter with thanks. For your information, the bin centre is part of a new pneumatic waste collection (suction) system. During operation of the system, some odour may be released temporarily, particularly when waste is being transported through the suction process. That said, we understand that strong or persistent odour is a concern. We have informed our maintenance team to check the system and carry out the necessary cleaning and rectification works to minimise the smell. We appreciate your understanding and bringing this matter to management attention.

1292-42212025-12-28Blk 49
Neighbor throwing food waste into recycling chute.

Title: Inconsiderate or stupid Chinese neighbour

Description: Hi management, we noticed, 2 days in a row that a neighbour on our floor is throwing food waste into the recycling chute as when we open the recycling chute to throw our recycling waste, we find food waste in the chute (yesterday it was noodles, today prawn shells and fish bones. There is a name on a piece of paper in today’s rubbish so we think it is a Chinese person with the name: Sun XiuQin. Could you please help to let this person know they need to check which chute they are throwing into?

MA Reply: [29 Dec 2025] Hi Ping Huang, thanks you for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand your concern regarding the improper disposal of food waste at the recycling chute. This act of irresponsibility by the neighbor can cause hygiene issues, unpleasant odor and contamination of recyclables. We have placed reminder notices on the main wooden door of the recycle chute bin and inform the residents accordingly to reinforce proper waste disposal practices. Our team will continue to monitor the situation and thank you for your feedback and cooperation in helping us maintain a clean and hygienic living environment for everyone.

Title: Block rubbish chute with trash from 03-05

Description: Tonight the rubbish chute on our level was blocked with rubbish stuffed insude with the address label for unit 03-05. I managed to clear the wedged trash and get it down the chute. Residents should check that their rubbish has gone down the chute and not assume that it has. This inconsiderate behaviour impacts the residents who have made Normanton Park their home, and devalues the condo as you expect ‘slum behaviour’ to be in a slum and not accepted at an affluent development such as Normanton Park. Residents caught being inconsiderate should be made aware of their actions and educated to be considerate towards other residents.

MA Reply: [21 Nov 2025] Dear Simon, Thank you for your email and for bringing this matter to our attention. We appreciate your initiative in clearing the blockage and informing us. For your information, we have contacted the resident and have reminded them to ensure that all waste is properly disposed of down the chute. We also advised them that larger or bulky items should be brought to B1 and disposed of at the designated refuse area.

1292-32292025-05-14Blk 55
Residents pouring waste into chutes without bags.

Title: Dirty Ribbish Chutes

Description: Dear team, residents have not been using bags and pouring their waste in both chutes including the recycling chute. It is dirty and smelly now. please look into it.

MA Reply: [14 May 2025] Thank you for the feedback. We are aware of the issue and share your concerns regarding the unhygienic disposal of waste. We will be placing reminder notices near the refuse and recycling chutes to inform residents about the proper disposal methods, including the requirement to bag all waste before disposal. The cleaner has clear the affected area We appreciate your vigilance and cooperation in maintaining a clean and pleasant living environment for all.

Closed
1292-32272025-05-13Blk 49
Food waste thrown into chute without bags at Blk 49.

Title: Irresponsible disposal of waste

Description: Blk 49, level 7, there are occurrences of iresponsible disposal of waste by residents here. Food waste is throw directly into the chute without disposal bags. This would easily attract pests and possibly infestations. Please help to arrange cleaning of the chute. Might warrant for an official reminder to residents as well.

MA Reply: [14 May 2025] Thank you for your feedback and for bringing this matter to our attention. We are aware of the irresponsible waste disposal occurring at Block 49, Level 7, particularly food waste being thrown directly into the chute without proper bagging. This indeed poses hygiene concerns and increases the risk of pest infestation. In response, we have taken the following actions: Preparing an official circular/reminder to be issued via HiLife and notice boards, urging residents to bag all waste properly before disposal and to observe responsible waste management practices. We appreciate your vigilance and cooperation in helping us maintain a clean and healthy environment for all residents.

Closed
1292-31882025-05-07Blk 59
Constant bad smell from lower floor rubbish chutes.

Title: Unresolved bad smell from rubbish chute

Description: There is a constant bad smell emitting from all the lower floors rubbish chutes every day. It is ridiculous that residents and visitors to our homes have to endure such bad smell. The smell is so bad that we have to keep our main doors closed all the time. I think there's a need to clean the internal shafts of the rubbish chutes on the lower floors and the main chute point at the basement floor on a daily basis now since this bad smell problem hasn’t been resolved since more than 6 months ago.

MA Reply: [08 May 2025] Thank you for your feedback and for highlighting the persistent issue with the odor from the rubbish chutes on the lower floors. We sincerely apologies for the inconvenience caused and understand how unpleasant and frustrating this has been for you and your neighbors. Please be assured that we take this matter seriously. Our cleaning contractor has been instructed to increase the frequency of chute flushing and deodorizing, especially at the lower levels and the main chute chamber at the basement. ` We will also monitor the situation closely and assess the effectiveness of these measures over the coming weeks. Your continued feedback is valuable to us in ensuring a clean and comfortable living environment for all residents. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

Kone will review again on the lift condition after 4.30pm and will update again.

Description: The last two issues reported last year were not adddressed. This was regarding the lift announcements no longer working in Block 51 and the spring on the rubbish chute on Block 51, level 03 being broken. The rubbish chute is often open as a result and the floor smells of rubbish.

MA Reply: [02 Feb 2026] Dear Andrew, We refer to your feedback regarding both subject matter with thanks. For your information, the bin chute issue has been attended to by our technical team. The bin chute spring has been replaced, and the system is now functioning normally. With regard to the lift issue, we have assigned the matter to our lift contractor for inspection and for any necessary rectification works. We appreciate for your patience and understanding.

Meanwhile we will remind the relevant residents not to park any bicycles, tricycles or personal mobility items in front of the lift lobby. 1292-39462025-10-24Blk 51
Blk 51 lift stays at top floor, not responding to B1 calls.

Title: lifts stay at top floor !

Description: I would like to report that the lift at blk 51 often stays at the top floor and does not come down when we press the lift button at Basement 1 (B1). This has been happening frequently and causes inconvenience, especially for residents who need to use the lift from B1. Please arrange for the maintenance team to check and fix the issue as soon as possible. pls advise asap. thank u

MA Reply: [25 Oct 2025] Thank you for your feedback regarding the lift at Block 51. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention. The matter has been reported to our lift maintenance contractor, KONE, for their immediate inspection and rectification. The team will look into the issue to ensure the lift functions according to the intended settings, with one lift stationed at B1, Level 10, and Level 20 respectively. We apologies for any inconvenience caused and thank you for your kind understanding and patience while the issue is being resolved.

Description: No description

Closed
1292-37912025-09-19Blk 59
Request to improve elevator operations and dispatch logic.

Title: Request for Improvement on Elevator Operations

Description: I would like to formally raise my concerns regarding the operation of the elevators in our condominium. • Although there are 3 elevators, only 2 are frequently in operation, which results in insufficient capacity. • When calling from Level 1, the elevators often first go to B1 due to other residents pressing the button, and by the time they arrive at Level 1, they are already full. • During peak hours, it is common to wait for more than 5 minutes before being able to board, which causes significant inconvenience. For the comfort of all residents, I kindly request the following improvements: 1. Ensure that all 3 elevators are in operation at all times. 2. Review the elevator usage during peak hours and implement necessary measures. Your prompt attention to this matter would be highly appreciated.

MA Reply: [22 Sep 2025] Dear Ernko, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation of the lift operation during the peak hours. We would like to highlight that there are instances where the fire lift is used for deliveries/moving-in of bulky items. This will result in the fire lift being "out of service" for that point in time. However, such moving-in are only permitted outside of the peak hours after 09:30am. We note your request and will review the lift usage and will implement new measures if deemed necessary. Thank you for your kind attention. As no further response is required regarding this matter, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed

Closed
1292-34542025-07-08Blk 61
Lift protection cover should not be permanent fixture.

Title: fireman lift

Description: understand the lift protection covet is to protect the lift when moving. it is not a permanent fixture nor a cargo lift. it should only be put up whrn someone apply to move. if it always there people will not bothet to apply permit when moving. and most importantly it makes the lift small and hot and unsightly.

MA Reply: [08 Jul 2025] Dear Ms Jane Wong, thanks for the feedback. We understand your concern and base on our records, it shows that there are a lot of deliveries practically every day so the padding inside the fireman lift is still needed. For all the deliveries, moving in or moving out, the contractors / owner need to apply the permit otherwise we will stop the contractors immediately if there isn't any permit. Let us discuss with the Manager and we shall do the best we can. In the meantime. we will get KONE Team to check on the ventilation fan speed and the padding condition as well. Thanks again.

1292-46212026-03-02Blk 53
Blk 53 lift PL9 door stuck between floors, safety concern.

Title: Blk 53 lift pl9 door is not working

Description: hi, I have safety concerns over the lift pl9. I took it yesterday. it got stuck on level 15-16, was unstable a while and the door was like trying to open itself when it was between both floors. in the end it open doors on level 16 so I quickly got out. Can someone look into it and fix it ?

MA Reply: [02 Mar 2026] Dear Mindy, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks. For your information, we have assigned this serious matter to the lift vendor. Thanks for bringing this matter to management attention. --- [04 Mar 2026] Dear Mindy, We apoligise for the late update. According to the lift vendor findings, the lift door was not functioning normally on that day due to a faulty rope pulley at both lift car door. The vendor has since replaced the rope pulleys and carried out the necessary adjustments to the door operation. The lift doors were tested and monitored at all floors, and the door operations are now functioning normally. It carried out on 02 March 2026, at 8pm. Please kindly find the attachment below for your reference. Thank you for your patience and understanding. Best Regards, Alfred

Closed

Lift down button between PL13-PL14 not working again.

Title: Faulty Down Button at PL13–PL14 Lift Lobby

Description: Dear MCST, I am writing with reference to Case No. [phone] dated 10 January 2026 regarding the lift issue at Block 57, Level 19 lift lobby. The “Down” button between PL13 and PL14 is not working again. This has caused inconvenience, especially during peak hours when residents are in a hurry to go down. We would appreciate your prompt action to rectify and repair the button as soon as possible. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [27 Feb 2026] Dear resident, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks. For your information, we have assigned this matter to lift contactor for the necessary checking and repairing work on it. Thanks for bringing this serious matter to management attention. Best Regards, Alfred --- [05 Mar 2026] Dear resident, We apologies for the late update. We wish to highlight that the lift vendor has attended to the matter and the lift vendor has tightened the wire connection from the buzzer to the button and replaced the button PCB board. The up and down button functions have been tested and are now operating normally. Please kindly find the attachment below for your reference. We appreciate your patience and understanding. Best regards, Alfred

1292-45132026-02-11Blk 47
Blk 47 middle lift not responding to call buttons.

Title: lift not working efficiently

Description: PL3 and PL2 are travelling up but middle lift is not moving. seems like middle lift is not responding to the call button. neighbor from level 9 came down to level 8, thinking there was an issue with level 9 call button, but it was not. the middle lift is stuck at level 15 while the other 2 lifts are travelling up

MA Reply: [11 Feb 2026] Hi Yoke Chye Koh, thanks for the feedback. We have referred this matter to KONE team, and they have discovered that the middle lift photocell was interrupted for more than a minute due to the cleaner blocking the door lift while cleaning the lift flooring. We have advised the cleaner not to block the lift door in future but instead they can still clean lift floor while the lift is moving. Thank again.

Closed
1292-33532025-06-12Blk 45
Lift ventilation fan not working at Blk 45.

Title: Blk 45 Lift Ventilation Fan NOT WORKING

Description: Can you get lift technical team to check and repair the lift ventilation fan asap? it is extremely suffer especially when full of peoples in the lift. Second, as I have reported to Condo manager last month. Pls adjust back one of the lift waiting at high zone as before. Lift is critical to us as we are using it daily multiple times especially for us in high zone. Due to slow pace lift, the lift go up to high zone always taking longer time. I have feedback to Management several times but the issues are still pending. Welcome to call me if further clarification needed. Thanks

MA Reply: [17 Jun 2025] We have informed the lift technician regarding the recent issues. Below are the rectification actions taken: Ventilation Fan Coil: A new fan coil has been installed in PL1 lift. We will continue monitoring the ventilation performance. The remaining two lifts will also have their fan coils replaced in due course. Lift Speed: Regarding the reported issue of slow lifts, the technician confirmed that the lifts are operating at their optimized speed as per the original specifications. Lift Parking Adjustment: To improve efficiency, the parking levels for the lifts have been adjusted as follows: PL1: Level 10 PL2: Level 20 FL1: Basement 1 (B1) We will continue to monitor the situation and update accordingly -w

Closed

Please do let us know if the issue persists or if you notice anything unusual, and we will investigate further. Thank you for your understanding. -W

1292-50362026-04-22Blk 61

Title: Lift vibration and stamp

Description: BLK 61When the lift reached the 23rd floor, it suddenly vibrated violently and stopped completely for about 10 seconds. For safety reasons, we exited the lift early at the 22nd floor. As a member of the Management Council, I am concerned about the safety of our residents. Please arrange for a KONE technician to conduct a comprehensive safety check on this lift immediately to identify the cause of the vibration and the temporary stall.

MA Reply: [23 Apr 2026] Dear Ms Hanni, Thank you for your feedback and for bringing this matter to our attention. We have informed our lift contractor, KONE, and their technician has since attended to the lift. The system has been checked and tested, and the lift is currently operating normally. We will continue to monitor the situation closely, and if you experience any further irregularities, please do not hesitate to let us know. The safety of all residents remains our top priority. Thank you for your cooperation and understanding. Regards, Rui Ling

MA Reply: [17 Mar 2026] Hi Mr. Steve Swa, thanks for the e-mail. We have entered the unit 19-01 & 21-01 with KONE team and after checking, they have confirmed that the noise is coming from the lift motor bearing at the centre lift PL2. Title: cabinet door sag down and unable to close

MA Reply: [11 Oct 2025] Dear Lay, We refer to your feedback above with thanks. Please be informed that the Defect Liability Period (DLP) for your unit has ended. As such, the contractor is no longer responsible for rectifying issues reported after this period. Once the DLP has expired: • The developer is no longer liable for non-structural defects, unless they fall under long-term structural warranties (e.g., 10 years for major structural issues under the Building Control Act). • The homeowner becomes responsible for identifying and addressing any issues that arise. • Homeowners or tenants are required to engage their own contractors and bear the cost of any repairs or maintenance. We encourage you to engage a contractor of your choice to carry out the necessary repairs. Thanks again.

Title: Assistance Required for Air Conditioner Repair

Description: Dear Management Team, I hope this message finds you well. I would like to report an issue with my bedroom air conditioner — the air being blown out is no longer cool. I would appreciate your assistance in checking whether it is still under warranty. I understand that the air conditioner should have a warranty period of about 2–3 years. I have engaged a service technician to inspect the unit, and he informed me that the green circuit board inside the air conditioner is faulty. Could you please advise who I should contact to resolve this issue, or help arrange for the necessary repairs? I emailed the management office but there is no reply. Hence, can you help assist in this issue? Thank you for your kind assistance. Warm regards, Anne

MA Reply: [05 Nov 2025] Dear Anne, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks. Please be informed that the air conditioning system is warranty against manufacturing defects for a period of 12 months commencing from the Temporary Occupancy Permit (TOP) Date and additional 48 months for the compressor only (excluding transport and labour charges). Kindly refer to the attachment for your reference, you may contact the contractor at [phone].

Closed
1292-38472025-10-02Blk 49
Basin has rough patches getting worse, reported at move-in.

Title: rough patches

Description: my basin has 2 rough patches. i highlighted this to the developer when we moved in but now it has become worst. Is there a way to get rid of it?

MA Reply: [02 Oct 2025] Please be informed that the Defect Liability Period (DLP) for your unit has ended. As such, the contractor is no longer responsible for rectifying issues reported after this period. Once the DLP has expired: • The developer is no longer liable for non-structural defects, unless they fall under long-term structural warranties (e.g., 10 years for major structural issues under the Building Control Act). • The homeowner becomes responsible for identifying and addressing any issues that arise. • Homeowners or tenants are required to engage their own contractors and bear the cost of any repairs or maintenance.

As such, the contractor is no longer responsible for rectifying issues reported after this period. Once the DLP has expired: The developer is no longer liable for non-structural defects, unless they fall under long-term structural warranties (e.g., 10 years for major structural issues under the Building Control Act). The homeowner becomes responsible for identifying and addressing any issues that arise. Homeowners or tenants are required to engage their own contractors and bear the cost of any repairs or maintenance. Thanks again for bringing up this matter to us.

Closed
1292-47442026-03-19Blk 47
Guard house and estate signage in poor condition.

Title: Guard House and DIY signs

Description: To the Management Office, I am writing again to express my concern regarding the current state of the main guard house and the general upkeep of estate signage. As the primary entrance to our development, the guard house serves as the "face" of the property. Currently, it is cluttered with non-permanent fixtures, including a folding table with a mismatched cloth and loose items (as seen in the attached photo). These temporary setups, combined with the haphazard placement of signs, give the impression that the property is poorly managed and lack the premium feel residents expect. Furthermore, many of the in-house signs around the development appear dated or "DIY" in nature. This inconsistent aesthetic significantly lowers the perceived value and prestige of our estate. I would appreciate it if the Management could look into the following: • Replacing temporary furniture: If a workspace is required outside the guard house, please install a permanent, high-quality fixture that matches the building’s architecture. • Signage Audit: Review and replace all aging or unprofessional signage with a standardized, high-quality design consistent with a well-managed condominium. • Clutter Management: Ensure the entrance area is kept clear of loose debris and non-essential items. We take pride in our home and hope the Management will take the necessary steps to restore the professional standard of the development. I look forward to seeing improvements in these areas soon.

MA Reply: [20 Mar 2026] Dear Ms Carmen, Thank you for the feedback. We will work on the outlook of the furniture outside the guardhouse and ensure professionalism. As for the signages, we will also be engaging the signage company to replace those current existing temporary signages. We will also ensure that the main entrance and other common areas within the estate are properly managed. Once again, we sincerely thank you for your feedback. Best Regards, Desmond

Closed
1292-47142026-03-15Blk 47
B1 lobby ceiling plaster sagging and water-damaged.

Title: Maintenance Work - Blk 47 B1 Lobby

Description: Dear MA team, what is this maintenance works for and last for how many days / weeks ? The ceiling plaster board looks heavy, sunken and like going to drop. Nearby ceiling board also got water marks . As it is above notice board, poses a risk / hazard then suggest to cordon off the area with cones and maintenance signage , in case really drop when resident stand near the noticeboard area maybe injured. Thanks.

MA Reply: [16 Mar 2026] Thank you for your feedback regarding the maintenance works at Blk 47 B1. The air-conditioning contractor will be checking and repairing the ducting where the possible seepage is coming from. Once the issue has been rectified, our technical team will proceed to patch up and repaint the affected area. The ceiling plasterboard is currently not wet and should not drop of. Best Regards - Joanne

In Progress
1292-45662026-02-20Blk 47
Main entrance appearance and signage in poor condition.

Title: Maintenance and Aesthetic of Main Entrance

Description: Dear Management Office / Building Committee, I am writing to share my concerns regarding the poor visual impression and maintenance standards at the main entrance of our estate. While the primary architecture of the entrance features a premium, modern design, the current state of the security guard house and barrier setup detracts significantly from the property’s prestige and overall upkeep. Specifically, I would like to highlight the following points: 1. Unkempt Security Guard House The guard house currently presents a cluttered and disorganized appearance. • Improvisation: The use of a makeshift table covered with a cloth, placed outside the guard house, looks unprofessional and temporary. • Visual Clutter: Items such as loose water bottles, plastic bins, and miscellaneous equipment scattered around the base of the structure create an impression of neglect. 2. Barrier Extension The barrier arm setup is particularly distracting and appears to be in a state of disrepair. • Haphazard Repairs: The red-and-white tape used to secure the extension to the main barrier arm looks like a low-budget, temporary fix. It is highly visible to every resident and guest entering the premises. 3. Impact on Property Value The entrance is the first point of contact for visitors and prospective buyers. Currently, the "ad-hoc" nature of the security post gives the impression of a lack of management oversight. This does not reflect the premium positioning of the estate. Request for Action: I would appreciate it if the management could look into: • Standardizing the furniture and storage within the guard house to remove external clutter. • Replacing the taped barrier extension with a permanent, professionally integrated arm that matches the estate's aesthetic. Thank you for your attention to maintaining the standards of our home. I look forward to seeing improvements soon.

MA Reply: [24 Feb 2026] Thank you for your feedback regarding your observation at our main entrance. We will coordinate with our technical team to enhance the area to improve both its aesthetics and practical functionality. Our goal is to ensure the entrance reflects a professional image while remaining efficient for daily operations. We appreciate your input and will keep you updated on the progress. Best regards - Desmond

Closed
1292-42762026-01-06Blk 57
No-smoking signage keeps blowing off, smoking persists at staircase.

Title: No-Smoking Signage

Description: Hi! MCST, the no-smoking signage keep dropping down due to strong wind keep blowing the signage, please assist to put it back at Blk 57, Level 19 staircase door use more stronger sticker. FYI, still got people smoking at the staircase, yet don’t know which level. Thank you.

MA Reply: [06 Jan 2026] Thank you for your feedback. The no-smoking signage keeps falling due to strong winds. We will arrange for the cleaner to pick it up. In the meantime, we have put up a notice to inform residents not to smoke at the staircase.

Closed
1292-34132025-06-29Blk 53
Carpark signage too small, Grab drivers still confused.

Title: Print Signages Bigger using the Proper Guidelines

Description: Understand that you have printed addl signages but all Grab drivers still complained about it. Why? it's not following the proper guidelines of a traffic direction sign. Here are some pointers: (1) bigger and NON-reflective same as the "B1" and "10 kmh" signages as attached here. (2) (2) do not just print A4 or A3 sized papers like just a temporary sign. these should be permanent and long lasting. The "10kmh" sign is a good example of a proper one. (3) avoid laminating it or covering it with plastic cover as it is reflective and drivers cannot see it when it hits the light

MA Reply: [30 Jun 2025] Dear Chiii, thank you for your feedback. We note your point with regards to the temporary signages that has been put up in the carpark. We will take your point into consideration with regards to better signage in the carpark and will assess the condition in the carpark before beginning the provision and installation of new signs. Kindly be informed that such provisions will require approval from the management council and can only move forward when such approval is given. We hope you understand where we are coming from. Rest assured, your feedback has been recorded and be floated up to the council/developer at the next meeting. As no response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-32912025-05-27Blk 53
Request for proper signage for elevator permits in lobby.

Title: Proper signage

Description: Please make proper signage case inside elevator ( like permit to operate elevator) or elevator lobby at B1 and Level 1 instead of displaying the warning letter in each level with ugly format as attached photo. Warning contents itself are already disgusting. Ugly displaying makes even worse.

MA Reply: [28 May 2025] Thank you for your feedback regarding the warning letters currently displayed on each residential level. We understand and appreciate your concern about the formatting and visual impact of the notices. Please be assured that these notices are only displayed temporarily and are removed after a few days, as they are meant to address time-sensitive or important issues. Due to safety regulations and building guidelines, we are unable to place notices inside lift cabins. Furthermore, permanent or long-term signage is typically reserved for essential operational notices, such as lift permits or emergency procedures. We will review how such temporary notices are formatted and aim to improve their clarity and appearance in future instances to ensure they remain informative yet respectful of the estate's overall aesthetics. We appreciate your understanding and continued support in maintaining a pleasant living environment. William Condo executive

Closed

1292-31772025-05-06Blk 49

Title: Exit and Block Signage

We look forward to your response. --- [15 May 2025] Dear Wong, we note your request to increase the size of the signage. We will take it into consideration when we design more signages as and when necessary. With regards to the overhead signage, this was designed and erected during the construction phase and will not be able to change as easily due to the costs it might incur. Feedback we have received from other residents pointed out that the signage is actually visible when driving at the carpark speed limit of 10km/h. We hope that we have addressed your feedback amicably. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.
