1292-50432026-04-22Blk 49
Security or access issue reported at Block 49.

Title: Lvl 1 Doors at block 49 are kept open often

Description: The doors at block 49 level 1 are often kept open (both sides). Given we don’t have security to prevent anyone from accessing any floor, such actions are irresponsible and a threat to the security of residents in the block. Additionally the doors at lvl 1 of block 49 do not close completely. Since it is impossible to ensure that everyone does the right thing, it is recommended to change the design of the doors to those that cannot be held open (similar to those at the basement) to ensure security. This is also common place in other condominiums (such as stirling residences, commonwealth towers, queens arc, alex residences, echelon etc).

1292-46872026-03-11Blk 47
Resident flying drone near windows invading privacy.

Title: Drone usage outside block

Description: tenants of blk 47 [unit] are flying drone close to the windows. can you please let them know that this will invade privacy for others.

MA Reply: [12 Mar 2026] Hi Ms. Aishwarya, thanks for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand your concern regarding the use of drones flying close to your residential units, as this may potentially affect the privacy of residents. Please note that under the regulations of Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore, drone operators must follow strict guidelines when flying unmanned aircraft, including not flying recklessly or causing danger, nuisance, or invading the privacy of others. We will inform the security team to remain vigilant during their patrols and to approach any drone operator observed flying close to residential units within the condominium. Should you notice the drone activity again, we would appreciate it if you could inform the security team so that they can attend the case and appropriate action can be taken. Thank you for your feedback. Best regards, Shafiee

Description: I rated the reply but am not sure if the team can see it. The issue is not resolved. When the door swings open, it doesn't swing back fully thus leaving the door ajar (very small gap) and not locked which allows anyone to enter. Perhaps the staff can check again? Many thanks.

MA Reply: [10 Jan 2026] Dear Mr John, Thank you for your feedback. We have informed our technician team to investigate the issue. Upon inspection, they found that the emergency release button had been tampered with by unauthorized individuals. The issue has since been rectified, and the door is now in proper working condition. Regards, The Management

1292-41282025-12-07Blk 49
Inquiry about delivery access when no one is home.

Title: Delivery guy access when no one at home

Description: hi, how can delivery be done to unit when no one is in the unit? How do they get in lobby? Is there way to accept delivery when no one is at home? advise pls.

MA Reply: [08 Dec 2025] Regarding your question on how deliveries can reach your unit when no one is at home, we would like to share that we do not recommend allowing delivery personnel to enter the lobby without proper access. In most cases, they are only able to enter if another resident opens the door for them or if they tailgate behind someone entering the building. At the moment, there is no designated method to accept deliveries when no one is at home. For security reasons, access to the block should only be granted by residents or authorized individuals. We appreciate your understanding as we work to maintain a safe environment for all residents.

1292-37062025-08-29Blk 45
Unauthorized property flyers found in letter box again.

Title: Property Agent Flyers Drop in Letter Box

Description: I have to report this marketing flyers in our letter box AGAIN. Why flyer distributors can enter to our condo easily ? Security never notice when they dropping in letters box? Pls report to their agency or CEA to stop this distributions before our condo flooded with agent flyers. Thank you

MA Reply: [29 Aug 2025] Dear Foong, thank you for your feedback. We understand your frustration and agree that unauthorised flyer distribution within the estate is a matter of concern. Please be assured that the Management has since raised this matter with the security team and has instructed them to be extra vigilant in monitoring entry points. Any flyer distributors identified will be turned away and blacklisted from the estate. As such flyer distributors may enter the estate under the guise of visiting a unit, please do not hesitate to approach security at [phone] should you spot them doing such acts and they will be escorted out of the estate. Hope this addresses your feedback. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-36372025-08-18Blk 57
Workers appear to be living in B1 near Blk 57 lift lobby.

Title: workers living in B1

Description: Hi, it appears there are workers living in B1 near Blk 57 lift lobby. Is the management aware of this? Is this legal? Is this kind of housing considered safe and are proper sanitation facilities available for them? Could you let us know why these workers are there? And how long they're expected to be there? Thanks.

MA Reply: [18 Aug 2025] Dear Ra01234, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation and would like to clarify the purpose of the rest area. The area identified in the attached photo is actually a designated rest area for our contractors to use during their designated rest/lunch period. This is to provide them with privacy and minor comfort while taking their breaks. Rest assured that there are no workers living overnight in our estate as that would not be allowed. We hope that this addresses your feedback amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-36302025-08-16Blk 47
Security denied aircon contractor entry on weekend afternoon.

Title: air-conditioning contractors on weekends

Description: security guy Chong just denied entry to my air-con guys saying management denies entry to contractors on weekends, Saturday afternoon onwards. there is no such noise work in AC cleaning so how is the management causing a huge inconvenience to residents with this policy if you’re only allowing AC contractors to work within this small window on a Saturday morning?

MA Reply: [18 Aug 2025] Dear Hakeym, thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear about the issue you experienced. We will forward this matter to the security company for further action. Kindly note that the MCST has no restrictions to air-con contractors for servicing as long as it is within reasonable hours. However, prior approval has to be given before chemical/condenser washing as it may affect the floors below your unit. Thank you for bringing this matter up for our attention. Once again, we apologise for the inconvenience cause and will take the necessary follow-up action. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

1292-49902026-04-17Blk 47
Gate issue raising security concern at .

Title: Building Gate Left Open - Security Concern

Description: Hi, I’ve noticed that the building gate is still being left open despite reminder notices being pasted on the walls to remind residents to close the gate after them. However, it seems like a lot of residents are not heeding the notice. This poses a huge security risk to the residents here as any stranger can just enter the building and access any level. We pay a monthly maintenance fee for the premise to be secured. I would like to suggest that a hinge is installed at each building gate so that the gate closes automatically or implement life access control to each specific level. Our security is of utmost priority and I hope you will look into it

MA Reply: [17 Apr 2026] Dear Ms Abby, Thank you for your feedback regarding the building gate being left open and your concerns on security. We understand the importance of maintaining a secure environment for all residents. Please be informed that we have called for a quotation from our contractor to assess the installation and improvement works. We will arrange for the necessary rectification as soon as possible once the evaluation is completed. In the meantime, we will continue to remind residents to ensure that the gate is properly closed after use. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. Regards, Rui Ling

Closed
1292-48902026-04-04Blk 49
Request to restrict lift access to residents' own floors.

Title: Resident can access only their own floor

Description: Hi Desmond, another security issues which came to our attention. On multiples occasions, we have observed grab drivers, contractors and cleaning personnel entering through the basement door without using the intercom system. They often follow residents in when the door is opened. This raise concerns that unauthorized individuals can enter the premises and potentially access different levels after completing their deliveries or work. Similarly, tailgating at the bus stop/NTUC entrance allows anyone to enter the premises without proper authorization. Hence, we hope that MCST can review this matter seriously and implement stricter access control measures to prevent unauthorized entry entrance overall security and ensure the safety of all residents. We look forward to your reply. Have attached another condo access lift system for your reference. Thank you!

MA Reply: [13 Apr 2026] Hi Angel, Sorry for the delayed reply. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention, and we apologise for the concern caused. We understand your observations regarding individuals such as delivery drivers, contractors, and cleaning personnel entering via the basement door without using the intercom system, as well as the issue of tailgating at the bus stop/NTUC entrance. We acknowledge that such situations may pose security concerns and appreciate you highlighting them to us. Please be assured that the Management takes estate security seriously. We will review the current access control measures, including the basement entrance and other access points mentioned, and work with our security team to reinforce stricter monitoring and enforcement where necessary. At the same time, we would also like to remind all residents to avoid allowing unknown individuals to follow them into the premises and to ensure that access doors are properly secured after entry. Residents’ cooperation plays an important role in maintaining overall estate security. We have also noted the reference material you have shared and will take it into consideration as part of our review. Thank you once again for your feedback. We will continue to monitor the situation and implement improvements where feasible to enhance the safety and security of all residents. Rgds, Desmond

Closed
1292-48892026-04-04Blk 49
Security or access issue reported at estate.

Title: Entrance door is open at level 1

Description: Hi Desmond, the last feedback was on 9 Mar 2026 regarding the entrance door at level 1. Understand that there is a note stating to close the door entrance at all times. However, within this 1 month, the door is still open almost everyday. This photo is taken today that both doors are open. (You may wish to view the camera) I hope MCST can take into action to broadcast and highlight the importance to close the entrance door at all times. This is to ensure everyone’s safety. Another consideration is to put alarm if the door is not close. Look forward to hearing from you. Thanks!

MA Reply: [13 Apr 2026] Hi Angel, Thank you for your follow-up and for bringing this to our attention again. We apologise that the issue has persisted despite the earlier notice put up at the entrance. We understand your concern regarding the Level 1 entrance doors being left open, and we agree that this poses a security risk to all residents. Please be assured that the Management takes this matter seriously. While reminders have already been placed at the entrance, we acknowledge that compliance has not been consistent. We have also instructed our security and cleaners to assist closing the door whenever they see it left opened. In addition, we will highlight this matter to our security team to step up monitoring and conduct more frequent checks at the affected access point. We have also taken note of your suggestion to install an alarm system for doors that are left open, and will review its feasibility with the relevant parties. We appreciate your continued feedback and vigilance in helping to maintain the safety and security of the estate. Please do not hesitate to reach out if you observe further concerns. Thank you for your understanding. Rgds, Desmond

1292-47462026-03-20Blk 55
Cleaners leaving Blk 55 level 1 doors open daily.

Title: Blk 55 Level 1 Gates

Description: Noticed that the cleaners always leave the door open at Blk 55 lv 1 open after their daily routine cleaning. I believe the doors were meant to be closed at all times for security purposes. would like management to consider if it is possible to remove the mechanism to keep the door open at 90 degrees. Also noticed that the door will bounce and might not click onto the magnet at certain angle (approximately 5 degrees), i would recommend for management to consider moving the magnet of the door out by 3 to 5cm so that the door will naturally click onto the magnet. Please look into this as it is a security issue.

MA Reply: [20 Mar 2026] Dear Kaien Goh, Thank you for your feedback. We will speak to the cleaning staff regarding leaving the door open, which is not supposed to. As we have also observed, some residents also tend to leave the door opened for their convenience. We will further instruct our cleaning and security team to close the door if they see it being left open. Once again, we thank you for your feedback and we truly appreciate it. Best Regards, Desmond

1292-46912026-03-12Blk 55
Blk 55 level 1 door stays open, not swinging shut.

Title: Blk 55 door level 1 is open all the time

Description: in addition to my previous feedback, the issue is still persisting. I have noticed the signs, but the door remains open. see pictures last evening, this morning. we find the issue is that the door does not swing back if opened all the way. perhaps to check the stopper for the gate as people don’t really turn back to close the door.

MA Reply: [13 Mar 2026] Dear valarie, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks and we understand your concern. Based on our observation, the gate being left open may occur when it is not fully closed after residents enter or exit the premises. For security purposes, we kindly encourage all residents to ensure that the main gate closes completely after passing through. At the same time, we will remind our vendors and management staff to check that the gate is properly closed whenever they pass by during their routine patrols. We appreciate your vigilance in helping to maintain the safety of the building. Best regards, Alfred

1292-46732026-03-10Blk 57
Shop staff using side gate meant only for residents.

Title: Clarification on Visitor Access via Side Gates

Description: Dear MCST, Referring to the notice at the gate stating that the side gates are for residents only and that all visitors must report to the main gate to gain access to the premises (please see attached). However, a staff member from either Cheers or NTUC (see attached) informed a visitor that they could enter through the side gate when it is open. We would appreciate your assistance in informing all retail tenants and their respective persons-in-charge not to share such information with visitors when they ask how to enter the premises. This is important to ensure that the access control measures are properly followed. Safety remains our priority and concern. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [11 Mar 2026] Dear resident, Thank you for your feedback. Please be informed that we will speak to the vendor who provided the incorrect information and remind all retail tenants and their respective staff to refrain from directing visitors to use the side gates. They will also be reminded to advise visitors to report to the main gate for access in accordance with the existing access control measures. We appreciate your feedback and your cooperation in helping us maintain proper security procedures within the premises. Best regards, Alfred

1292-42732026-01-06Blk 53
Safety concern about baseball practice in common areas.

Title: Concern Regarding Baseball Practice

Description: Dear Management Office, I would like to bring to your attention a safety and property concern within the Normanton compound. On one occasion, I noticed a man and a woman practising baseball in the common areas. I observed them practising in the basement car park beside the tennis court, as there was no space available at the basketball court. I also observed them using the basketball court area, hitting the ball against the wall and the gate. The ball was hit with significant force, causing the gate to shake. This may pose a safety risk to nearby residents and could potentially cause damage to the wall and gate over time. There was also a child present in the basketball court area at the time, and there were a few near-miss incidents where the ball came very close to hitting people nearby, which is quite concerning from a safety perspective. I kindly hope that the management can consider sending out a notice or reminder to residents regarding the appropriate use of common facilities, to help ensure residents’ safety and prevent possible damage to the property. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [06 Jan 2026] Hi Eunice Tan, thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We acknowledge your feedback regarding the individual's playing baseball at the basement car park as this poses a safety hazard to residents and motorists passing through the area. In future, if this activity happens again, kindly call the security directly at [phone] so that they can advise the residents. We appreciate your vigilance and cooperation in helping us maintain a safe living environment for all residents.

Closed
1292-38442025-10-01Blk 57
Inconsistent entry policy enforcement by security for Grab rides.

Title: Inconsistent Enforcement of Entry Policy

Description: Dear Management Team, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my concern regarding what appears to be inconsistent enforcement of the estate’s entry policy by the security guards. On 24 October 2025, I returned home via a Grab Private Hire (GPH) and attempted to enter through the side gate (Blk 57). Despite identifying myself as a resident and flash my NP card, the guard on duty denied entry to the GPH vehicle, citing estate policy. I fully respected this decision, I apologized to the driver, and we made a detour to enter through the main gate instead. However, on 1 October 2025, around 4:00pm, I personally witnessed a GPH vehicle entering via the side gate to pick up a neighbour who was standing right beside me at the time. I was also waiting for my own GPH pickup. This incident raises concerns about the inconsistent enforcement of estate rules by the security team. I would appreciate it if you could clarify the following: 1. What is the official policy regarding GPH entry through the side gate? 2. Why was the policy applied to me on 24 October, but not to the other resident and GPH on 1 October? 3. What steps will be taken to ensure consistent and fair treatment for all residents and their visitors? I trust that this matter will be looked into seriously, as consistent application of policies is essential to maintaining a fair and respectful living environment. Thank you for your time and understanding. I look forward to your response.

MA Reply: [02 Oct 2025] Hi ACAC, thank you for your feedback regarding the enforcement of the entry policy. We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience caused by the inconsistencies you have experienced. The 2nd gate is meant for all resident car with DECAL label and if the car is not registered, the gate will not be opened and need to reverse but just in case if there are a lot of cars behind where it creates a high risk to reverse, then the Security Officer will register the car and allow them to enter. Talking about on 1st Oct'25 at 4pm where the Security Officer lapse in the enforcement, we will forward this matter to the service provider OMEGA to address the issue and to review again the current procedures, reminding all the Security Officers on the importance of enforcing the entry policy consistently. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation. Thanks again.

Closed

Our team will assist you in checking and resetting the card to ensure proper access throughout the building. Please feel free to visit us during office hours, and please take note that avoid our lunch hour 1pm to 2 pm. Thanks again.

Closed
1292-32252025-05-13Blk 61
Level 1 gate always left open due to design issue.

Title: l1 gate always not closed

Description: the gate are always left not closed and left wide open. this is due to the fact that residents tend to push hard and then it stays there. pls fix it. due to the recent case of house broken in in nearby condo reminds us that we should not take safety for granted. tks

MA Reply: [14 May 2025] Thank you for your feedback. We understand your concern regarding the gates being left wide open, and we agree that this poses a potential safety risk—especially in light of the recent break-in incident at a nearby condominium. The issue appears to stem from residents pushing the gates too forcefully, causing them to remain ajar. To address this, we will take the following actions: Signage Reminder: We have placed clear signage near the gates to remind residents to close them gently and ensure they are securely shut. Security Patrol Alert: The security team has been instructed to monitor the gates closely during their rounds and close any that are left open. Cleaner Briefing: Cleaning staff will also assist in ensuring that gates are closed properly during their routine work. We have posted a reminder/circular on the HiLife announcement platform to remind residents to keep their doors and gates closed. We appreciate your vigilance and cooperation in helping maintain the safety and security of our community.

Closed

1292-49072026-04-06Blk 57
Concern over removal of security guard at Gate 2.

Title: Removal of Security at Gate 2

Description: Hi noted that there was a notice on the removal of security guard at Gate 2 on the pretext of enhanced security. however gate 2 is one subject to non authorised visitors strolling in. with the removal of the security guards how will the security at gate 2 be enhanced to prevent these?

MA Reply: [13 Apr 2026] Hi Mdm, Thank you for your feedback and for sharing your concerns regarding the security arrangements at Gate 2. We understand your concerns, particularly with regard to the possibility of unauthorised individuals entering through this access point. Please be assured that the Management has been closely monitoring the situation following the recent changes. In light of the observations and feedback received from residents, we are currently reviewing the effectiveness of the existing arrangement. We are also considering reinstating a security officer at Gate 2, and this will very likely be implemented to further strengthen access control and address the concerns raised. In the meantime, our security team has been advised to step up patrols and monitoring of the area to minimise any potential risks. We appreciate your vigilance and feedback, and we will continue to review and enhance our security measures to ensure the safety of all residents. Thank you for your understanding. Rgds, Desmond

Closed
1292-47542026-03-22Blk 45
Security rude to resident about pet poop incident.

Title: rude security staff

Description: I happened to bring my two puppies down for a short walk and forgot to bring my poop bags and water down which I usually did. hence when my puppies pooped unexpectedly as they usually don’t outside, i had to turn away from the poop to look for leaves to help clear the poop. but your security guard who was at the side monitoring me with an unfriendly expression saw me struggling with the two dogs who bring puppies were trying to go other ways, and she said this “ then u leave it like this?”, referring to the poop. but I wasn’t leaving it like this, based on bodily structure I had to leave the poop to take the leaves and was struggling with two dogs at the same time. her tone was accusatory and uncalled for. she could have been more helpful and understanding to clarify my intention before just shooting out words like this and accusing people of things which may not be their intention. the security is here to help keep order with the end aim of creating a safe environment to live in. but how safe is it when we have to dismay at meeting such security guards when just walking out of our house to enjoy the estate? is there a better way the guard could have communicate that may result in a better outcome? I explained to her I was going to look for leaves to clear the poop, and can she be kinder and more understanding before just accusing? I asked her what’s her name and she mumbled something unclear (why are u afraid to say clearly when you were clearly accusing people of doing things just a few moments ago)? and she continue to say why? I’m just doing my job. very unkind and one day u will know how it feels to be treated the same. please educate your security to moderate their behaviour and don’t behave as if they own the entire place. thank you.

MA Reply: [24 Mar 2026] Dear Ms Victoria, Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. Firstly, we sincerely apologise for the unpleasant experience you encountered during your interaction with our security officer. We understand how such an incident may have caused frustration and we regret the discomfort caused. Please be assured that our security officers are tasked with maintaining cleanliness and order within the estate. In the course of their duties, they have previously encountered instances where pet owners did not clean up after their pets, and are therefore vigilant in monitoring such situations. That said, we acknowledge that the manner of communication is equally important. We will be speaking with the officer involved to reinforce the importance of professionalism, empathy, and clear communication when engaging with residents, so as to avoid similar misunderstandings in future. Thank you for your feedback and understanding. Best Regards, Desmond / Rui Ling

Closed

Thank you for keeping the pool safe for residents.

MA Reply: [07 Feb 2026] Thank you for your compliments regarding our security guard. We have forwarded your kind feedback to the security company. We appreciate you taking the time to share this with us.

1292-38202025-09-27Blk 53
Complaint about rude security guard to visitor at midnight.

Title: Rude Security Guard

Description: I want to lodge a complaint against one of the security guards on duty around 12 midnight on 27 Sep. He is an Indian man who claimed his name is Jonathan. My visitor arrived at NP around midnight (27 Sep) and when the security guard asked for the block number and unit number, my visitor told him to wait so he can check which block number it was. The security guard then mumbled something softly which my visitor did not manage to hear. He then asked for the guard’s name but the guard responded very rudely in a loud voice “why should I tell you my name?”Then the security guard reluctantly gave his name. This is not the first time my visitors have encountered rude behaviour from the security guards here. I hope MA talk to the security firm and impress upon them that residents and visitors are not here to bear the brunt of their anger.

MA Reply: [27 Sep 2025] Dear Tricia, We refer to your feedback above with thank. We sincerely apologize for the unpleasant experience your visitor had. Please be assured that we take such matters seriously. We will raise this issue with the security supervisor and the company to ensure appropriate follow up and to remind all guards to maintain professionalism at all times. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention.

1292-36102025-08-11Blk 55
Unauthorized vehicle parked near resident entrance area.

Title: unauthorised vehicle

Description: Hi, noted as I was driving in at the residents' entrance, the driver of this car was probably instructed by our security guard to return to the main entrance, but it so happened to park near where I stopped and I saw the driver park (poorly parked and sticking out of the lot into the driving path) and enter block 57. Thus it is unauthorised parking. Fyi. Appreciate that our security is doing a great job so far but there are still errant visitor drivers.

MA Reply: [11 Aug 2025] Dear Sharon, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation with regards to the unregistered vehicle entering via the 2nd entrance located near Blk 57. We have forwarded this matter to security to take the necessary action. At the same time, we would like to explain that due to the design of the 2nd entrance, it is not safe to request vehicles that have mistakenly enter the entrance to turn around/reverse back into the main road. Rather, security at the guard post will register their details as per the main entrance and proceed to allow entry. However, that does not mean that visitors coming via this entrance are permitted to park in B1 Upper. Therefore, security will be activated to take the necessary action on this vehicle. We hope this provides an amicable response to your feedback. As no response is required, we will close this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

MA Reply: [26 May 2025] Thank you for bringing to our attention the concerns regarding the conduct of the security guard on duty at the second entry/exit post. We have already communicated your feedback to the security company and have emphasized the importance of professionalism and vigilance in their duties. We are committed to ensuring that all security staff adhere to the highest standards of conduct to maintain a safe and respectful environment for all residents. We appreciate your vigilance in helping us maintain the quality of our community and thank you for your continued support.

Closed
1292-38162025-09-25Blk 59
External cameras on landed units covering shared footpaths and pool.

Title: Cameras at landed units

Description: Several of the landed properties have installed external cameras on their units. While the intention is understandable, a number of these cameras appear to cover significant portions of the shared footpaths and pool areas. This creates a legitimate concern for residents’ sense of privacy and comfort in using common facilities, particularly for families with young children. Could management please review whether the placement of these cameras complies with the development’s by-laws and community standards, and where necessary, engage the property owners to adjust their cameras so the focus of the monitoring is on their own property?

MA Reply: [25 Sep 2025] Dear Paul, thank you for taking the time to write in to us regarding this matter. We understand that the installation of the CCTVs by some of the terrace houses may be a cause for concern. Our response below will be based on the Building Maintenance & Strata Management Act as well as the section on CCTV cameras under the Private Security Industry Act. According to Section 37A of the Building Maintenance and Strata Management Act (BMSMA), subsidiary proprietors are permitted to install safety equipment — which includes CCTV cameras — on their lots, including windows or doors facing outdoors. This is allowed provided the installation: 1. Does not damage any part of the common property; 2. Is carried out properly and professionally; 3. Maintains the aesthetic of the development; and 4. Does not unreasonably interfere with the peaceful enjoyment of other residents. Since the MCST does not have the legal authority to prohibit the installation of such devices on private property, we are only able to encourage/advise terrace house owners to be mindful with where their CCTV cameras are pointing, as long as the purpose is for personal safety. Since you brought this matter up, the management will be taking active steps to walk the ground and identify units that have cameras pointing outwards, to ensure that they are not being intrusive to the use of the facilities in our estate. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We hope that we were able to address your feedback amicably and apologise if the answer may not be the one you expected. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for your kind attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed

Case IDDateBlockTitleStatus
1292-49082026-04-06Blk 45
Security or access issue reported at estate.

Title: HiLife Panel Malfunction and Remote Access Issue

Description: Dear Sir/Mdm, Thank you for your previous updates. The new features are indeed appreciated and have made things more convenient for residents. However, I would like to seek clarification on how we can release the door lock remotely from within the home or via mobile devices, as mentioned in your announcement dated 1 April 2026. I would also like to bring to your attention that since this update, the HiLife display panel in my home has not been functioning properly. The system frequently hangs or restarts when attempting to release the EM lock or view visitors. Initially, I assumed this was part of ongoing upgrading or fine-tuning, but the issue has persisted and appears to be worsening. The panel is now completely unresponsive (blackout) and no longer functional. This has caused significant inconvenience, especially when I need to contact family members to assist with unlocking the door, as mobile reception in the basement remains poor despite prior feedback. I kindly request that this matter be treated with urgency and that you assist in checking and restoring my home system as soon as possible. The malfunction has greatly affected our daily convenience. Additionally, I would appreciate it if the newly installed features could be more clearly indicated, including where they can be accessed or viewed (e.g., EM lock access/view locations). Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I look forward to your assistance. Yours sincerely, Toh

MA Reply: [07 Apr 2026] Dear Mr Desmond, Thank you for your feedback. Please be informed that the Defect Liability Period (DLP) for your unit has ended. You may need to engage the contractor or your own contractor at your own cost. Intercom, doorbell contractor details: HILIFE INTERACTIVE PTE LTD Contact: [phone] Email: [email] Best Regards, Rui Ling

Title: baby stroller

MA Reply: [19 Jul 2025] Hi Ms. Racheal Lam, thanks for the feedback. We have sent out the notification on 'NOTICE OF VIOLATION ENFORCEMENT' dated 9th June 2025 highlighting to all residents to remove all the personal belongings at the common passageway, riser, fire exit staircase including the fire hose reel box within 3 days from the date of the notice being issued out. This notice was sent out through HiLife and notice board on the 9th June 2025. Based on our records, we did the clearance at blk 51 on 18th June 2025 in the morning, and we even knock at your unit 20-28 but no one opened the door, so we have decided to remove all the items in the Fire Hose Reel Box. Furthermore, SCDF have given us (MA) stern warning to ensure that nothing to be kept in the Fire Hose Reel Box, otherwise the SCDF will penalize us if items are still kept in the box. For example, if at that time SCDF needs to use the fire hose reel to put off the fire and with these items blocking the equipment, it will delay the Firemaen job thus causing more casualties. Attach is the notification as for your reference. Sorry for any inconvenience.

Closed
1292-33482025-06-11Blk 53
Questioning misdated violation enforcement notice on pram.

Title: Question on notice of violation enforcement

Description: I saw the attached notice (misdated 4 February 2025) posted on my pram today (it certainly has not been pasted there since February 2025). A picture of the pram is attached for your reference. I'd like to ask your office exactly what the issue is here?

MA Reply: [11 Jun 2025] Possible Reasons for the Encroachment Letter: Obstruction of Common Areas: 1)Management may consider your pram as obstructing a common corridor or exit route, especially if it’s left outside your unit for extended periods. 2)Fire Safety Regulations: SCDF guidelines state that common corridors (especially those less than 1.2m wide) must be kept clear to allow safe evacuation during emergencies. 3)Routine Enforcement: Management be conducting periodic checks and issuing standard notices to all items left in common areas. Any enquires, do drop us feedback. -w

MA Reply: [08 May 2025] Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We have arranged for the removal of the carton box from the carpark lot. Our cleaning team will ensure the area is cleared promptly. Regarding your suggestion to install a CCTV camera near the entrance, we appreciate your feedback and understand your concerns. We will review the feasibility of camera installation at the suggested location, including storage and retrieval options for recorded footage, and discuss it with the Management Council for further consideration. Please feel free to share any additional feedback or observations.

1292-42782026-01-06Blk 49
Bicycle parking area still messy with abandoned bikes.

Title: bicycle parking

Description: thanks for implementing the measure for the bicycle parking. MA Reply: [06 Jan 2026] Dear Eric, thank you for your feedback. Clearance of items at the bicycle parking area will only be conducted after the bicycle registration exercise has commenced. When the time comes and approval has been given, we will post out details regarding bicycle registration via HiLife as a circular as well as put it up on the notice boards. When ample time has been given for awarded bicycle tags to be tied and displayed on the allocated bicycles, management will begin the clearing process of any items found at the bicycle parking area that is not a registered bicycle. We hope this answers your feedback amicably at this point in time. You can expect more details regarding bicycle registration to be released around February 2026. Thank you for your kind attention. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically.

Closed
1292-41942025-12-22Blk 55
Inquiry about bicycle registration launch date and old bicycle removal.

Title: bicycle notices

Description: Hi, currently I saw the notice on hiLife apps on the bicycle portion. understand that management will give notice to those non bicycle stuff first. but I would like to know when the management launch the registration for bicycle and also some bicycles that not in use what or how the management handle with it? currently I am still using my bicycle on and off, but I park at a non parking area cause of no available rack to park.

MA Reply: [22 Dec 2025] Dear Keith, thank you for your feedback. Correct, the management is currently enforcing on non-bicycle items first to free up more space for bicycles to park at the designated parking area. Regarding bicycle registration, the management will release more details on how residents can register their bicycles with the office in due course. Removal of unused/derelict bicycles will be done along with non-registered bicycles after the first registration window closes. We hope this provides an amicable response at this point in time. Rest assured that more details regarding subsequent phases of the bicycle registration will be released before any further enforcement actions will be taken. Thank you for your kind attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-41842025-12-21Blk 55
Bicycles repeatedly left in non-compliant areas impeding carpark road.

Title: recurrent obstructions at car park area

Description: it seems the residents continue to leave their bicycles in non compliant areas such as in the photo. perhaps security will have to be more vigilant to remove these obstructions regularly each day at more frequency. it is unsightly and sends a message to residents that they can continue this behavior if allowed to continue without consequence. someone even left a chair there.

MA Reply: [22 Dec 2025] Dear Valerie, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation of the recurring bicycles found at the bicycle parking area as per the attached image. The estate is in the midst of conducting its bicycle registration exercise. The aim of the exercise is to significantly reduce the population of bicycles at the bicycle parking areas by restricting the parking area to only registered bicycles. All non-bicycle items, unclaimed and derelict bicycles will be removed upon completion of the exercise. This will free up space at the designated bicycle parking area for the registered bicycles. We aim to complete the bicycle registration sometime before the Lunar New Year, which will allow the management with security to perform a sweep of unclaimed, derelict and unregistered bicycles accordingly. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. Your kind understanding and patience will be appreciated as we work towards resolving this matter and prevent any further overcrowding at the bicycle parking area. Thank you for your kind attention and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed

Title: bicycle area

Description: Hi BM, 2 months ago, you guys circulated the bicycle parking letter. today the parking area at b49 is still as messy. Hope you guys can look into it

MA Reply: [24 Nov 2025] Dear Eric, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation regarding the bicycle situation at the designated bicycle parking lots. Kindly note that the management is in preparation for the next phase of the bicycle enforcement. This next phase is scheduled to begin after the General Meeting, sometime in Mid-December 2025.After the announcement is made, security will begin enforcing and removing any items found at the bicycle parking area that is not a bicycle, such as prams, trolleys and scooters. This is to free up space so that bicycles can be parked in a safer manner at the parking areas. For the time being, we seek your kind understanding as we continue to work within our capacity to ensure that the bicycles parked do not obstruct traffic flow in the carpark. Thank you for your kind attention. We hope that we were able to address your feedback amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

We hope that this will provide more parking spaces for bicycles to be parked in the designated areas before commencing the registration exercise in 2026. We hope that this addresses your feedback amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Closed
1292-36992025-08-28Blk 55
Disagreement with bicycle rack usage enforcement notice.

Title: notice of bicycle rack/stand use

Description: as an owner/resident, I find your notice unfair, unreasonable and not addressing any key issues . I do applaude the initiative to register bicycles and remove unused bicycles. however, a few key points are left unaddressed: 1) why is there discrimination of children bicycles; 2) inadequate of bicycle racks/stands and 3) how are you going to allocate the stands and racks (base on current usage? totally unfair as those who collected keys are able to use them earlier. base on a lottery system? others?) in lieu of such unaddressed items and with AGM due later this year, may I suggest only a registration of bicycles are done at this stage and the inadequacy of racks/stands be resolved later with owners/residents input.

MA Reply: [28 Aug 2025] Dear JiunWoei, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation regarding the latest circular on bicycle registration exercise. The initial intention of excluding children's bicycles from the registration exercise is because management observes a higher number of adult bicycles. To cater the largest possible number of lots for a larger volume of bicycles, we decided to make the decision to only allow adult bicycles for registration. After much deliberation, management has decided that it will only be enforcing and removing non-bicycle items found at the designated bicycle areas. For points 2 and 3, more information will be given when it is firmed up. kindly note that the circular is only put out to announce that enforcement and removal of unauthorised items at the parking areas. Bicycle registration is scheduled to be held at a tentative date between Nov-Dec 2025. Rest assured that the registration of bicycle will be conducted in the fairest form possible, in order to prevent any discrepancy. We appreciate your feedback and will take it into consideration regarding bicycle registration. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for your kind attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-36982025-08-28Blk 55
Concern about restriction on children bicycles at parking.

Title: bicycle parking Memo

Description: Subject: Concern Regarding Restriction on Children’s Bicycles Dear [Managing Agent’s Name / Management Office], I refer to the recent notice stating that bicycle parking facilities in the development may only be used for adult bicycles, and that children’s bicycles are not allowed. Firstly, thank you for initiating the tagging exercise to better manage bicycle usage. This is a positive and much needed step. At the same time, it is important that such measures are implemented in a fair and non-discriminatory manner, ensuring that all residents—including families with children—can reasonably use common facilities. The current policy restricting bicycle racks to adult bicycles appears arbitrary and discriminatory. A bicycle is a bicycle regardless of the rider’s age, and as the development is still in the initial period with no management committee formed or by-laws passed by subsidiary proprietors, this policy is not currently enforceable. While existing bicycle racks are heavily used, practical solutions could be explored at the first AGM. For example, a small number of underutilized car park lots—particularly those far from residential blocks and seldom used—could be temporarily converted to bicycle parking. Even a handful of such lots among the 1,870 available car park lots could significantly relieve pressure on existing bicycle facilities. I hope the management will review this policy and adopt an approach that is fair, inclusive, and consistent with reasonable use of common property, helping to foster a cooperative and harmonious community. Thank you for your attention and understanding.

MA Reply: [28 Aug 2025] Dear Nikhil, thank you for your comprehensive feedback. We note your observation and have decided that we will not enforce/remove children's bicycles for this bicycle exercise. Rather, we will only be enforcing/removing any other items found in the area. Rest assured that another announcement will be made regarding this change in policy, as well as further details to clarify any ambiguity within the previous notice. We will take your suggestion on converting parking lots into temporary bicycle holding areas and will reserve them to the management council upon its inception post-annual general meeting. We hope this responds to your feedback amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed

Description: dear MA, our bicycle parking place is going to be like the extended store room. I do not have a bicycle below but this is getting out of hand and unsightly already. some of them has parked them since TOP and not even move. MA should do something to control it

MA Reply: [19 Aug 2025] Dear Eric, Thank you for your feedback. We note your observation and appreciate the time taken to send us photos of this matter. The management will organise a bicycle tagging exercise in due course to control the population of bicycles. Meanwhile, we will put up an announcement soon to inform residents that only bicycles are permitted to be stored at the bicycle racks. Anything that is not classified as a bicycle will be tagged and removed after sufficient notice is given. We hope that this provides an amicable response to your feedback. Once again, thank you for taking the time to write in to us regarding this issue and we wish you a pleasant day ahead. As no further response is required, we will close this feedback automatically.

Title: safety hazard follow up

I’m fearful about my family safety as well as our other neighbours.

MA Reply: [06 Jun 2025] Thank you for bringing to our attention the issue of the motorized bicycle and two scooters obstructing the stairway. We understand your concern regarding this matter. Please be assured that we have reported the situation to the relevant authorities for further investigation and action. We appreciate your vigilance in helping maintain the safety and accessibility of our community spaces --- [17 Jun 2025] This matter has escalated to the SCDF. We will follow up and take necessary action to address this matter. Any enquires, please do drop us feedback.

Closed
1292-33042025-05-31Blk 59
Scooters and motorised bikes blocking entrance, safety hazard.

Title: safety hazard

Description: hello. it’s been a few days and still no action taken. the scooters are still blocking the entrance and the motorised bicycle is blocking the stairwell. this is a safety hazard and should not be delayed in taking actions. I hope the management office can take immediate action, as it will have dire consequences in the event of fire or other similar situation.

MA Reply: [03 Jun 2025] Thank you for your feedback regarding the ongoing obstruction caused by scooters and a motorized bicycle in the common areas. We acknowledge the seriousness of this issue, as it poses significant safety hazards, particularly in the event of an emergency such as a fire. As per the Singapore Civil Defense Force (SCDF) guidelines, common areas, including stairwells and corridors, must remain unobstructed to ensure safe evacuation and access for emergency services. We have approached the resident responsible and issued a final warning. They are now required to remove the motorized bicycle from the staircase within three days and either store it within their unit or park it in the designated bicycle lot. Failure to comply will result in further action, including the possibility of reporting the matter to the Singapore Civil Defense Force (SCDF) for enforcement under the Fire Safety Act. We appreciate your vigilance in bringing this matter to our attention and assure you that we are taking all necessary steps to uphold the safety and well-being of all residents.

Closed
1292-32982025-05-30Blk 59
Scooters blocking fire exit and bomb shelter at Blk 59.

Title: safety hazard

Description: hi. this is blk 59, level 23. these scooters have been blocking the fire exit and bomb shelter for a long time. I’ve seen notices given by the management team to the owners for these scooters at least twice and no further actions were taken and the scooters still remains there. this is a safety hazard and I suggest that management team enforces the warning given to the owners. the scooters belong to 23-63. sometimes he also leave his motorised bicycle in the stairway and block the exit. I’m not so sure if he uses the plug to charge though.

MA Reply: [30 May 2025] Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We acknowledge your concern regarding the repeated obstruction of the fire exit and bomb shelter area at Block 59, Level 23 by scooters belonging to Unit [unit]. Safety is a top priority, and we agree that such obstructions pose a serious hazard, particularly in the event of an emergency. Our team has previously issued written advisories to the resident of [unit], and we regret to hear that the situation has not improved. Security have been informed to carry out advisory reminder We will also conduct a check to determine whether any charging of motorized vehicles is occurring in the stairwell, and if found, we will address this immediately in coordination with SCDF guidelines. -W

1292-47912026-03-27Blk 49
Items and cartons blocking fire safety door in corridor.

Title: Fire Safety Hazard Obstruction at Common Corridor

Description: Hi Management, I would like to report a fire safety hazard at the common corridor outside unit 23-19. There are items/cartons that have been placed there for many days. These items appear to be positioned directly beside / in front of the fire safety cabinet area, which may obstruct emergency access or delay response during a fire incident. This issue is recurring, as similar items are frequently left along the corridor. Based on observation, these appear to be deliveries from overseas courier shipments and are left unattended for extended periods. This is a concern because: fire safety equipment should remain readily accessible at all times items left at the common corridor may obstruct movement during an emergency evacuation cartons and stored materials may add unnecessary fire load in the common area Kindly arrange for the items to be removed and advise the unit owner to refrain from placing belongings at the fire safety access area or along the common corridor. Please investigate and take the necessary corrective action. Thank you.

MA Reply: [27 Mar 2026] Dear JTCCC, Thank you for your feedback and for bringing this matter to our attention. We take fire safety concerns seriously and will be speaking to the resident of the unit accordingly to advise them not to place any belongings in front of or near the fire hose reel and along the common corridor, as this may obstruct emergency access. We will also continue to monitor the situation and take the necessary actions to prevent recurrence. Best Regards, Desmond / Rui Ling

Closed
1292-33512025-06-12Blk 53
Follow-up on violation enforcement letter for corridor items.

Title: follow up on violation enforcement letter

Description: I refer to the misdated violation enforcement notice (attached) as well as the response from your office (also attached). The pram is clearly not blocking access to any common corridor or exit route. Further, the size of the corridor is more than 1.2m and my pram does not reduce the width of the corridor to less than 1.2m (pictured). I have checked the other units on my level who similarly have personal belongings stretching out past their door - pictures are attached of a child's scooter and bicycle, and of a very large plant which is entirely on the common area. Neither of these appear to have had similar notices placed on them, despite being on the same floor as me. I've also checked a couple of other floors - many other residents have plants, flower pots, shoe cabinets and even door mats stretching out onto and/or placed entirely on the common area. I've attached pictures of these as well, and as you will no doubt see, not a single one of these had the same notices pasted on them. This is on top of the many instances of shoes placed along the common corridor as well. Each and every one of these should similarly raise the same concerns that your office mentioned in your reply to me. Yet no notices seem to have been issued for any of these. The failure to be even-handed in the alleged enforcement of by-laws is concerning. Furthermore, the pram as currently positioned does not obstruct movement in the corridor nor intrude on any person's rights. I'm sure you would be aware of the case of the shoe cabinet at The Infiniti - https://www.tnp.sg/news/singapore/judge-rejects-condos-bid-remove-cabinet-corridor where the judge refused to grant an order for the removal of a shoe cabinet along the common corridor for precisely the same reasons. Take notice that if my pram is removed by your office, I will be seeking full compensation for the cost of replacing the pram immediately. Your office's time and efforts would be far better spent on actual issues within the development, such as the regularly failing gates, security guards sleeping on the job and failing to man their stations, and the continued lack of mobile reception almost 2 years after key collection (and despite promises that it would have been completed by Q125).

MA Reply: [12 Jun 2025] Thank you for your feedback and for taking the time to share your concerns, including the photographs and reference materials. We acknowledge your feedback regarding the placement of your pram, as well as your observations of similar items found in the common corridors across the development. We also note your reference to the corridor width and the case involving The Infiniti condominium. Please allow us to clarify that under the Building and Strata Management Act (BMSMA) and SCDF Fire Safety Guidelines, items placed in common corridors must not obstruct safe and unhindered access for residents and emergency responders. While your measurements may suggest compliance with the 1.2-metre minimum clearance, enforcement is not solely based on measurements but also on placement, frequency of use, and potential hazard assessment. Regarding the photos of other items in the common area, we appreciate your bringing these to our attention. We will be conducting a thorough inspection across all floors to ensure consistent enforcement of the by-laws. If any non-compliant items are found, appropriate notices will be issued. As for your reference to The Infiniti case, while we acknowledge that precedent, it is important to note that each development may have its own set of by-laws and contextual considerations. The ruling in that case does not necessarily override local MCST rules, which are formulated in line with statutory requirements and the specific needs of the estate. Please also be assured that the misdated notice has been noted and rectified in our internal records. We understand your frustrations regarding broader estate issues such as gate reliability, mobile signal coverage, and security staffing. These matters are being reviewed, and we are working closely with relevant contractors and service providers to implement improvements where possible. We appreciate your cooperation in keeping the estate safe and accessible for all residents. Should you wish to discuss this matter further, you may also raise it at the upcoming AGM or request a formal meeting with the Management Office. Thank you for your understanding. -w --- [12 Jun 2025] Thank you for your feedback and for taking the time to share your observations. We understand your perspective regarding the pram and appreciate your reference to similar cases. While individual items may not always seem obstructive, our obligation is to adhere strictly to SCDF fire safety requirements and the estate’s House Rules. These regulations mandate that all common corridors must be kept clear to ensure safe evacuation routes in the event of an emergency. To ensure consistency and compliance across all blocks, we will be carrying out a block-wide removal of items deemed non-compliant with SCDF regulations. This includes any items placed in common areas that could pose a fire or obstruction risk. We are currently reviewing the other cases you’ve highlighted, and appropriate action will be taken where necessary. If you wish to discuss this further or would like clarification on specific cases, please feel free to contact us directly. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in maintaining a safe living environment for all residents -W --- [12 Jun 2025] Thank you for your clarification regarding the placement of the prams. Please be assured that we will not remove the prams at this point. We will reassess the area to ensure that it complies with SCDF requirements. We appreciate your understanding and thank you for your cooperation in helping us maintain a safe and orderly environment. --- [17 Jun 2025] We will close this feedback. Any enquires, please do drop us feedback.

Request to trace residents dumping bulky trash via CCTV.

Title: Bulky Refuse Disposals

Description: Dear MA team, are we able to trace from CCTV the owners/residents who leave these bulky trash outside of the Bin instead of inside? Should identify which units and send them violation notice especially the Furniture Dumpster should bring to main rubbish centre. MA Reply: [16 Mar 2026] Thank you for your feedback. Please see our responses below: 1) Security is unable to trace which unit is involved without the actual date and time of the incident. 2) We will write to Cora to check if they are able to provide open-gap bins. 3) We are sourcing for CCTV pricing on Shopee and will revert to the Council once we have the details. 4) We have informed the cleaner to remove all the rubbish and dispose of it at the bin center. Best Regards - Joanne

Title: Delay in Gym Maintenance

Description: Hi, the treadmills in the gym have not been fully working since last year, in November - you can refer to my feedback history. The mgmt has been saying that treadmills will be replaced / fixed since January, and then said end of March, and now only end of April. Please expedite and ensure that the new treadmills will be installed soon. There are only three working treadmills and this is not enough for a development of this size - at the worst time, there was only two. This is getting really unacceptable, I go to the gym every other day and there is almost always a line for the treadmill. It has taken almost half a year to fix this problem. Why are the existing treadmills not being fixed while the new treadmills are being procured, especially given the month long delay?

MA Reply: [07 Apr 2026] Dear Elizabeth, Thank you for your feedback. Due to the excess usage, the main components of the treadmills are spoilt. The cost of parts replacement and overhauling of motors are very high hence the purchase of new machines. It's expected to come 3rd - 4th week of April.

1292-47682026-03-24Blk 61
Three of five gym running machines out of order.

Title: Repair the running machines as soon as possible

Description: I would like to provide some feedback regarding the gym facilities in the apartment. Currently, three out of the five running machines are out of order, which has significantly reduced the availability of equipment. As a result, it is often difficult for residents to use the machines, especially during peak hours. I kindly request that the management arrange for repairs as soon as possible. Restoring the running machines to working condition would greatly improve the overall experience for residents and help ease the current congestion in the gym. 1292-46802026-03-10Blk 47
Multiple treadmills unusable for months, inadequate gym.

Title: Complaint Regarding Unusable Treadmills and Insuff

Description: Dear Management, I would like to raise a concern regarding the current condition and capacity of the condo gym facilities. Several treadmills in the gym have been unusable for an extended period. The last update provided about their repair was on 10 January, and it has now been two full months without resolution. This delay is unacceptable, and I would appreciate an immediate update and a clear timeline for when the treadmills will be repaired and returned to service. In addition, the gym facilities are severely insufficient for a development of approximately 1,800 units. The limited number of machines, combined with the number of residents using the gym, results in very long waiting times for equipment. It is common to wait significant periods just to access basic machines such as treadmills, which significantly affects residents’ ability to exercise. The space and equipment capacity of the gym clearly do not match the size of the resident population. This leads to overcrowding and an overall poor user experience for residents who rely on these facilities. I urge the management to address the following as soon as possible: Provide an immediate update and timeline for repairing the unusable treadmills. Improve maintenance responsiveness for gym equipment. Review the adequacy of gym facilities and equipment given the number of units in the development. As residents, we expect the facilities we pay for through maintenance fees to be properly maintained and adequate for the community. I look forward to your prompt response and concrete actions to resolve these issues.

MA Reply: [11 Mar 2026] Dear Lena, Thank you for your feedback. We are in the final phase of getting new treadmills for our estate, and we hope to be able to complete that before end of the month. We apologise for the delay but we are trying our best to expedite. Best regards, Desmond

1292-39362025-10-19Blk 59
Gym treadmill 30-minute rule repeatedly breached by resident.

Title: Follow-up on Formal Complaint – Repeated Breach of

Description: Dear Management, Thank you for your previous response. However, I regret to inform you that the same issue has occurred again today, despite the clear 30-minute usage notice being displayed in the gym. It appears that certain residents continue to occupy the cardio machines far beyond the 30-minute limit, even during peak hours, preventing others from having fair access. I would like to kindly request that management consider implementing more active monitoring or enforcement measures, such as: • Random checks by security during busy hours, or • A sign-in sheet or timer system to track equipment usage. As this has become a repeated issue affecting multiple residents, I hope the management can take stronger steps to ensure fair usage for everyone. Thank you for your understanding and assistance.

MA Reply: [21 Oct 2025] Hi emko, thanks for the feedback. Currently the lift parking configuration at any point of time for the 3 lifts is at level 1, 10 and 20. This morning our technician came down and check on all the 3 lifts and so far, the lift parking landing are accordingly to the current configuration so there is no issue, and we close the case. At the same time, we also feedback to KONE team for them to follow up. Thanks again.

Closed
1292-39002025-10-12Blk 59
Resident hogging gym treadmill beyond 30-minute limit repeatedly.

Title: Formal Complaint – Repeated Breach of Gym Usage

Description: Dear Management, I would like to formally report a repeated breach of the gym usage rules. Despite the clear 30-minute usage limit label placed on the machines and previous reminders from MCST, one resident continues to occupy the treadmill for around 90 minutes each time. This occurred on 12/Oct/2025, at 9:30AM I would appreciate it if the management could take appropriate action, such as issuing a warning to the resident, as the repeated disregard for the house rules affects the fair usage for all residents.Or please send security to check around 9:00-10:00 AM every Sunday(This is obviously peak time) Thank you for your understanding and assistance. Best regards,

MA Reply: [13 Oct 2025] Dear Sir/Mdm, Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We appreciate your concern regarding the fair usage of the gym equipment, especially during peak hours. The 30-minute usage label on the machines is intended as a courtesy reminder to all residents to be considerate and allow equitable access for others, particularly when the gym is busy. We have placed clear notices in the gym reminding users to limit equipment use to 30 minutes, so that everyone has a fair opportunity to enjoy the facilities. The security already conducts regular patrols, including in the gym, as part of their routine rounds. Regards, The Management

1292-47922026-03-27Blk 53
Private-hire cars queue at second entrance causing backup.

Title: second entrance security

Description: hi just saw the notice, but second entrance often times PHC drivers tried to enter but got sticker and the residents’ cars are all waiting behind. When the queue build up it is impossible for the car to get out and u turn. what is the solution to this? unless the management has a solution to prevent cab and PHC drivers from accessing the second entrance, this may increase the hassle of entrance blockage having to wait the security officer to be deployed from first entrance to resolve the issue

MA Reply: [31 Mar 2026] Dear Mr Sean, Thank you for your feedback. To address this, we will be putting up clearer signage before the turn-in towards our carpark to better inform and deter unauthorized vehicles from entering via the second entrance. In the meantime, should there be a situation where an unauthorized vehicle is unable to reverse due to vehicles queueing behind, residents may use the intercom located at the entrance to contact our security officers at the main guardhouse for assistance. We appreciate your understanding and patience as we work to improve the situation. Best Regards, Desmond / Rui Ling

1292-47872026-03-26Blk 45
Motorcycles parked improperly every day.

Title: Motor cycle parking

Description: No change from last time when I raised the issue of inappropriate parking of bikes. Refer to photo for details. This is a problem everyday!

MA Reply: [27 Mar 2026] Dear GSSGP, Thank you for your feedback and for bringing this matter to our attention. We would like to assure you that Management is aware of the ongoing issue regarding the inappropriate parking of motorcycles. Actions have already been taken, including the blacklisting of several offending motorcycles. However, we have observed that in some instances, by the time our security personnel arrive to investigate upon receiving feedback, the motorcycles have already left the area. This has posed some challenges in enforcement. Nonetheless, we will be enhancing our security patrols, particularly during peak hours, to better monitor and deter such occurrences. We appreciate your patience and understanding, and we will continue to work towards improving the situation. Best Regards, Desmond / Rui Ling

Our security team will remind delivery riders to park only at the designed parking lots. During security patrols, if they do not comply, the security will give them a warning letter or clamp the vehicle. Best Regards, Rui Ling

1292-42802026-01-06Terrace
Non-residents advised by clinic to park at condo carpark.

Title: Vistors to NP Clinic advised to park at NP

Description: I was informed by my colleague that she visited the NP clinic this afternoon and she had her car parked at NP carpark - as advised by the clinic staff to do so and was allowed entry to the carpark by security guards at main gate when the reason given was “To visit the clinic”. After consultation, my colleague, instead of going through the main gate, she conveniently tried her luck at the side gate (outside NTUC) and informed the security guard that she needed “to collect car”. She was granted entry again by the guard on duty with no question asked. May I demand 1) why there is no standard safety protocols in placed? 2) Are our security guards so “accommodating” to allow non residents to enter our private premise without taking particulars? Are they trained to work in a professional way? While we cannot stop the clinic to advise their patients to park at NP carpark but yet our security guards are “supporting” that. If every outsiders are to use this excuse to enter NP, what makes us a private property? Importantly, where is the safety? Obviously our security guards are not doing their job which they are tasked to do! Why do we need them in the first place if they are not responsible in their roles and duties?

MA Reply: [07 Jan 2026] Hi Ms. Veronica Wong, thank you for taking the time to share your feedback regarding the performance of the security officers. We sincerely regret to learn of your concerns and appreciate you bringing this matter to our attention. We have highlighted the issue to the security service provide for their immediate review and improvement with the required standard operating procedures. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

1292-38752025-10-06Blk 45

Title: Main carpark entry

Description: Hi mgt, it is very irresponsible to say drivers are expected to slow down where the entrance is built with an up hill manner where the stop line is hidden as it's at the bottom of the slope. If anything undesirable accident should happen, I'm sure the management is implicated. As for the exit at the security guard post, placing a mirror helps regulate the traffic safety. When such safety hazard is identified, even HDB will take action to erect the mirror and you are saying the professional is telling you it's fine, then get the professional to mitigate for you when mgt is implicated.

1292-38502025-10-02Blk 57
Disputing management response about gantry wait time issue.

Title: Case No.: [phone]

Description: Dear Team, Thank you for your response, I do not accept your explanation. There wasn’t any cars coming in after the GPH while I was waiting for my GPH for 10-mins and when I travel out from the side exit there wasn’t any cars at the side entrance.

MA Reply: [03 Oct 2025] Hi ACAC, thanks for the feedback. We have checked from the CCTV and discovered that there was a car behind the GPH car. Before the Security released the GPH car, he took down all necessary details before open the door. Sorry for any inconvenience and thank you for your understanding.

Title: 2nd entrance

Description: hi, I would just like to feedback that I have seen cars reversing from the 2nd gantry plenty of times and sometimes residents have to reverse for them too if we are waiting behind. this is very dangerous. can I pls suggest that in future, the guards just tell the drivers to drive down the ramp and make a u turn and come back out instead of reversing onto the main road pla

MA Reply: [13 Aug 2025] Dear Joy, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation regarding this mater and concur that it compromises the safety of road users when cars reverse from the 2nd gantry. We will bring this matter up to security to enforce protocols that were put in place to prevent this from happening. Instead of requesting the vehicles to reverse, they should register the vehicles and allow them to go down the ramp instead. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We will look into this matter and ensure that an amicable solution is reached. As your response is not required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for your kind attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-35722025-08-02Blk 55
Unauthorized car wash service operating regularly in Blk 55 carpark.

Title: CARPARK AREA BEING USED AS CAR WASH

Description: Cars near blk 55 lift lobby are having car washes regularly and as per your security its done by a "known car wash person" in the neighbourhood. I find this ridiculous as the car park is constantly wet and dirty from this "service" that has been "allowed". My kids have on many occasions almost slipped and fall from the dirty water patches left from these car washes. Our carpark is already in a horrible state even though the Condo is less than 2 hrs old. Please do something urgently about this as its a safety concern and also a waste of maintenance fees!

MA Reply: [02 Aug 2025] Hi Ms. Suriyana Rahmat, thanks for the feedback. This 'known car wash person' has been engaged by the residents to wash their car during nighttime. If the car's owner not able to remove their car to the washing bay on time when the washer comes down, then the car washer has no choice but to wash within the parking lot. So, to avoid any further slip and fall issue, we have informed the night shift Security Officer to inform this car washer to make good on the flooring after each car wash. We will monitor the area closely and appreciate your cooperation in maintaining a safe and pleasant environment for all residents. Thanks again.

Closed

Title: carpark floor is wet

Description: my son fell on car park and had some abrasions on knee. it was due to wet floor surround a car. the was was believed to wash it at car park slot, causing wet floor surround the car. I have took few photos, please feedback to car owner.

MA Reply: [24 Jul 2025] Dear Angela, thanks for the feedback. We are very sorry to hear that and hope that the abrasion on your son's knee is not that serious. We will approach the car owner and advise them to carry out the next car washing at the washing bay which has been provided. For the photos, we will also share with them. Thanks again.
