Should you encounter such errant vehicles again, we suggest that you call security at [phone] for them to take immediate action. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-34552025-07-08Blk 57
Motorbike parking in visitor lot every night since last year.

Title: visitor parking every night

Description: Motorbike FBT4480S has been parking everyday since MAY last year. I have feedback this and you said it has been resolved. HOW? how has it been resolved? it's been OVER A YEAR, if he is a resident, why he doesn't need to have a tag? there should be visitor lots so that residents who have paid for the label can have parking.

MA Reply: [09 Jul 2025] Dear Harris, Thank you for your feedback. We have checked our record that this motorbike belongs to a resident. 1. For motorbike parking management, the condo management keeps a record of all registered motorbikes. 2. The car decal is not suitable for display on motorbikes, as they are often stolen by others. Therefore, motorbikes are exempt from displaying the decal, but we do keep a proper record of them.

Closed
1292-34232025-07-01Blk 53
Grab drivers still cannot find directions despite new signage.

Title: Grab drivers still complain with directions

Description: To the Management: We've been hearing a lot of complaints STILL from the drivers that they CAN'T see anything. Aside from NO NETWORK SIGNAL, the signs are not that effective (not too mention poorly printed still too SMALL) and reflective. Please have it created by a professional traffic sign designer. Don't just print them with plastic cover. This is becoming a big problem for both residents and private hailing can drivers like Grab. We already noticed some drivers cancelling if once they know they would need to pickup or drop in the basement

MA Reply: [01 Jul 2025] Thank you for your feedback and we sincerely apologize for the delay and any inconvenience this matter has caused you. At this stage, the Management has extended all necessary assistance in facilitating the mobile network enhancement works within the estate. We have ensured full cooperation with StarHub, Singtel, and M1 to support this joint effort on behalf of our residents and visitors. They have experience disruption of works being done and expected completion to Q3 2025 we're hopeful that these upgrades will significantly improve the mobile reception in the basement level and throughout our estate. We'll keep you updated on the progress and let you know as soon as the work is completed. Thank you for your understanding and patience. We appreciate your attention to this matter and wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Wishing you a pleasant day ahead. --- [10 Jul 2025] Dear lay san, thank you for your feedback. We will alert security on this matter and reach out to the driver if the car is still in the carpark. Closed
1292-33542025-06-13Blk 45
Resident nearly hit by van at arrival hall.

Title: I almost get hit by a car

Description: At around 9:27am today 13 Jun I almost get hit by a car, white van at the arrival hall. The car, was requested by a management staff, move backward without activate alert sound, I was at the left of the car starting to go into the direction if I did not very well aware the sudden move. The van did not stop and the staff also not alert that even I talk to him. Actually during that time there is also another car horn and the van still move backward. And finally he returned one sentence saying that I need to take care myself. In my opinion, at the situation that when there are multiple car stuck. The staff shall ask the most front car who block the queue to move instead. I can imagine if it was not me but a child is at the place at that moment. Please remind the staff who do traffic control to be serious and alert all the time. Thanks.

MA Reply: [13 Jun 2025] Thank you for bringing this important safety concern to our attention. We take all incidents involving the safety of our residents seriously. We have forwarded your feedback to the security company responsible for traffic control at the arrival hall. We have also requested them to: Review the incident with their staff, Reinforce proper safety protocols including the use of reverse alert signals, Emphasize the importance of vigilance and professionalism in managing vehicle movements, and Ensure staff respond appropriately and courteously to residents’ concerns. Please be assured that resident safety is our top priority, and we will continue to monitor the situation closely. Should you have any further concerns or observations, please do not hesitate to contact us. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation. -w

1292-50372026-04-22Blk 45
Request to enforce pet restrictions near poolside common area.

Title: Feedback on Pet Control Near Poolside Area

Description: Dear Management, I am writing to raise a concern regarding pets being brought to the poolside area. Recently, some residents have been allowing their dogs to urinate and defecate near the pool. This has resulted in unpleasant odors, which significantly affect the environment and comfort of residents who are using the swimming facilities. For hygiene and safety reasons, I would like to suggest that stricter guidelines be implemented to restrict pets from entering or walking near the poolside deck area. In particular, the poolside deck with dark brown flooring, as well as the adjacent grass areas, should be clearly designated as no-pet zones. Additionally, it would be helpful if management could establish a dedicated dog-walking area for pet owners. The surrounding walking paths around the condominium would be a more appropriate space for this purpose and would help balance the needs of both pet owners and other residents. Clear signage and communication of these guidelines would also help ensure better compliance. Thank you for your attention to this matter. I appreciate your efforts in maintaining a clean and pleasant living environment for all residents. Yours sincerely, Amelia

Pending
1292-50322026-04-21Blk 47
Request to enforce pet rules in common areas.

Title: Request to Reinforce Safety Measures for Pet Owner

Description: Dear Management Team, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to kindly request that the management consider reinforcing safety guidelines for residents who keep cats, particularly regarding securing windows and balconies. Unfortunately, a tragic incident occurred this morning in our condo where a cat fell from height and passed away. This incident is deeply saddening, and I believe it highlights the importance of preventive measures to ensure the safety of pets. It would be greatly appreciated if the management could remind or encourage pet owners to install proper window grilles (narrow grilles, wide grilles are not safe for cats) or protective netting on balconies and windows. Taking such precautions could help prevent similar accidents in the future and promote a safer living environment for both residents and their pets. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Kind regards,

MA Reply: [22 Apr 2026] Dear Sir/Mdm, Thank you for your feedback.

1292-49852026-04-16Blk 57
Security or access issue reported at estate.

Title: Monkeys spotted nearby NPC

Description: Hi MCST Team, I would like to inform you that monkeys have been seen wandering around the bus stop near the side gate, as well as the prayer burning bin area. Please see the attached photos for your reference. Thank you.

MA Reply: [16 Apr 2026] Dear ACAC, Thank you for your feedback and for bringing this matter to our attention, as well as for the photos provided. We would like to inform you that our team will closely monitor and maintain the affected areas, including the bus stop near the side gate and the prayer burning bin area, to ensure that there are no accessible food sources that may attract the monkeys. In addition, we will be reporting this matter to the relevant authorities (AVA / NParks) for their assessment and necessary action. We appreciate your vigilance and cooperation in helping us maintain a safe and pleasant environment for all residents. Regards, Rui Ling

Closed
1292-49842026-04-16Blk 61
Persistent dog barking from unit at Block 61 disturbing neighbours.

Title: Persistent Dog Barking from Unit [unit] blk 61

Description: I would like to report a neighbour disturbance issue from the resident at Blk 61, [unit]. Their dog barks continuously and excessively every day once the owners leave home, typically from around 7:30–8:00 AM onwards. The prolonged barking causes serious disturbance to surrounding residents and affects our quality of life. I kindly request the management to look into this matter and advise the unit owner to address the situation, such as by making appropriate arrangements for their pet when they are away. Thank you for your assistance.

MA Reply: [16 Apr 2026] Dear Sir/Mdm, Thank you for your feedback regarding the disturbance. We understand your concerns and would like to share that the residents of the mentioned unit have just moved in, and their dog is still not familiar with the new environment. As such, this may have contributed to the barking when the owners are away. The unit owner has been informed of the situation and will make efforts to minimise the noise. We kindly seek your understanding and patience to allow a few more days for the dog to adjust and become familiar with its surroundings. Regards, Rui Ling

Closed
1292-49652026-04-14Blk 57
Stray cat loitering around estate; removal update requested.

Title: Cat

Description: I still seeing the Cat loitering around blk 57. is there any update on removing the cat. I noticed there are couples of animal poop around the estate might came from the cat. this is a private estate should not have animal loitering around and dirty the common area

MA Reply: [14 Apr 2026] Dear Ms Peilu, Thank you for your feedback. We have tried may ways to remove the stray cats including bringing them to the forest quite far away but they still comeback. We have already highlighted the issue to the "cat welfare society" and they have replied that they are still looking for some families to foster to them (they have successfully paired two families to two cats previously already). Best Regards, Rui Ling

Repeated dog urination incidents in lifts reported by resident.

Title: Feedback on Repeated Dog Pee Incidents in lifts

Description: I am writing as a frustrated and increasingly annoyed resident regarding an issue that has been happening far too often in Blk 59’s lifts. Despite previous reports to the security team, an irresponsible dog owner continues to allow their dog to pee inside the lift. This is unacceptable, unhygienic, and frankly shows a complete disregard for the shared living environment. I am providing screenshots of my earlier reports as well as photos of the repeated incidents (the most recent one happened last night before 10pm) to show just how long this problem has been going on. It is extremely disappointing that such behaviour still persists. I urge the management to take concrete and visible actions immediately: 1. Formally remind and educate all dog owners in the estate about their responsibilities, including ensuring their pets do not relieve themselves in common areas. 2. Review the CCTV footage for the dates and times of the reported incidents to identify the irresponsible owner. 3. Take firm action against the repeat offender to prevent this from continuing. Such behaviour should not be tolerated, and residents should not have to repeatedly report the same issue. We all pay maintenance fees to enjoy a clean and safe environment. It is unreasonable for residents to enter lifts that smell of urine because someone refuses to control their pet. I hope to see prompt and effective measures taken (including, but not limited to, putting up notices in the lifts to remind pet owners to control their pets, or use preventive measures like wearing a pet diaper if it is due to medical conditions). Thank you.

MA Reply: [13 Apr 2026] Dear Issac, Please accept our sincere apologies for the delayed response, and thank you for taking the time to write in and for providing detailed information and photos regarding the matter. We are sorry to hear about the repeated incidents in the lifts at Blk 59 and fully understand your frustration and concern. We agree that such behaviour is unacceptable, unhygienic, and does not reflect the standard of living that residents should expect within the estate. Please be assured that we take this matter seriously. For your information, we have already been putting up notices in the lifts and common areas, as well as issuing circulars to residents to remind pet owners of their responsibilities in maintaining cleanliness and ensuring their pets do not soil common property. Despite these efforts, we acknowledge that the issue has persisted and will continue to reinforce these reminders. In addition, we will review the relevant CCTV footage based on the information provided in order to identify the responsible party. Should the individual be identified, appropriate action will be taken to address the matter and prevent further recurrence. We appreciate your suggestion on additional preventive measures and will continue to monitor the situation closely while exploring further steps where necessary. Thank you once again for bringing this to our attention and for your patience. We remain committed to maintaining a clean and pleasant living environment for all residents. Rgds, Desmond

Closed

1292-47712026-03-24Blk 47
Dog waste at playground posing health risk for toddlers.

Title: To improve hygiene and safety at playground

Description: Dear Management, As a parent of a toddler, I have noticed an increasing amount of dog waste🐶💩 at the playground in our estate. This appears to be due to irresponsible behaviour by some dog owners or helpers. This situation poses a serious hygiene and safety concern, especially for young children who frequently play in these areas and may come into direct contact with contaminated surfaces. I would like to respectfully request the following actions: - Prohibit dogs from entering playground areas - Install clear and prominent signage to communicate this rule - Installation of CCTV or increase supervision and enforcement - Implement appropriate penalties for irresponsible behaviour Maintaining a clean and safe environment for children should be a shared priority. I hope the management will take this matter seriously and take prompt action. Thank you for your attention. Best Regards Adaline Pan

MA Reply: [25 Mar 2026] Dear Ms Adaline Pan, Thank you for your feedback and bringing this matter to our attention. We would like to highlight that pets are strictly not permitted within the playground area. Pet owners are also responsible for ensuring that any pet waste is promptly and properly cleared. We have instructed our security officers to increase patrols in the area and additional notices will be put up to remind all pet owners of these regulations. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. Best Regards, Rui Ling

Closed
1292-41272025-12-06Blk 53
Pet waste on lawn causing dead grass patches and smell.

Title: Ongoing Pet Waste Issue & Lawn Condition

Description: Hi management, I would like to raise an ongoing concern regarding the lawn and pet waste near my block. Many pets are still using the grass as a toilet, and large patches have already turned yellow and died. The smell along the path has also become quite strong and unpleasant. This has been an ongoing issue for a looooong time. and I hope to return home to a clean, healthy and pleasant environment—not one filled with strong odors and an unsightly lawn. Although your previous notices have been issued, the situation has not improved. Could you please advise whether the damaged lawn will be replaced? and what additional and more strict measures will be taken to address this issue? can we enforce a rule that pets should relieve themselves only outside the condominium? thanks.

MA Reply: [08 Dec 2025] Dear Johice, Thank you for your feedback and for taking the time to share your ongoing concerns with us. We fully understand how unpleasant this situation has been, and we sincerely appreciate your patience. We would like to update you that the management has already scheduled the replacement of the grass patches that have been damaged by pet urine. This work will be carried out progressively to restore the affected areas and improve the overall environment around the blocks. With regard to stricter measures, we agree that the current situation needs to be better managed. As you may be aware, any rule that restricts where pets may be walked within the estate—including a prohibition on pets relieving themselves in common areas—must be introduced as a house rule or by-law, which can only be deliberated and decided by the incoming Management Council under the Building (Strata Management) Act. Once the new Council is formed, we will formally bring this matter to their attention for discussion. This includes exploring whether additional regulations can be introduced to better manage pet activity in the estate. In the meantime, the management will continue to work with residents and the pet-owner community to encourage responsible pet-handling practices and to reduce instances of improper pet toileting on the grass. Our team will also continue to monitor the affected areas closely. Thank you once again for highlighting this matter. We appreciate your understanding as we work towards a cleaner and more pleasant living environment for all residents. Please feel free to reach out if you have further questions. --- [09 Dec 2025] Dear Johice, thank you for getting back to us. The management council will be formed after the 1st AGM which is happening this Saturday, 13 Dec 2025. Besides posting on HiLife and the notice boards, we have also been working closely with a community-driven pet group in Normanton Park to enforce and educate pet owners on the importance of keeping the estate clean. Other measures taken include engaging our service providers such as our cleaners & security to keep a lookout for any pets found on the common area and advise the pet owners accordingly to ensure that they have the necessary cleaning materials for the pets. We believe that this provides an amicable measure to controlling the current pet situation at this point in time. As a matter of fact, we are observing a reduced number of reported pet incidents in the estate since working together with the pets group. Please be assured that upon the formation of the council, this matter will be brought forward to them to see if additional measures should be taken to address this situation. We hope that we have answered your enquiry amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for your kind attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed

Title: irresponsible pet owner at level 4

Description: A dog owner did not clean up after her dog pee at the corner of the glass door at Basement 1 of Block 45. she stays at level 4, either units 01,02,03 or 04 and her dog is a grey schnauzer. that’s very very irresponsible behaviour.

MA Reply: [31 Oct 2025] Hi Stella Leong, thanks for the feedback. We have arranged the block cleaners to clean up the dog pee at B1 level. Thanks again. --- [31 Oct 2025] Thank you for informing us about the incident at Basement 1 of Block 45. We appreciate your vigilance in helping to maintain a clean and pleasant environment for all residents. We take such matters seriously and will look into this issue. A reminder will be sent to all pet owners to clean up after their pets and to be considerate of shared spaces. Our cleaning team will also attend to the affected area promptly. Thank you once again for bringing this to our attention.

Closed
1292-39392025-10-20Blk 55
Big snake seen near Blk 45, unsafe for children.

Title: snakes in the condo !!!!!!

Description: we saw a snake in block 45. it was a big snake and we were with babies. it is very unsafe

MA Reply: [21 Oct 2025] Hi Apoorva, Thank you for letting us know. We understand your concern, and we’re taking it seriously. As a preventive step, we’ll be putting sulphur along the perimeter fence, which helps to keep snakes away. Thanks again for your feedback and helping us keep the condo safe.

Closed
1292-39382025-10-20Blk 61
Stray cats multiplying in NP, no action taken by management.

Title: Stray Cats roaming

Description: I am wondering why is there no action taken to curb the roaming of stray cats in NP. it started with one small cat ( which has now grown) to at least 3 full grown cats now ( 2 grey and 1 ginger). Security told me there is nothing that can’t be done. These cats fight ( don’t know with what) late at night and you can then shrieking from my level. I see them daily 6.45-7.15am and evening 8pm onwards. Are we going to wait till the population increases before taking some serious actions, eg have pest control etc. Security doesn’t help as they already told me ‘nothing can be done’ which surprises me. I hope you will take some action as this is not a public estate. Hint- you willl see them in front of Blk 59 and this morning (6.45am) was sitting right in front of the gate to 7/11, and I have to take a longer detour to exit, as they refuse to move away from the gate.

MA Reply: [21 Oct 2025] Dear Theresa, Thank you for taking the time to share your concerns regarding the presence of stray cats around Normanton Park, particularly near Block 59 and the 7-Eleven gate. We understand your discomfort and concerns regarding the roaming cats and the disturbances caused, especially during the late hours. Please be assured that your feedback is taken seriously. To address the situation, we have been in communication with the Cat Welfare Society (CWS) regarding humane and effective ways to manage the community cat. According to their guidance and best practices, the most sustainable and humane approach is the TNRM method – Trap, Neuter, Return, and Manage. This method helps control the cat population over time and prevents the arrival of new cats by maintaining a stable colony. We would like to share that residents within Normanton Park who are part of a local cat welfare group have already taken proactive steps. They have arranged for the sterilisation of the cats (ligation) to prevent breeding. Going forward, they are also considering adoption efforts to remove the cats. It’s important to note that community cats are generally not aggressive and will not pose a threat unless they feel provoked. Nonetheless, we understand that the current presence and noise can be distressing. We are working with the welfare group to monitor the situation and manage it respectfully and responsibly. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation as we strive to maintain a balanced, humane, and pleasant living environment for all residents.

Closed
1292-39132025-10-14Blk 55
Dog faeces on walkway near Blk 51 pool area, requesting ban.

Title: Animal Faeces On Common Areas (2)

Description: Faeces in front of Blk 51 beside the pool. I reiterate whatever you are doing is a waste of time. Everyday we see shit everywhere in our estate. I could post everyday. Just ban dogs from walking around in our estate. And put fences around the grass area to stop the dogs and people from walking over them, shitting and urinating on them!

MA Reply: [14 Oct 2025] Dear Ameer, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation and have forwarded the photo and this matter to the cleaners for their immediate action. With regards to your suggestion on banning dogs from walking on the estate, we regret that the MCST is not empowered at this point in time to enforce such a policy as it will require a passing of a resolution to enshrine it as a by-law. Instead, the management will work closely with our service providers (security, etc.) to educate pet owners that they should not allow their pets to defecate openly on the common area, and to clean up after them should it accidentally happen. For your point on fencing the grass area, such modification to the common area will present a significant change to the facade of our estate and therefore will not be able to be enacted. A compromise that the management will take into action is to put up signages in these grassy areas to remind pet owners that they should not let their pets foul indiscriminately. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. Rest assured the management will take this matter seriously and will implement the necessary changes to resolve this matter. Thank you for your kind attention. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-39092025-10-14Blk 55
Dog feces at Blk 55, needs clearing.

Title: dog shit at 55

Description: dog shit at 55 please clear

MA Reply: [14 Oct 2025] Dear Gervin, We refer to your feedback above with the photo attached regarding the subject matter with thanks. We wish to highlight that we have assigned the matter to our cleaning team and it have cleared. Thank you again for bringing this matter to management attention.

Closed
1292-39042025-10-13Blk 55
Dog faeces on walkways near Blk 53, 55, and 49.

Title: Animal Faeces On Common Areas

Description: Noticed dog faeces at the walkway between Blk 53 & 55 and a trail of dog faeces on the walkway in front of Blk 49 while I was taking a walk with my daughter this morning. For the upteen time also she stepped on it and almost slipped when she did. I’m wondering why dog owners are not being held accountable for this atrocity and why the management has allowed this situation to continue for the last 2 yrs. The landscape has been ruined by this and not to mention the yellow/brown grass patches due to dog urine. Clearly whatever you guys are doing isn’t working and none of the dog owners are taking you seriously. I also did not remember buying into a pet friendly condo when I bought my 5 bedder at Normanton Park. I’ll be waiting for a fall to happen one of these days due to the animal faeces in the area before suing.

MA Reply: [13 Oct 2025] Dear Mr. Ameer Mohamed, thank you for your feedback regarding the issue of dog faeces found at the common areas. We understand your concern and sincerely apologize for the inconvenience caused. Please be assured that the management has been actively addressing this matter. Despite repeated reminders, signages and regular cleaning, we note that some pet owners still continue to disregard the estate's rules on responsible pet ownership. We will continue to monitor the affected areas closely and appreciate your understanding and patience as we work towards maintaining a clean and pleasant environment for all residents.

Closed

Suggest designated dog walking area due to frequent feces on paths.

Title: Regulations to pet owners

Description: I would suggest to set up a designated area for the pet owners to walk their pets. I can easily spot quite a number of stools everywhere near the condo after 10:30 during the night time. it’s not fair to other residents here to bear these cleaning costs and tolerate their unresponsive tenants.

MA Reply: [06 Oct 2025] Thank you for sharing your suggestion and concern with us. We understand your frustration regarding the current situation and agree that responsible pet ownership is important to maintaining a clean and pleasant environment for all residents. Your suggestion to set up a designated area for pet walking has been noted, and we will bring it to the attention of the incoming council for further discussion and consideration. In the meantime, we will continue to remind pet owners to clean up after their pets and act responsibly in shared spaces.

Title: info on circular on leash and muzzle regulations

This is because the information in the circular is vague, hence I would like clarity in this. Was there another event that trigger the release of the circular? Also, may I know which act or regulation did you base your information on? Thank you,

MA Reply: [30 Jul 2025] Dear Ms Kellyn, thank you for reaching out and for sharing your thoughtful feedback on the recent circular about pet leash and muzzle regulations. The circular serves as a gently reminder to all pet owners about the importance of creating a safe and harmonious living environment for everyone in the estate. The guidelines shared are provided by the Animal & Veterinary Service (AVS) website, under the National Parks Board (NParks). https://www.nparks.gov.sg/avs/pets/owning-a-pet/caring-for-your-pet/legal-and-communal-guidelines-for-owning-a-dog Regards, The Management

Closed
1292-33932025-06-22Blk 51
Multiple snake and large lizard sightings in poor lighting.

Title: snake and rep like spotting in Normanton Park

Description: Hi in past few days there have been multiple spotting of snakes and large lizards in the premises . With poor lighting and small children playing in the premises what is the measures the management office taking to prevent any mishap . This is a serious security issue for small children in the society ,

MA Reply: [23 Jun 2025] Thank you for alerting us to the recent sightings of snakes and large lizards on the premises. We understand your concern, especially considering the limited lighting and presence of young children. Your family’s safety is our top priority. Our landscape team is regularly trimming grass and vegetation to eliminate hiding spots and improve visibility. Our pest control contractors will inspect the grounds to identify and seal off entry points and apply targeted treatments specific to our environment. Our technical team is reviewing lighting levels in play areas and pathways. We’ll install brighter, motion-activated fixtures if needed to improve safety.

In addition, we have instructed our landscaping team to apply smoke powder (snake repellent) in the affected area and surrounding greenery, as a preventive measure. We appreciate your vigilance in helping to maintain a safe environment for all residents. -w

1292-31692025-05-05Blk 49
Dog urine repeatedly found outside unit doorstep.

Title: Dog pee on my flooring just outside my door

Description: This is not the first time we have tolerated our neighbor’s / neighbors’ dog(s) peeing at our lift area and we have previously written in after few incidents. It stopped for a while after the management stepped in, sent out a memo and we thank you guys for that. Just few hours ago, we saw dog pee stain on our flooring just outside our door! As mentioned we have been a very patient, peace-loving neighbour and we are muslims and we are never against dogs but crossing our boundary is just too much! It’s bad enough that the dogs, especially the one next door barks loudly at any time of the day, now they are peeing at neighbours’ doorsteps. We always order food from Grab or Foodpanda and also purchase items online and many times the delivery guys would simply leave the food and items outside our door. What if the food and items are placed where the dog pee is and we end up touching the dog pee? We are taking about hygiene and potential disease here. Please take serious action on this very irresponsible and disgusting behaviour of dog owners. The ugly yellow stain is still outside my door at 49, [unit], you guys can take a look for yourselves and decide if a firm action is too much to ask.

MA Reply: [05 May 2025] Thank You for the feedback. We have attend your unit regarding this matter today at 05/05/2025. If you have any information, please do contact us. --- [05 May 2025] Dear KhaiMas, thanks for the feedback. I have arranged the cleaners to clean up the dog pee on the flooring. Pertaining to the dog always barking during daytime, please share with us the unit number where the dog barking coming from so that we can approach your neighbor and advise them. Thanks again.

Pool users not following hygiene rules and proper attire.

Title: swimming pool

Description: I would like to raise a concern regarding hygiene and proper usage of the swimming pool, which is a shared common area for all residents. I have noticed that some individuals are not washing their legs before entering the pool area, are not wearing proper swimwear, and are dipping their legs directly into the pool. This raises hygiene concerns, as such behavior may pollute the pool and affect the health and comfort of other swimmers. I would appreciate it if management could look into this matter, possibly by reinforcing pool rules through signage or reminders, to ensure the pool remains clean and safe for everyone’s use. Thank you for your attention and support.

MA Reply: [07 Feb 2026] Thank you for your feedback. We have noticed that some individuals are not washing their legs before entering the pool area, are not wearing proper swimwear, and are dipping their legs directly into the pool. To maintain hygiene and safety, we will be placing an additional notice reminding residents: -No dipping of legs into the pool -Washing of legs is required before entering the pool Additionally, we will remind security personnel during their patrols to inform residents and help enforce these rules. Best Regards - Joanne

1292-42062025-12-24Blk 49
Security stopped RC boat use at pool after 10pm unnecessarily.

Title: security on high horse

Description: security( Raj) said I could not ( after years of doing so) play with my RC boat after 10 10pm, as there was people in the pool(kids pool). there were no kids as the pools close at 10pm, I have countless witnesses. They said they have been here for 6 months and the acting supervisor has been here for 2 years, ( I have never seen them once) can you tell me what the policy is for RC vehicles, skate boards, roller skates, scooters,bicycles, etc for the condo is? as I read,and understand, there is not much. So how can security not allow RC, scooters, skates after 10pm but allow screaming and events to continue?

MA Reply: [26 Dec 2025] Dear Wesley, thank you for your feedback. Kindly be advised that the pools are closed for use after 10pm. We would also like to highlight an excerpt from the condominium handbook: "All Occupier(s) and/or their invited guest(s) shall not: ... Use, ride or park bicycles, tricycles, children’s riding toys, roller-skate and the like in the car park or common areas." All noise complaints that are brought to the attention of the security after 10:00pm are expected to be attended by security. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

1292-34142025-06-29Blk 55
Security chased resident out of pool near strata houses.

Title: Pool in front of Strata Houses

Description: Dear Management, Today 2 security officers chased me out of the pool in front of the strata houses. They claimed that the pools there are only for the residents staying in the strata houses. Firstly, I did not see any signs that states as such. In fact the officers told me that they had been removed. Secondly, when we purchased the house when it was first lauched, I vividly remember that the developer specifically said that the pools in front of the strata houses were for all residents. Thirdly, in the Normanton Park telegram group chat, this question was asked before and the reply from the residents in the Strata houses was that it is for everyone’s use. I am frankly appalled that I was asked to leave the pool. I don’t think the residents in the strata houses pay a premium to use the pools in front of their house and I would like the management to clarify this with all residents and security. Thank you.

MA Reply: [30 Jun 2025] Dear Varun, thank you for your feedback on this matter. We apologise for the inconvenience you experienced yesterday, and we will open an investigation into this matter. Rest assured we will identify the security staff involved in this matter and revert to you by Wednesday, 02 July with the outcome of this matter. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead. --- [07 Jul 2025] Dear Varun, thank you for your update. After our investigation and identifying the root cause of such miscommunication, we determine that there was a misconception within the security team that the pools in front of the strata houses are reserved for only these residents. We have since educated the security team that there is no such rule of exclusive use of the swimming pools and they have agreed to comply. We apologise for the misunderstanding caused by this incident. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Title: Urgent! Sand Fly Infestation at Blk 57 Walkway

Description: Dear MCST Team, I would like to bring to your urgent attention the issue of sand flies active around the walkway area at Blk 57, Level 1. Recently, while assisting my wheelchair-bound brother with his walking therapy using the walkway railing (please refer to the attached photo), I was bitten multiple times by sand flies. As a result, my leg has become swollen, very itchy, and developed red rashes (please see attached photo). The condition has caused significant discomfort. This sand fly issue has been ongoing for almost three years, and many residents and visitors have been affected during this period. Despite the recurring problem, it appears that the situation has not been properly resolved. We kindly request that the management take immediate action to inspect the area and implement effective pest control measures to ensure the safety and well-being of residents and guests. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I look forward to your response and updates on the actions taken.

MA Reply: [12 Feb 2026] Hi ACAC, thanks for the feedback regarding the sandfly bite incident and your concern that this matter has been ongoing for some time. We are sorry to hear about your experience and understand your concern for the safety and well-being of residents and guests. We have immediately informed the pest control service provider to conduct an inspection of the affected areas and to implement the necessary treatment and control measures. Today is the routine fogging and we have instructed the pest control to carry out the chemical spraying around the area of blk 57 level 1. We appreciate your patience and cooperation as we work to mitigate the issue. Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

1292-40692025-11-23Blk 53
Toilet door hinges jammed and wood termites found.

Title: toilet doors

Description: toilet door hinges constantly jammed and recently wood termites found at toilet doors

MA Reply: [24 Nov 2025] Please be informed that the Defect Liability Period (DLP) for your unit has ended. As such, the contractor is no longer responsible for rectifying issues reported after this period. Once the DLP has expired: • The developer is no longer liable for non-structural defects, unless they fall under long-term structural warranties (e.g., 10 years for major structural issues under the Building Control Act). • The homeowner becomes responsible for identifying and addressing any issues that arise. • Homeowners or tenants are required to engage their own contractors and bear the cost of any repairs or maintenance. For the toilet door - call handyman Mr Hu at [phone] and Pest Control call - Elite Pest [phone]/ [phone] Mr Bernard Tan.

Title: mosquito cove pavilion

Description: my family organised a kids birthday party event at Cove Pavilion on 25th October Saturday evening from 5pm onwards. we sprayed mosquito repellant on all our guests feet and even had mosquito coil beneath our table but the number of bites we received was astonishing, not just from me but from all my guests. my son had 11 bites, my friend’s son 10 bites, my friend had 20 bites, my husband 25 bites, for me 12 bites (please refer to photos). this is a little too extreme, we killed 4 blood filled mosquitoes and the bites did not lessen to any degree we left early. it is very worrying as this is what I perceive as an uncontrolled mosquito situation for my family and guests. please look into mosquito control. we certainly do not wish for a dengue cluster to spring out of normanton park.

Title: Response to [phone]

Description: I refuse to believe that there aren't any sandflies - there has been multiple complaints on the Normanton telegram chat by various neighbours of the same issue. Please do a thorough check. If it's not sandflies, it's bound to be some other pest or insects that is biting us.

MA Reply: [11 Oct 2025] Dear owner, Thank you for your feedback. We understand your concerns and acknowledge similar reports from other residents regarding insect bites. For your information, the pest control team conducts misting and fogging every Monday and Thursday, targeting mosquitoes, sandflies, and other pests. The treatments have already been carried out for mosquitoes, sandflies, and other reported issues. However, we have assigned the matter to the pest control team for further investigation. Thanks again.

Closed

Title: Sandflies at 57 BBQ Pits area

MA Reply: [10 Oct 2025] Dear owner, We refer to your feedback regarding the subject matter above with thanks. Please be informed that we had previously reported the issue at the mentioned location to our pest control team. Although they were unable to detect any presence of sandflies during their inspection, they proceeded to treat the area as a precaution. Following your recent feedback, we have immediately reassigned the matter to the pest control team for further investigation. Thanks again to bringing this matter to our attention.

MA Reply: [06 Oct 2025] Hi Hao Xun Kuah, thank you for your feedback and for bringing this matter to our attention. We are sorry to hear about your attendees were bitten by insects at the Cove Pavilion BBQ pits. Please be assured that the Management is taking this matter seriously. We have informed the pest control about this matter, and they will carry out the treatment at the affected areas to help reduce the sandfly activity. We appreciated your understanding and cooperation as we work to improve the environment for all residents.

Dog waste issue: Our security team has informed the dog owner during their patrols, and management has also issued multiple notices to address the recurring issue of dog droppings in shared spaces. We sincerely appreciate your patience. Sandfly/pest concerns: We have engaged a professional pest control team to investigate and treat the affected areas, specifically targeting low-flying pests like sandflies (biting midges) that thrive in damp environments.

Closed
1292-37132025-09-01Blk 57
Painful sandfly bites on legs near Blk 57 grass patch.

Title: sandfly bites extremely painful and itchy

Description: Sandflies? I was standing near the grass patch in front of blk 57 for about 10 mins on Friday evening at around 8pm, speaking to someone. Legs were exposed below knee. Thought I was getting bitten by mosquitoes. Saw angry red bumps on my legs the next day. The worst part is how unbearably itchy they are. Made it hard even to sleep. after searching on Google, I figure they're sandfly bites. please find out what landscaping treatment options there are to get rid of sandflies. We don't have marshes or water bodies there (Kent ridge pond and dragonfly pond are some distance away), so I can only assume these sandflies are in the soil. We need better pest control. this is injurious to our health. imagine our kids who are at the playground every evening.

MA Reply: [02 Sep 2025] Hi Ra01234, thank you for your feedback and bringing this matter to our attention. We are very sorry to hear that you have experienced discomfort from Sandfly bites within the estate. Please be assured that we take this matter seriously. To address the issue, we are working with the pest control team who are specialized in the Sandfly to explore suitable treatment options. We will continue to monitor the situation closely and once again thank you for the feedback.

1292-33202025-06-04Blk 49
Silverfish coming out of wardrobes in unit.

Title: silverfish infestation

Description: Hi team, I am finding silverfish coming out from my wardrobes every now and then. Are there cases of silverfish infestation at Normantan park? do you have advice to eliminate them?

MA Reply: [05 Jun 2025] Thank you for your feedback. Please note that pest issues occurring within individual units, such as silverfish in wardrobes, fall under the responsibility of the respective unit owner or tenant. The Management is responsible for maintaining common areas, We recommend engaging a licensed pest control company to inspect and treat the affected areas professionally. They will be able to assess potential entry points and apply safe, targeted solutions to help eliminate the infestation effectively. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

1292-45162026-02-11Blk 53
Water heater leaking rusty liquid, damaging ceiling.

Title: heater issue

Description: the heater in toilet seems to be leaking rusty liquid..now it’s making a hole in the ceiling. can I know whom can we contact for urgent fixing, or can the maintenance team help to fix

MA Reply: [11 Feb 2026] Dear Kean, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks, and we understand your concern. Please be informed that the Defect Liability Period (DLP) for your unit has ended. As such, the contractor is no longer responsible for rectifying issues reported after this period. Once the DLP has expired: • The developer is no longer liable for non-structural defects, unless they fall under long-term structural warranties (e.g., 10 years for major structural issues under the Building Control Act). • The homeowner becomes responsible for identifying and addressing any issues that arise. • Homeowners or tenants are required to engage their own contractors and bear the cost of any repairs or maintenance. You may need to engage the contractor or your own contractor at your own cost. CASA Pte Ltd Contact: [phone] Email: [email] We appreciate your patience and understanding. Warmest Regards, Alfred

1292-46362026-03-04Blk 47
Blk 47 lobby ceiling leaking and mouldy after aircon leak.

Title: previously reported - leaking and mouldy ceiling

Description: I previously reported that the air-conditioning units in the lobby of Block 47 may be leaking, which appears to have caused ceiling damage and mould growth. It seems the root cause has not been properly resolved, as the paintwork completed 1–2 months ago is already showing signs of deterioration and mould recurrence. I would suggest that the team investigate and rectify the underlying issue rather than applying surface-level fixes, and only close this feedback once the matter has been satisfactorily addressed. The current condition is unsightly and may pose hygiene concerns for users of the common space. Two areas have been highlighted in this report for reference.

MA Reply: [05 Mar 2026] Hi Joel, thanks for the feedback. Our technicians have removed one of the lightings to check on the root causes and discovered that there is some leaking within the aircon pipeline that cause the false ceiling to be damaged and mould growth. We will contact the aircon service provider to come down and check on it. Enclosed is the photo for your understanding and will update again. Best regards, Shafiee

Title: Drainage issue

Description: The developer need to sort out the drainage issue. This is crazy.

MA Reply: [09 Jul 2025] Thanks for flagging the situation at the side gate. The flooding after heavy downpours is due to intense flash floods—not because of any blockage—especially during such sudden, heavy rainfall. We’ve had our technical team inspect all drainage systems, and we are pleased to confirm they're clear and functioning as designed. After the rain stops, the water does flow away naturally, albeit requiring a little time to do so.

Closed
1292-41812025-12-19Blk 57
StarHub and Eight users still lack B1 carpark network as of Dec 2025.

Title: lack of Broadband Connectivity at B1

Description: Dear MCST, We are writing to formally escalate an ongoing and unresolved issue regarding the lack of broadband connectivity at the B1 car park. As of December 2025, StarHub and Eight users still do not have network access at B1, which directly prevents residents from connecting to the HiLife application to enter the block. This has caused persistent inconvenience and operational difficulties for residents on a daily basis. This issue has been raised repeatedly over the past almost three years, yet there has been no effective resolution, clear explanation, or confirmed completion timeline. The prolonged delay is unacceptable, especially considering that the HiLife system is a critical access and security feature that depends entirely on stable connectivity. We request the MCST to provide, within a reasonable timeframe, the following: 1. A clear explanation for why this issue has remained unresolved for such an extended period 2. The current status of any works or coordination with service providers 3. A firm and confirmed timeline for when broadband connectivity at B1 will be made fully operational If this matter cannot be resolved promptly, residents will have no choice but to seek further clarification and escalation through the appropriate channels, as the lack of access affects safety, accessibility, and daily living. We trust that the MCST will treat this matter with the urgency it warrants and provide a concrete action plan without further delay. We look forward to your immediate response.

MA Reply: [22 Dec 2025] Thank you for your email, and we sincerely apologize for the delay and any inconvenience this matter has caused you. At this stage, the Management has extended all necessary assistance in facilitating the mobile network enhancement works within the estate. We have ensured full cooperation with StarHub and M1 to support this joint effort on behalf of our residents and visitors. They have experience disruption of works being done and expected completion to Q1 2026 we're hopeful that these upgrades will significantly improve the mobile reception in the basement level and throughout our estate. We'll keep you updated on the progress and let you know as soon as the work is completed.

Closed
1292-40762025-11-24Blk 59
Inquiry about when full mobile coverage in carpark will be ready.

Title: Mobile coverage in the car park

Description: Hi - can you provide an update on when mobile coverage will be fully available in the car park?

MA Reply: [25 Nov 2025] Dear Paul Sumner, thank you for your feedback. As of the writing of this email, we are hearing reports that SIMBA and Singtel mobile coverage is available in the carpark. After speaking with Starhub & M1, they updated us that the upgrading works will only complete in Q1 2026. They apologise for the unexpected delay and will work towards expediting it as soon as possible. They are currently working on connecting the mobile antenna infrastructure with the fibre network mains, you can observe them working near the main entrance and roundabout. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for your kind attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

For the time being, SIMBA & Singtel had already boosted their mobile network coverage in Normanton Park and are able to receive their reception in the carpark. We're hopeful that these upgrades will significantly improve the mobile reception in the basement level and throughout our estate. Thank you for your understanding and patience. We appreciate your attention to this matter and wish you a pleasant day ahead.

1292-39242025-10-16Blk 47
Basement telco connection missing since TOP.

Title: connection at basement

Description: hi. telco connection at basement has been missing since TOP. please kindly address this urgently.

MA Reply: [16 Oct 2025] Dear Joel, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation regarding the telco connection in our basement. While we have received feedback that SIMBA and SingTel network is available in our basement area, we have recently received information from Starhub regarding delays in their installation works. They have informed us that the reason why there has been a delay in setting up the network is due to the estate being an uncovered fixed location for their network. Therefore, there has been delays caused by space constraints and receiving approval from various authorities for laying of new pipelines near the expressways and such. We hope that this provides an amicable explanation as to the delay for the affected telecoms. Rest assured that the management has provided all resources to expedite this matter, and the current works are under the purview of the telco operators. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for your kind attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Title: internet connectivity

Ongoing works will complete around the end of Q3/beginning of Q4 2025. Rest assured that management will keep all residents updated via notices and circulars when we receive any from the mobile network operators. Thank you for your kind attention and patience on this matter. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Follow-up on mobile coverage, asking which blocks improved.

Closed
1292-33862025-06-22Blk 47
Entrance lights left on during daytime, wasteful.

Title: why lights are on during day time

Description: Hi can we ensure the lights are off at least in the enterance during day time and add to wasteful expenditure

MA Reply: [23 Jun 2025] Dear Mr. Suraj Serikar, thanks for the feedback. Let us discuss with our technician on this electrical circuit diagram and hope that we are able to have some cost saving on this matter. Will update again. Thanks again. --- [28 Jul 2025] Dear Suraj, thank you for your feedback. We apologise for not being able to get back to you sooner. Thank you for your feedback regarding the lights at the drop-off point and main arrival lobby. The lights are kept on during the day to meet the minimum illuminance requirement as stated in the Singapore Fire Code for all common access routes, including covered lobbies. Natural daylight is often insufficient to meet this standard, so the lighting remains on to ensure consistent safety and compliance at all times. Please be assured that the fixtures used are low-power, energy-efficient LED luminaires designed in accordance with BCA's guidelines to ensure minimal energy consumption while maintaining the required brightness for the area in question. We hope that we have addressed your feedback amicably. As no further response is required for this matter, we will close this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

MA Reply: [09 Mar 2026] Hi Xiaofan Xu, thank you for the feedback the regarding the incident a group of individuals attempting to retrieve a lost baseball which resulted in damage to the greenery. Unfortunately, there is no CCTV coverage in the specific area to review the footage and identify the individuals. We appreciate your vigilance feedback. Thank you for your understanding and support.

1292-43412026-01-18Blk 59
Grass patches dying, possibly due to dog urine.

Title: bald grass patch

Description: recently I saw there are a lot of balding grass patches. it does not make sense as the weather is not hot. what could be the reasons? I saw many dogs peeing at these areas that I snapped the photo at. is that the cause?

MA Reply: [19 Jan 2026] Hi Mr. Javin Chew, thank you for bringing to our attention the issue of balding grass patches within the estate. We appreciate your observation and understand your concern regarding this matter. It is true that the possible impact is coming from the dog urine on the landscaped areas. Our landscaping team will inspect the affected areas and take the necessary steps to restore the bald grass. At the same time, we will continue to remind pet owners to be mindful when walking their dogs and to avoid allowing pets to urinate on the landscaped areas. Your feedback is valuable to us as it helps maintain a pleasant and well kept environment for all residents. Thank you for your cooperation and understanding.

MA Reply: [29 Dec 2025] Thank you for your feedback. The plants were uprooted more than three months ago due to heavy rainstorms. We have informed the landscape supervisor to investigate why the plants are not regrowing and to take the necessary actions.

Title: Request for cleaners to clean common area

Description: Hi Management team, I’m the owner of Terrace House 15 and would like to raise a concern about the large amount of leaves falling daily from the tree in front of our house. We have to clear the leaves once or twice a day as they keep accumulating (photo attached, taken just now). Regards, Chanchala

MA Reply: [02 Dec 2025] Hi Chanchala Singh, thank you for bringing the matter to our attention. We understand that a large amount of leaves has been accumulating in front of your terrace house unit, and we appreciate your concern about the cleanliness of the area. We wish to clarify that the space directly outside a private area and not part of the common property maintained by the estate's cleaning team. As such, the cleaners are not authorized to carry out routine sweeping or maintenance within these private frontages. Nevertheless, we will remind the landscaping team to clear the leaves during their maintenance work. You may also wish to clear the immediate frontage as needed to maintain the cleanliness around your unit. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

Closed
1292-37462025-09-10Blk 59
Suggestion to enclose grass patch near gym for dog-friendly area.

Title: Suggestion for landscaping enhancement

Description: There is a patch of grass with some seating in front of the gym. It is very rarely used by anyone as a seating area. Given it's in a low traffic area and is separated from the rest of the facilities, could management and the developer consider enclosing it with a fence and gate to create a small 'dog run' where dog owners could let their pets play safely and without disturbing other residents? This would complement the existing pet friendly areas in the development and also make it easier to encourage pet owners to use this new area rather than areas where pets are not allowed such as the children's playground, benefitting all residents.

MA Reply: [10 Sep 2025] Dear Paul Summer, thank you for your feedback. We note your observation regarding considering enclosing the grass area in front of the gym as a "dog run" area. As the MCST is currently serving its Initial Period before the formation of its 1st Management Council, regretfully we are not able to push for such changes in the use of the common area at the point of this writing. Moving forward, we have recorded your feedback and will bring it up to the council upon its formation after the 1st Annual General Meeting. We hope this addresses your feedback amicably. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

1292-36232025-08-14Blk 49
Tools left in maze garden for 2 years; sprinklers near Blk 49 faulty.

Title: Tools in maze garden/ water sprinkler Blk 49

Description: Hi Management office, i would like to bring your attention to this spade and bag of cement left in maze garden since i moved in almost 2 years ago. Kindly remove it if it is not required. Also could you look at the water sprinklers around blk 49(Heliconia walk and chill out lawn) the standing water in soil may lead to mosquito breeding and water seeping out to pavement on a daily basis may be a safety issue ?

MA Reply: [14 Aug 2025] Hi Hwee Cheng Na, thanks for the feedback. We have arranged with the Landscape Team and they have cleared away the blue plastic basket and meanwhile for the standing water on the soil, the team had already some abate in pallets form whereby, it helps to kill the larvae in standing water before they develop into adults. We have also contacted the Irrigation Team to check on the water sprinklers just in case there are water leakage around the area. Thanks again.

Title: Landscaping at block 47

Description: Hi, it has been 2 weeks I got to your attention regarding bad state of landscaping and the grass patches contractor hasn’t done a single patch of grass replacement. As a managing agent are you going around and checking the estate?if contractor is not working let’s put them on notice

MA Reply: [14 Jul 2025] Thank you for the feedback. We understand your concerns regarding the landscaping and the uncompleted grass patch replacements. Please be assured that our team conducts regular site inspections to monitor the contractor’s progress. We have identified that the irrigation pipe was faulty, which has caused delays in the landscaping works. The contractor has been informed of this issue, and rectification works are underway. We will continue to closely monitor the situation and take further action, including issuing a formal notice to the contractor if there is no improvement within the stipulated timeline. We appreciate your patience and will keep residents updated on the progress. Thank you for your understanding. -w

In-unit indoor monitor stopped working, needs repair.

Title: Faulty Indoor Monitor - Request for Review

Description: Dear Joanne, Thank you for your reply. However, I would like to clarify that the indoor monitor in my unit (03-02, Blk 45) stopped functioning suddenly without any damage or misuse on our part. As a result, visitors are unable to use the doorbell, which is causing inconvenience. Since this appears to be a technical fault and not due to negligence, I do not think it is fair for the charges to be borne by the resident. I would appreciate it if management could kindly review this matter and arrange for an inspection first before determining responsibility for any costs. Thank you for your understanding, and I look forward to your response.

MA Reply: [23 Feb 2026] Dear Noreen, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks. Please be informed that the Defect Liability Period (DLP) for your unit has ended. As such, the contractor is no longer responsible for rectifying issues reported after this period. Once the DLP has expired: • The developer is no longer liable for non-structural defects, unless they fall under long-term structural warranties (e.g., 10 years for major structural issues under the Building Control Act). • The homeowner becomes responsible for identifying and addressing any issues that arise. • Homeowners or tenants are required to engage their own contractors and bear the cost of any repairs or maintenance. You may need to engage HiLife to rectify the issue at your own cost. HiLife Interactive Pte Ltd Contact: [phone] Email: [email] Warmest Regards, Alfred

Title: Stolen bicyle seats at Blk 61 B1 bicycle park

Description: As reported, my bicycle seat was stolen on 5 Mar 2026 10+pm. Later i realised that my car was parked near bicycle park area and extracted the car camera with videos of the thief in action. There are 3 key videos which I would like to share 1) The thief came out from Blk 61 carpark side door and target at my bicycle ( I suggest MA checked the video at B1 lobby and lift between 22:00-22:03, you should be able to get his face) 2) Second video shown the secuirty came by while the thief is benting over still trying to steal my bicycle seat 3) Third video shown the thief successfully stolen the seat and walked away. I would like to make police report tomorrow and hope MA could help extract his photo from B1 lobby camara to facilitate the police report between 22:00-22:03

MA Reply: [06 Mar 2026] Dear Sandra, Thank you for sharing the following dashcam videos with us. We have shared them with security for their necessary follow-up actions. We will keep you updated of any further developments of this matter. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead. Warmest Regards, Nurhidayat

In Progress
1292-46442026-03-05Blk 61
Bicycle seat stolen at Blk 61 B1, security saw thief.

Title: Stolen bicycle seat at Blk 61 B1 bicycle park area

Description: Between 22:15-22:30 on 5 Mar 2026 , someone stolen my bicycle seat at Blk 61 B1 bicycle park area. One security guard saw the thief and questioned him and understand that the thief said he was repairing his bicycle and shown the security guard his Hilife app. My son happend to come back same time and saw the security guard talking to the thief. My son did not realise that thief was targeting my bicycle and when home sensed something wrong. He immediately went down to B1 and found that the seat was stolen. Hence, he informed the same security guard who spoke to the thief. Understand that the thief is chinese, medium height slightly long hair. Two questions for MA: 1) should police report be made ? 2) when can camera be installed to capture future thief ?

MA Reply: [06 Mar 2026] Dear Sandra, thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear about the stolen bicycle seat. Rest assured that we will work closely with security to review our CCTV cameras to see if we can further pinpoint where the suspect stays (since he showed the HiLife app, we assume that he is a resident here). We are also aware that you were able to capture some more photos and videos of the suspect and have shared them with security. Concurrently, we encourage you to lodge a police report while we conduct our investigations and will corroborate any further information with the Investigation Officer when approached by them. Regarding future CCTV cameras deployed, let us take this matter up to the Management Council to seek approval for the procurement of cameras to be deployed for deterrence in the future. We hope that we have addressed your feedback amicably for now. We will keep you updated of any further developments in this feedback. Thank you for your kind attention and we wish you a pleasant day ahead. Regards, Nurhidayat

1292-42142025-12-26Blk 55
Two theft incidents in unit over recent months, security concern.

Title: report incidents of stolen case

Description: I’m writing to provide feedback and raise a concern about security and safety in the condo, as my household has experienced two theft incidents within the last few months. 1) August incident: A packet/bag of cherries was stolen. 2) December incident (recent): A pair of my husband’s shoes was stolen. These incidents have made us feel increasingly uneasy about day-to-day safety in the common areas. We would appreciate the Management Office’s support to strengthen preventive measures.

MA Reply: [27 Dec 2025] Dear Xupan Zhou, thank you for your feedback. We note your observations regarding the theft incidents reported over the past few months. We strongly encourage you to lodge a police report in relation to the recent incident, and please be assured that the management will render full cooperation to the authorities and assist with their investigations to the best of our ability. We understand the concerns this may raise regarding safety within the common areas. While the security team continues to provide estate-level security coverage, residents are also encouraged to take proactive precautions, such as avoiding leaving valuables unattended in common areas. Some residents have also implemented additional deterrent measures, such as installing CCTV coverage at the frontage of their units, where permissible, to enhance personal security. We hope this provides an amicable response to your feedback. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for your kind attention and we wish you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-39112025-10-14Blk 53
Someone attempted to access apartment via door lock keypad.

Title: Possible Attempt to Access My Apartment

Description: I am writing to report a security concern that occurred this morning, 9:15 - 9:25 AM. I cannot record the exact timing, but during that time, I heard multiple attempts on my door lock keypad — it sounded as though someone was trying to enter my apartment by testing the password, approximately 2 to 3 times, before leaving. When I went to check what was happening, I also heard footsteps moving away from my door, someone might had been standing right outside. However, when I checked the door monitor immediately afterward, I did not see anyone at the corridor. Initially, I assumed it might have been a delivery, but there were no parcel or delivery notices left at my door step. Could you please review the building’s CCTV footage or check any access records to see if there was anyone in the corridor or near my apartment during that period? Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your follow-up and any findings from your investigation.

MA Reply: [14 Oct 2025] Dear Johice, Thank you for your message and for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand your concern regarding the incident outside your apartment between the period. Unfortunately, we currently do not have CCTV coverage in the corridor areas and therefore there are no CCTV footage available. However, please be assured we take all feedback seriously. Should you encounter such incident again, we strongly encourage you to contact our security team immediately via the guardhouse or management hotline [phone]. Thank you again.

Person seen picking flowers from public garden area.

Title: stealing flowers

Description: the lady harvesting blossoms in the public area . . .

MA Reply: [14 Oct 2025] Dear HrBrt, thank you for sharing this matter with us. Kindly provide us with an estimated time when this incident occur so that we may deploy security in the future to intervene and educate that such flowers are not meant to be plucked. We look forward to your response. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead. --- [14 Oct 2025] Dear HrBrt, we have gone to clarify with the landscaper and the supervisor clarified that while in the midst of pruning the plants as part of the routine maintenance, the lady requested if she could take the cut flowers for her own use. We have already discouraged the landscaper from allowing anyone to take any of the plants, even though it has been pruned as it was already sprayed with chemicals as part of fortifying the plants against external factors. Thus, these plant materials may not be suitable to be kept at home as well. As no further response is required, we will be closing this feedback automatically. Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention, and we wish you a pleasant day ahead. --- [14 Oct 2025] Dear owner, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks. For your information, we have checked with our landscape contractor, and they informed us that the resident had asked for the trimmed blossoms to be kept aside during their trimming work, as she wanted to take them home. However, please note that removing or damage plants from common areas is not allowed. Thanks again bringing this matter to management attention.

Closed
1292-33762025-06-18Blk 61
Shoes stolen from doorstep shoe rack.

Title: Shoe theft in condo

Description: A pair of shoes disappeared from my shoe rack on my doorstep and I suspect they have been stolen. Unfortunately I have no way of knowing who. I would like to ask that we step up security or request all residents to comply with advisories again such as ensuring L1 gates are closed because I notice our L1 gates at Block 61 are always left open. or is there a way to ensure they close automatically, or that lift levels are restricted to card access? on the whole it just feels like security is quite lax here and now that shoes have disappeared off my shoe rack - and I believe previously there was a spate of bicycle thefts - I really think management should step up on security measures.

MA Reply: [19 Jun 2025] Thank you for your Feedback. We are sorry to hear about the missing pair of shoes outside your unit and understand your concerns regarding the overall security in the estate. We recommend that you file a police report regarding this incident so that it can be properly documented and investigated. Level 1 Gate Closure: We acknowledge that the Level 1 gate at Block 61 is sometimes left open. The installing a spring-loaded or hydraulic auto-closer or a magnetic locking device, subject to Management Council approval and budget availability. Circular/Notice pasted on the gate to remind the resident to close the gate after opening. Lift Access Control: Lifts in our estate are not currently programmed with a card access control system, and such a system was not included in the original design and installation. As such, restricting lift access by resident keycard would require additional hardware upgrades and integration, subject to Council review and budget consideration. Security Measures: We will also request our security personnel to increase their vigilance and patrols, especially in common areas such as lift lobbies and corridors. Additionally, we will review the current CCTV coverage and assess if enhancements are needed in light of recent incidents. While we regret that we are unable to trace the missing shoes without direct evidence or CCTV footage in the specific area, we encourage all residents to keep valuables within the unit whenever possible. Thank you once again for your feedback. -w

Closed
1292-32142025-05-12Blk 49
Request for responsible access gate closing by residents.

Title: Access control

Description: With recent thefts in other condos, can I request that we remind our residents to br responsible about closing access gates which are often just left open (daily especially in morning and late afternoon). I’ll suggest installing a ringing alert that’s goes off the gates are not closed. Also, there should be increased vigilance about tail gating given the continuous stream of delivery personnel pls. Pls take action to improve the current situation. Thanks.

MA Reply: [13 May 2025] Thank you for highlighting your concerns and suggestions regarding the estate’s security. We acknowledge the issue of gates being left open and the risk of tailgating, especially during peak hours and times of high delivery activity. Tailgating can be challenging to manage as it often occurs quickly and discreetly, particularly when access is granted to legitimate visitors or delivery personnel. A reminder will be issued to all residents to ensure that access gates are properly closed behind them. We will explore the feasibility of installing an alert system or chime that activates when gates are left open for an extended period. Security will be reminded to stay vigilant and to monitor for tailgating more closely, especially during high-traffic periods involving delivery personnel. We appreciate your feedback and proactive suggestions. Your cooperation plays a vital role in keeping our community safe.

Closed
1292-32022025-05-11Blk 59
Door mat tampered with and building security concern.

Title: Tampered Door Mat and Building Security Concern

Description: Dear Management I am writing to formally raise two concerns regarding recent issues at our residence. Firstly, I discovered that my door mat, placed just outside my unit, has been deliberately cut. This act of vandalism is both unsettling and unacceptable, and I urge the management to investigate the matter. Secondly, I would like to highlight a recurring security concern — the entrance door to the building is frequently left unlocked. This compromises the safety of all residents and potentially allows unauthorized individuals to access the premises. I kindly request prompt action to: 1. Investigate the tampering of my door mat. 2. Ensure the building’s entrance door remains securely locked at all times. Thank you for your attention to these matters. I hope for your swift response and resolution. Sincerely,

MA Reply: [13 May 2025] Thank you for informing us of these matters. We are sorry to hear about the damage to your door mat. While we regret to inform you that there is no CCTV coverage in that specific area, we will be increasing patrols and keeping a closer watch in the vicinity. We also encourage you to report any suspicious activity you may observe, as it can help us in preventing future occurrences. Regarding the issue of the entrance door being frequently left unlocked, we agree this poses a safety concern. We will inspect the door to ensure the locking mechanism is functioning properly, and reminders will be issued to residents and relevant personnel to ensure the door is securely closed at all times. We appreciate your vigilance and will continue to monitor both matters. Please feel free to reach out if the issues persist or if you notice any further developments.

Closed
1292-31712025-05-05Blk 47

Title: Disturbance from security guard

Description: My part time helper, Sandar came in this morning from the side gate next to the AYE bus stop at about 9am. She registered and then the security guard started saying I Love you Sandar, and smiled at her. It terrified her! She described the guy is walking with a limp. I am not sure if he is a full time security guard but it poses insecurity in our community. Do report with his manager on such inappropriate behavior.

MA Reply: [05 May 2025] Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We are very sorry to hear about the inappropriate and unsettling experience your helper, Sandar, encountered this morning at the side gate. The safety and comfort of all residents and their visitors are of utmost importance to us. We take such reports seriously and have already initiated an investigation into the incident. Based on the description provided, we are working with the security agency to identify the guard involved. The individual will be interviewed, and appropriate disciplinary actions will be taken in accordance with our community’s code of conduct. Please be assured that we have reminded all security personnel of the importance of maintaining professionalism at all times. Additional training will also be conducted to reinforce expected behavior and boundaries. We deeply regret any distress caused to Sandar and to you as a resident. Should there be any further concerns or if Sandar is willing to provide additional details, we would appreciate the opportunity to follow up. Thank you again for your vigilance in helping maintain a safe and respectful living environment.
