1292-44642026-02-06Blk 55
Inquiry about removed playground toys and their location.

Title: toys at kids playgrounds

Description: hi. there is no reply button on feedback. so I’m putting my reply through the new feedback. I’m referring to my Enquiry attached regarding kids toys at playgrounds. you have mentioned all toys have been removed due to safety concerns posed to children. I, as a parent. not sure what safety concern toys might pose to children. aren’t toys produced with safety regulations in mind. Normanton park is supposedly a family friendly estate with a lot of family size units. however management would agree that playgrounds have very limited equipment for children to play with. that’s why parents started bringing toddler toys down for all the kids to share and enjoy common space in safe manner. families and small kids have been enjoying them for the last two years since TOP and I’m not aware of any injuries or complains. what change has happen now? has there been any injury? as I haven’t seen any notice on HILi posted by maanegemt. as well as I haven’t seen any notice, that toys will be removed and reason behind it. I see it as lack in communication and no regard for the residents/ owners to just take all the toys (which have been kindly brought by residents) and remove and dispose them. without any notice. I hope toys are still safely kept and we can have a discussion with management and resident committee on forward actions. and last question. has residents committee been consulted on this matter? and was it instructions from them? so myself and other residents know with who to address an issue. appreciate your understanding. Ala

MA Reply: [07 Feb 2026] Thank you for bringing to our attention your concern regarding the toys at the kids' playground. We would like to highlight that many damaged toys have been left at the playground. In addition, some residents do not put the toys back properly after use, resulting in toys being scattered around the area. We would like to clarify that the Management does not keep any toys for personal storage. As part of our routine maintenance and safety checks, the Management removes toys that are found to be damaged or unsafe. This measure is taken to ensure the safety of all children using the facilities and to maintain a clean and orderly common area. The Management will not be responsible for any loss or damage to items left at the playground. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation. Kind regards - Joanne

Closed
1292-44242026-02-02Blk 55
Shared playground toys removed, asking where they went.

Title: toys at kids playgrounds

Description: hi. residents noticed that all the toys kind residents brought down to share with condo kids have disappeared from playgrounds. does management know if they have been kept anywhere by cleaners maybe? thanks

MA Reply: [03 Feb 2026] Hi Ala K. Thank you for bringing to our attention your concern regarding the toys at the kids' playground. We would like to clarify that the Management has not kept any toys for personal storage. as part of our routine maintenance and safety checks, the Management has removed toys that were found to be damaged, unsafe, or left unattended at the playground for an extended period. This measure was taken to ensure the safety of all children using the facilities and to maintain a clean and orderly common area. So, the Management will not be responsible for any loss or damage of items left at the playground. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation.

Closed
1292-44232026-02-02Blk 59
Scooters, rollerblades, and bicycles pose danger in condo.

Title: kids on bicycles, scooter and roller blades

Description: I saw a number of children using scooters, roller blades and even adult riding bicycles within the common areas. it poses dangers to the people, especially young kids and old people, who are not quick to avoid them. most condo prohibit the uses of such equipment within the estates and expect people to walk with the bikes instead. can the MA look into the safety of the residents or visitors to avoid law suits.

MA Reply: [03 Feb 2026] Hi Javin Chew, thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We acknowledge your feedback and fully understand your concern, as such activities may pose safety risks to residents, especially at common area where pedestrian traffic is high. Please be assured that the Management takes residents' safety seriously. We will remind the residents of the house rules and safety guidelines. Furthermore, we have informed the security to increase the patrol at the pool deck area as to ensure that these activities are controlled and managed. If needed, the security will approach the children to have their address taken and after which they will highlight to their parents on the hazard of fast speed cycling. We appreciate your feedback and cooperation in helping us maintain a safe and pleasant environment within the estate.

Title: football in the playground.

MA Reply: [26 May 2025] Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We understand your concern regarding the recent football activity at the playground area, including the damage to the landscaping and the potential safety risks to younger children. We appreciate your efforts to maintain the estate's greenery and ensure a safe environment for all residents. Currently, the playground is not a designated area for ball games, and we do not encourage football to be played there. However, due to the limited open spaces within the estate, some older children may use the area informally, especially during school holidays or weekends. We will remind our security team to be more vigilant and advise the children to avoid playing football in landscaped and shared spaces that are not meant for such activities. Thank you once again for your constructive feedback and understanding. William Wong (Mr.) Condominium Executive Normanton Park

Closed
1292-45202026-02-11Terrace
Faulty fire alarm needs urgent replacement.

Title: Alarm went off again

Description: I need a replacement alarm urgently. It is absolutely jarring

MA Reply: [11 Feb 2026] Dear Resident, We refer to your feedback regarding the above subject matter with thanks, and we understand your concern. Please be informed that the Defect Liability Period (DLP) for your unit has ended. As such, the contractor is no longer responsible for rectifying issues reported after this period. Once the DLP has expired: • The developer is no longer liable for non-structural defects, unless they fall under long-term structural warranties (e.g., 10 years for major structural issues under the Building Control Act). • The homeowner becomes responsible for identifying and addressing any issues that arise. • Homeowners or tenants are required to engage their own contractors and bear the cost of any repairs or maintenance. You may need to engage the contractor or your own contractor at your own cost. B.S. ENGINEERING CO PTE LTD Contact: [phone] Email: [email] We appreciate your patience and understanding. Warmest Regards, Alfred

Closed
1292-39752025-10-30Blk 45
Fire exit at Blk 45 found locked, serious safety risk.

Title: Locked Fire Exit at 45 Normanton Park

Description: Dear Property Management, I am a resident at Normanton Park. At 1:50 pm today, I informed the office staff at the function room that the fire exit at 45 Normanton Park was locked. A locked exit is a serious life-safety risk. I was told a contractor would fix it today. For the record: • Location: Fire exit serving 45 Normanton Park • Issue: Exit door locked from the egress side • Reported: 1:50 pm today to office staff at the function room Under the Singapore Fire Code and basic life-safety practice, means of egress must be unobstructed and openable from the egress side without keys or special knowledge during occupancy. A locked exit can delay evacuation and exposes residents, visitors, and workers to harm. I am requesting: 1. Immediate action today to unlock and make the exit fully operable and compliant. 2. A written confirmation today once rectified, including the time of correction and the steps taken to prevent recurrence. 3. If the lock is required for security, confirmation that panic hardware or an equivalent release is installed so the door opens freely from the inside at all times. If the hazard is not corrected today, I will report it to the authorities tomorrow as a fire safety hazard. I can provide photos and video and a brief statement if helpful. Please acknowledge this report and share the interim controls you will put in place until the door is verified safe. Thank you for your prompt attention.

MA Reply: [31 Oct 2025] Dear Simon, Thank you for your feedback regarding the fire exit at 45 Normanton Park. We appreciate your concern for fire safety. Please be assured that the fire exit door is equipped with an access control system designed to balance both fire safety and building security. While the door requires a resident access card to enter from the outside for security reasons, it is openable from the inside at any point of time. We wish to highlight that the fire exit door lock will automatically release when the alarm system is activated, allowing free and immediate egress in full compliance with the Singapore Fire Code. This ensures that all occupants can evacuate safely without delay. We understand your concern and thank you for bringing it to our attention.

Closed

1292-35752025-08-03Blk 53
Possible gas smell detected at Blk 53 level 24 since early Aug.

Title: Urgent: Possible Gas Smell at Blk 53

Description: We would like to bring to your attention that since 3 August, we have been experiencing an intermittent gas-like smell at Block 53, Level 24. Upon checking with others in the group chat, we found that we are not the only residents noticing this. Could you kindly look into this matter urgently and check for any possible gas leak or related issues in the building? We would appreciate it if you could confirm whether there is any safety concern and ensure that everything is under control. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

MA Reply: [04 Aug 2025] Hi Eunice Tan, thank you for bringing to our attention the concern regarding the intermittent gas-like smell at level 24. This morning, we went up to the level 24 to check on the situation, but it seems there isn't and gas-like smell. Since it is intermittent happening, we will check randomly on the situation. Should you notice the smell again, please inform the Security Officers immediately at the 24hrs hotline no. [phone]. In the meantime, we kindly advise to exercise caution and to avoid using any open flames in the vicinity if the smell is detected. We will continue to monitor the situation closely and will update you once we have further findings. Thank you once again for your vigilance and cooperation.

Closed
1292-33222025-06-05Blk 57
Break glass placed beside card access at Blk 57 basement.

Title: BLOCK 57 Basement access

Description: there is a fault in the design which the breakglass is install beside the card access system into the Basement corridor. the breakglass should be install inside the lobby and not outside. Breakglass is install in case there is an emergency situation when you need to escape and the door release is not working. by installing the breakglass together with the card access system mean I dont need the card I can also access by pushing the breakglass. like that any burglar can just break the breakglass and access to any level

MA Reply: [06 Jun 2025] Thank you for your feedback regarding the manual fire alarm call point located at the building entrance. We appreciate your concern and the opportunity to clarify its purpose and placement. Under the Singapore Fire Code (2021 edition), manual call points (also known as break-glass units) are a mandatory requirement and must be located along exit access routes and at or near exits, including building entrances. We understand the concern brought up of possible trespassing incidents. This has been taken into consideration during the building design, which is why CCTV cameras have been installed at building entrances and exits to deter such incidents from occurring. We hope that we have addressed your feedback amicably. As no further action is required, we will be closing this thread automatically. Wishing you a pleasant day ahead.

Closed
1292-33212025-06-05Blk 51
Renovation contractor misusing common space at Blk 51.

Title: Inappropriate usages of common space

Description: Hi, I wanted to bring something to your attention regarding the renovation at Block 51, Level 14. The contractor appears to be using the fire hose riser area for paint storage. I'm concerned this could impede access to the fire hose in an emergency, which is a serious safety issue.

MA Reply: [06 Jun 2025] Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We take fire safety very seriously and have immediately alerted the renovation contractor regarding the improper use of the fire hose riser (FHR) compartment. We will to remove all stored materials from the compartment with immediate effect and reminded that such storage is strictly prohibited under SCDF fire safety regulations. Our team will conduct an inspection to ensure full compliance, and we will continue to monitor the site to prevent recurrence. Should you observe any similar issues in the future, please do not hesitate to let us know. We appreciate your vigilance in helping to maintain a safe living environment for all residents. --- [06 Jun 2025] Dear KC20, thanks for the feedback. We have arranged the cleaner to remove all the paint and disposed it. Thanks again. --- [06 Jun 2025] Thank you for bringing to our attention the issue of the contractor using the fire hose riser area for paint storage during renovations at Block 51, Level 14. We understand the serious safety concerns this poses, as it may impede access to essential firefighting equipment in an emergency. Please be assured that we have informed our cleaning team to remove the stored items by the end of today. Your vigilance in helping maintain the safety and accessibility of our community spaces is greatly appreciated.

Closed

Red dots = months where this block + issue cluster had feedback reported. Issues appearing 3+ months indicate a persistent problem needing root-cause action.

9. Top 20 Priority Issues -- MA Action List

These are the 20 most critical specific issues requiring MA attention, ranked by severity (High first, then Medium), with trend, duration, and resident impact context.

#1. Gym: 3 of 6 Treadmills Broken for 2+ Months (Gym Equipment)

Trend: GrowingDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: High81 cases / 7 open

7 open cases (27-60 days old). Residents queuing daily. Multiple complaints cite 2+ month wait with no repair timeline from Active Lifestyle Pte Ltd.

Recommended Action: Escalate to Active Lifestyle Pte Ltd for immediate replacement or temporary units. Set SLA: max 7 days for gym equipment repair. Add penalty clause to maintenance contract.

#2. Blk 55: Ceiling Leak and Hole at B1 Lobby (349 Days Open) (Water & Plumbing)

Trend: StableDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: High1 cases / 1 open

Case 1292-3135 open for 349 days. Visible hole in ceiling with active water leaking. Safety and structural concern. No resolution despite nearly a year.

Recommended Action: Emergency repair order. Engage structural engineer to assess. This is the oldest open case with potential safety risk.

#3. Blk 61: Ceiling Surface Peeling at Carpark Driveway (354 Days Open) (Building Maintenance)

Trend: StableDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: High1 cases / 1 open

Case 1292-3113 open for 354 days. Ceiling material peeling off above carpark driveway. Risk of debris falling on vehicles or pedestrians.

Recommended Action: Schedule immediate repair. Cordon off area if debris risk. This case has been open almost a year without resolution.

#4. Blk 47: External Walls Dirty (371 Days Open -- Oldest Case) (Building Maintenance)

Trend: StableDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: High1 cases / 1 open

Case 1292-3006 open for 371 days -- the oldest unresolved case. External wall wash was promised but never completed.

Recommended Action: Schedule external wall wash for Blk 47. Close or provide definitive timeline. This is the single oldest open feedback case.

#5. Blk 53: Lift PL9 Door Opening Between Floors (Lift Issues)

Trend: GrowingDuration: 10 monthsSeverity: High6 cases / 1 open

Lift PL9 at Blk 53 has recurring door malfunction -- opening between floors. 6 lift noise/fault cases at Blk 53 over 12 months. Safety risk.

Recommended Action: Escalate to Kone for urgent PL9 inspection and door mechanism replacement. This is a safety-critical issue.

#6. Blk 55: Level 1 Doors Not Closing (Recurring Security Risk) (Security & Access)

Trend: GrowingDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: High12 cases / 2 open

2 open cases (31 days). Both Level 1 doors at Blk 55 frequently found open -- magnetic lock failure and door closer malfunction. Reported repeatedly across 12 months.

Recommended Action: Replace door closer mechanisms. Install auto-close timer. Brief cleaning staff on door protocol. Consider door sensor alarm.

#7. Bicycle Seat Theft at Blk 61 B1 (Police Reports Filed) (Crime & Police)

Trend: GrowingDuration: 6 monthsSeverity: High5 cases / 2 open

3 cases in 2 months. Bicycle seats stolen at Blk 61 B1 bicycle park. Police report D/20260207/7059 filed. Security confronted thief but thefts continue. Dashcam evidence captured.

Recommended Action: Increase security patrol at B1 bicycle area. Review CCTV coverage and lighting. Consider gated bicycle storage. Share police advisory with residents.

#8. Blk 47: B1 Lobby Ceiling Water Damage and Sagging (Building Maintenance)

Trend: GrowingDuration: 3 monthsSeverity: High2 cases / 2 open

Case 1292-4714 (28 days). Ceiling plaster sagging and water-damaged at Blk 47 B1 lobby. Related to earlier leak case 1292-4636 (39 days, mouldy ceiling from aircon leak).

Recommended Action: Fix root cause (aircon condensation leak). Repair ceiling plaster. Both cases are linked -- resolve together.

#9. Pool Life Buoy Removed from Family Pool (Pool & Water Features)

Trend: RecentDuration: 1 monthSeverity: High1 cases / 1 open

Case 1292-4812 (12 days). Life buoy missing from family pool -- safety equipment removed by unknown person. Immediate safety risk for children.

Recommended Action: Replace life buoy immediately. Review CCTV to identify who removed it. Consider tamper-resistant mounting.

#10. Blk 45: Lift Noise (Grinding/Vibrating) (Lift Issues)

Trend: GrowingDuration: 8 monthsSeverity: High6 cases / 0 open

6 cases of lift noise at Blk 45 over 12 months. Residents report grinding, vibrating sounds. Multiple escalation attempts.

Recommended Action: Request Kone to conduct bearing inspection and noise assessment at Blk 45 lifts. Set acceptable noise threshold.

#11. Smoking on Balconies: Blk 47, 53, 59 (71 Cases, 12 Months) (Noise & Neighbour)

Trend: StableDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: Medium71 cases / 3 open

71 smoking/cigarette complaints across 12 months. Includes balcony smoking, cigarette butts dropping to lower floors, vaping. Same blocks repeatedly affected. 3 still open.

Recommended Action: Issue advisory notices to repeat blocks. Install additional signage. Document evidence for repeat offenders. Consider by-law enforcement at AGM for designated smoking areas.

#12. Noise: Repeat Offender Units (13 Open Cases) (Noise & Neighbour)

Trend: StableDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: Medium190 cases / 13 open

13 open noise cases. Includes: loud singing at Blk 61 (286 days open), furniture dragging at Blk 57 and 61, balcony banging at Blk 53, children rollerblading at Blk 45. Some residents escalating to police.

Recommended Action: Mediation for repeat offender units. Issue formal warning letters. Engage Community Mediation Centre (CMC) for persistent cases.

#13. Dog Poo/Pee in Playgrounds and Walkways (Animal Issues)

Trend: StableDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: Medium24 cases / 1 open

24 cases across 12 months. Concentrated near Blk 47, 49, 53 playgrounds. Same locations suggest repeat offender pet owners.

Recommended Action: Install pet waste bag stations at hotspot locations. Increase signage. Enforce pet registration. Consider CCTV at repeat locations.

#14. Rubbish Chute Blockage: Blk 61 (29 Cases) and Blk 47 (23 Cases) (Rubbish Chute)

Trend: StableDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: Medium147 cases / 0 open

147 chute cases over 12 months. Blk 61 and 47 are worst. Choked hoppers, jammed chutes, recycling chute issues. All currently closed but recur monthly.

Recommended Action: Schedule preventive chute maintenance bi-weekly for Blk 61 and 47. Brief residents on proper disposal. Consider chute flushing schedule with Cora Environment.

#15. Dirty Corridors: Blk 53 (19 Cases) and Blk 61 (15 Cases) (Cleanliness)

Trend: StableDuration: 12 monthsSeverity: Medium98 cases / 5 open

98 corridor/lobby complaints over 12 months. Residents note infrequent cleaning and visible dust/stains. 5 still open.

Recommended Action: Review ABM Century cleaning schedule for Blk 53 and 61. Increase frequency for high-traffic floors. Establish cleaning KPI with spot-check photos.

#16. Blk 53: Algae Forming at Lobby (279 Days Open) (Building Maintenance)

Trend: StableDuration: 9 monthsSeverity: Medium2 cases / 2 open

Two cases (1292-3444, 1292-3443) open for 279 days. Algae forming around lighting point at Blk 53 lobby. Aesthetic and hygiene issue.

Recommended Action: Clean algae and treat surface to prevent recurrence. Investigate moisture source causing algae growth.

#17. Terrace House: Carpark Floor Damage (4th Report, 141 Days Open) (Parking & Traffic)

Trend: StableDuration: 5 monthsSeverity: Medium1 cases / 1 open

Case 1292-4060 reported for the 4th time. Carpark floor damage at Terrace House area unresolved for 141 days.

Recommended Action: Schedule floor repair. This is a repeat escalation -- resident has reported 4 times. Prioritise to restore confidence.

#18. No Mobile Signal at B1 Carpark (Admin & General)

Trend: StableDuration: 6 monthsSeverity: Medium5 cases / 1 open

Multiple reports of no mobile signal in basement carpark. Residents cannot use HiLife app or make calls in B1. Safety concern in emergency.

Recommended Action: Engage Singtel/Starhub to assess B1 coverage. Consider mobile signal booster installation. This is both a convenience and safety issue.

#19. Blk 55: Playground Trampoline Damaged (Playground & Kids)

Trend: RecentDuration: 2 monthsSeverity: Medium1 cases / 1 open

Case 1292-4585 (46 days). Trampoline at Blk 55 playground damaged. Children's safety risk.

Recommended Action: Repair or replace trampoline. Cordon off if unsafe. Children's equipment should have priority SLA.

#20. Terrace House: Landscaping Ponding (106 Days Open) (Landscaping)

Trend: StableDuration: 4 monthsSeverity: Medium1 cases / 1 open

Case 1292-4216 (106 days). Plants behind villa uprooted months ago, not replaced, causing water ponding. Mosquito breeding risk.

Recommended Action: Replace uprooted plants and fix drainage. Ponding creates mosquito breeding ground -- link to pest control preventive action.
